text.en: Duas for Hajaat
Baqiyatus Salehat - Chapter Ten
Brief supplicatory prayers for this world and hereafter requests
<ref=2:13>Sahifa Sajjadia dua no 13 for seeking needs</ref>
First: Imam al-Sadiq (A.S.) is reported to have advised of saying this supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: O Allah, (please) make me fear You as if I can see You.
Make me happy to act piously towards You.
Do not distress me by granting me activity to commit acts of disobedience to You.
Choose for me through Your decree.
Bless me through Your determination

segments.arabic: حَتَّىٰ لا أُحِبَّ تَأْخِيرَ مَا عَجَّلْتَ

translation.en: so that I will not long for delaying that which You would hasten
or hastening that which You have delayed.
And (please) make my richness in my conscience

segments.transliteration: wa matti`ni bi sam`i wa basari

translation.en: and make me find enjoyment in my hearing and sight.
Make them sound until I am inherited.
Grant me victory over him who wrongs me,

segments.transliteration: wa arini fihi qudrataka ya rabbi

translation.en: make me see signs of Your power in him, O my Lord,
and make Your favors to be the delight of my eye.

text.en: Second: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is also reported to have advised of saying this supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ أَعِنِّي عَلَىٰ هَوْلِ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَامَةِ

translation.en: O Allah, (please) help me encounter the horror of the Resurrection Day,
take me out of this world soundly,
give me in marriage the Paradisiacal women,
save me from procuring the provisions of myself, my family members,
and people,
and include me, in the name of Your mercy, with Your righteous servants.

text.en: Third: This supplicatory prayer wards off sinning and brings about all this-worldly and otherworldly requests overall:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah; the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
O He Who has shown only goodness,
concealed the evil (deeds of His servants),
and has not exposed the hidden!
O He Who is generous in pardoning!
O He Who is Excellent in overlooking!
O He Who is Liberal in forgiving!
O He Who opens His hands wide with mercy!
O He Who is present in all confidential talks!
O He Who is the ultimate objective of all complaints!
O He Who is generous in condoning!
O He Who is great in favoring!

segments.arabic: يَا مُبْتَدِئَ كُلِّ نِعْمَةٍ قَبْلَ ٱسْتِحْقَاقِهَا
segments.transliteration: ya mubtadi'a kulli ni`matin qabla istihqaqiha

translation.en: O He Who takes the initiative in enduing with all favors before they are deserved!

segments.arabic: يَا رَبَّاهُ يَا سَيِّدَاهُ يَا مَوْلاَهُ
segments.transliteration: ya rabbahu ya sayyidahu ya mawlahu

translation.en: O my Lord! O my Master! O my Cherisher!
O my utmost hope! O my aide!
(Please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ لاَ تَجْعَلَنِي فِي ٱلنَّارِ
segments.transliteration: wa as'aluka an la taj`alani fi alnnari

translation.en: and I beseech You not to put me in Hellfire.

text.en: Fourth: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have said this supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: You are my trust in all hard times,

segments.transliteration: wa anta raja'i fi kulli shiddatin

translation.en: You are my hope in all ordeals,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ لِي فِي كُلِّ أَمْرٍ نَزَلَ بِي ثِقَةٌ وَعُدَّةٌ
segments.transliteration: wa anta li fi kulli amrin nazala bi thiqatun wa `uddatun

translation.en: and You alone are my reliance and support in every matter that afflicts me.

segments.arabic: كَمْ مِنْ كَرْبٍ يَضْعُفُ عَنْهُ ٱلْفُؤَادُ

translation.en: Too many are the agonies, which the heart is too weak to undergo,
which all stratagems fail to manage,
in which the friend and the stranger disappoint,
at which the enemy gloat,
and in which I lose any way out,
but when I referred them to You and complained about them to You,
abandoning all others than You,
You have relieved, removed, and saved me from it.
So, You are the patron of all graces,
the One Who stands for all requests,
and the utmost of all desires.
So, very much praise be to You
and abundant gratitude be to You.

text.en: This is the supplicatory prayer the Holy Prophet (s) said on the days of the Battle of Badr and the Battle of the Allies (i.e. al-Ahzab). The chief of martyrs; namely, Imam al-Husayn (`a), also said this supplicatory prayer in Karbala' on the `Ashura' Day

Furthermore, two more supplicatory prayers were said by Imam al-Husayn (`a) on that day. The first one is the supplicatory prayer he taught to his son Imam Zayn al-`ªbidin (`a) when he embraced him to his chest while blood was gushing from his body. Used in urgent cases, intense grief, harsh times, and difficult matters, the supplicatory prayer intended is as follows:

translation.en: In the name of Yasin and the Qur'an full of wisdom
and in the name of Taha and the Great Qur'an (I beseech You).

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ يَقْدِرُ عَلَىٰ حَوَائِجِ ٱلسَّائِلِينَ

translation.en: O He Who has power to grant the requests of the beseechers!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي ٱلضَّمِيرِ
segments.transliteration: ya man ya`lamu ma fi alddamiri

translation.en: O He Who knows what is in the inner selves!
O He Who alleviates the agonized ones!
O He Who relieves the distressed ones!
O He Who is merciful to the old ones!
O He Who provides the little babies with sustenance!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ لاَ يَحْتَاجُ إِلَىٰ ٱلتَّفْسِيرِ
segments.transliteration: ya man la yahtaju ila alttafsiri

translation.en: O He Who does not require explanation!
(Please) bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad and…One may now submit his requests

text.en: Fifth: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have raised his hands towards the sky and said this supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: رَبِّ لاَ تَكِلْنِي إِلىٰ نَفْسِي طَرْفَةَ عَيْنٍ أَبَداً
segments.transliteration: rabbi la takilni ila nafsi tarfata `aynin abadan

translation.en: O my Lord, never relegate me to myself even for a winking of an eye,

segments.arabic: لاَ أَقَلَّ مِنْ ذٰلِكَ وَلاَ أَكْثَرَ
segments.transliteration: la aqalla min dhalika wa la akthara

translation.en: less than that, or more than that.

text.en: Sixth: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is also reported to have advised of saying this supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: إِرْحَمْنِي مِمَّا لاَ طَاقَةَ لِي بِهِ وَلاَ صَبْرَ لِي عَلَيْهِ
segments.transliteration: irhamni mimma la taqata li bihi wa la sabra li `alayhi

translation.en: (Please) have mercy upon me against what I am too feeble to do and what I am too impatient to stand.

text.en: Seventh: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is also reported to have advised of saying this supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِجَلاَلِكَ وَجَمَالِكَ وَكَرَمِكَ أَنْ…
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka bijalalika wa jamalika wa karamika an…

translation.en: Allah, I beseech You in the name of Your majesty, beauty, and generosity to…

text.en: One may now submit his requests.
Eighth: Fadl ibn Yunus has reported that Imam al-Kazim (`a) advised him to repeat this supplicatory prayer very much:

translation.en: O Allah, (please) do not make me of the unfixed-hearted
and do not make me feel not negligent.

text.en: To explain, the first statement is a prayer for not including with those whose faith is as irregular as loans, which means that faith is not fixed in their hearts. However, the statement may also stand for a prayer for not including with those whom are relegated to themselves and Allah the Almighty has no interference in their affairs, just like a horse whose rein is thrown freely on its wither and is left to graze uncontrollably.

The second statement is a prayer for not including with those who feel themselves to have carried out their duties towards their Lord flawlessly. In fact, one must always feel that he cannot carry out his duties towards his Lord fully whatever efforts he would exert.
Ninth: Imam al-Baqir (`a) is reported to have said: Almighty Allah has forgiven a Bedouin man for two words he said in his supplicatory prayer. These two words are as follows:

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنْ تُعَذِّبْنِي فَأَهْلٌ لِذٰلِكَ أَنَٱ
segments.transliteration: allahumma in tu`adhdhibni fa'ahlun lidhalika ana

translation.en: O Allah, if You punish me, then this befits me,

segments.arabic: وَإِنْ تَغْفِرْ لِي فَأَهْلٌ لِذٰلِكَ أَنْتَ
segments.transliteration: wa in taghfir li fa'ahlun lidhalika anta

translation.en: but if You forgive me, then it befits You.

text.en: Tenth: Dawud al-Raqqi has said: Whenever he insisted in prayers, Imam al-Sadiq (`a) would pray Almighty Allah in the name of the Five Persons: the Holy Prophet, Imam `Ali, Lady Fatimah, Imam al-Hasan, and Imam al-Husayn, peace be upon them.
Eleventh: Yazid al-Sa'igh is reported to have said: I asked Imam al-Sadiq (`a) to pray Almighty Allah for our good. He therefore said:

translation.en: O Allah, (please) endue them with truthfulness in speech,
fulfillment of trusts,
and constant attendance to prayers.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنَّهُمْ أَحَقُّ خَلْقِكَ أَنْ تَفْعَلَهُ بِهِمْ
segments.transliteration: allahumma innahum ahaqqu khalqika an taf`alahu bihim

translation.en: O Allah, they are the worthiest of all of Your creatures to obtain this from You;
therefore, O Allah, do it to them.

text.en: Twelfth: Say this supplicatory prayer, which Imam `Ali Amir al-Mu'minin (`a) used to say:

translation.en: O Allah, endue me with reliance on You,
entrusting (all my affairs) with You,
satisfaction with Your decree,
and submission to Your ordinance

segments.arabic: حَتَّىٰ لاَ أُحِبَّ تَعْجِيلَ مَا أَخَّرْتَ
segments.transliteration: hatta la uhibba ta`jila ma akhkharta

translation.en: so that I would not long for hastening what You delay
and delaying what You hasten.
O Lord of the worlds!

text.en: Thirteenth: It is reported that Archangel Gabriel came to the Holy Prophet (s) and conveyed to him the following message from Almighty Allah: If you want to worship Me a whole night and a whole day perfectly, you may raise your hands towards Me and say this supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: O Allah, all praise be to You
that is as eternal as You are.
All praise be to You

segments.arabic: حَمْداً لاَ مُنْتَهَىٰ لَهُ دُونَ عِلْمِكَ

translation.en: that never ends save Your knowledge (about it).
All praise be to You

segments.arabic: حَمْداً لاَ أَمَدَ لَهُ دُونَ مَشِيئَتِكَ
segments.transliteration: hamdan la amada lahu duna mashi'atika

translation.en: that has no limit save when You will.
All praise be to You

segments.arabic: حَمْداً لاَ جَزَاءَ لِقَائِلِهِ إِلاَّ رِضَاكَ
segments.transliteration: hamdan la jaza'a liqa'ilihi illa ridaka

translation.en: the recompense of whose sayer is nothing save Your pleasure.
O Allah, all praise entirely be to You,
all gratitude entirely is Yours,
all pride entirely is Yours,
all splendor entirely is Yours,
the whole light entirely is Yours,
all mightiness entirely is Yours,
all grandeur entirely is Yours,
all greatness entirely is Yours,
the whole world entirely is Yours,
the whole Hereafter entirely is Yours,
all nights and all days entirely are Yours,
all creation entirely is Yours,
the good entirely is in Your hand,
and all affairs entirely are returned to You,
both the open and the hidden.
O Allah, all praise eternally be to You.
You are the sympathetic in ordeals,
the lofty in praiseworthiness,
the abundant in bestowals,
the just in decreeing,
the opulent in donation,
the excellent in graces,
the god in the earth,
and the god in the heavens.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ لَكَ ٱلْحَمْدُ فِي ٱلسَّبْعِ ٱلشِّدَادِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma laka alhamdu fi alssab`i alshshidadi

translation.en: O Allah, all praise in the seven strong skies be to You,

segments.transliteration: wa laka alhamdu fi al-ardi almihadi

translation.en: all praise in the plain lands be to You,
all praise that is as much as the scope of the servants be to You,
all praise that is as extensive as lands be to You,

segments.transliteration: wa laka alhamdu fi aljibali al-awtadi

translation.en: all praise in the mountains that are as projections be to You,

segments.arabic: وَلَكَ ٱلْحَمْدُ فِي ٱللَّيْلِ إِذَا يَغْشَىٰ
segments.transliteration: wa laka alhamdu fi allayli idha yaghsha

translation.en: all praise in the night when it draws a veil be to You,

segments.arabic: وَلَكَ ٱلْحَمْدُ فِي ٱلنَّهَارِ إِذَا تَجَلَّىٰ
segments.transliteration: wa laka alhamdu fi alnnahari idha tajalla

translation.en: all praise in the daylight when it shines in brightness be to You,

segments.transliteration: wa laka alhamdu fi al-akhirati wal-ula

translation.en: all praise in the last and the first be to You,

segments.arabic: وَلَكَ ٱلْحَمْدُ فِي ٱلْمَثَانِي وَٱلْقُرْآنِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ
segments.transliteration: wa laka alhamdu fi almathani walqur'ani al`azimi

translation.en: and all praise in the oft-repeated verses and the Great Qur'an be to You.
All glory be to Allah and in praise of Him.
The whole earth shall be in His grip on the Resurrection Day
and the heavens rolled up in His right hand.
Glory be to Him and may He be exalted above what they associate with Him.
All glory be to Allah and in praise of Him.
Everything is perishable except His Face.
Glory be to You, O our Lord, exalted be You,
blessed be You, and holy be You.
You have created all things by Your omnipotence,
predominated over all things by Your almightiness,
towered above all things by Your elevation,
overcome all things by Your power,
fashioned all things by Your wisdom and knowledge,
sent the messengers with Your Books,
guided the righteous ones by Your permission,
aided the believers by Your victory,
and overpowered all the creatures by Your authority.
There is no god save You;
You are one and only and there is no associate with You.
We serve none but You,
we beseech none but You,
and we desire for none but You.
You are the target of our complaints,
the utmost of our desires,
the God, and the Master of us.

text.en: Fourteenth: It is reported that a man came to Imam `Ali Amir al-Mu'minin (`a) and complained about the delay in response of his prayers.
“Have you tried the swift-responded prayer?” Imam `Ali (`a) asked.
“What prayer do you mean?” asked the man.
Imam `Ali (`a) thus taught him to say this supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You
in the name of Your Name; the great, the greatest,
the most majestic, the noblest,
the hoarded, the hidden,
the brilliant, the true,
the manifest demonstration,
which is light with light,
light out of light,
light in light,
light on all light,
light over all light,

segments.transliteration: wa nurun tudi'u bihi kulla zulmatin

translation.en: and light by which all darkness is shone
and all hardships,
all rebellious devils,
and all obstinate transgressors are unraveled.
Neither a land can bear it
nor can any sky hold it.
Through it, all fearful ones attain security

segments.transliteration: wa yabtulu bihi sihru kulli sahirin

translation.en: and sorceries of all sorcerers,
oppressions of all oppressors,
and envy of all envious ones are counteracted.
Because of its greatness, all lands and oceans split
and all arks stand stable
when the angel pronounces it;
so, waves will no more have any effect on these arks.
It is Your Name; the greatest, the greatest,
the most majestic, the most majestic,
the grandest light,
which You have used for Yourself
and by which You have prevailed on Your Throne.

segments.transliteration: wa atawajjahu ilayka bimuhammadin wa ahli baytihi

translation.en: I turn my face towards You in the name of Muhammad and his Household
and beseech You in the name of You and them

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَنْ…
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin wa an…

translation.en: to bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad and to…You may now submit your requests.

text.en: Fifteenth: `Amr ibn Abi’l-Miqdam has reported the following: Imam al-Sadi (`a) dictated this supplicatory prayer, which achieves benefits for this world and the other world: After praising and thanking Almighty Allah, you may say this:

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْحَلِيمُ ٱلْكَرِيمُ
segments.transliteration: allahumma anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alhalimu alkarimu

translation.en: O Allah, You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Forbearing, the All-generous.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta al`azizu alhakimu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Almighty, the All-wise.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْوَاحِدُ ٱلْقَهَّارُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alwahidu alqahharu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the One, the Supreme.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱلْجَبَّارُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta almaliku aljabbaru

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Master, the Compeller.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلرَّحِيمُ ٱلْغَفَّارُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alrrahimu alghaffaru

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the All-merciful, the Forgiver.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلشَّدِيدُ ٱلْمِحَالُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alshshdidu almihalu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Mighty in prowess.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْكَبِيرُ ٱلْمُتَعَالِ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alkabiru almuta`ali

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Great, the High Exalted.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْبَصِيرُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alssami`u albasiru

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Hearing, the Seeing.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْمَنِيعُ ٱلْقَدِيرُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta almani`u alqadiru

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Impregnable, the Omnipotent.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلشَّكُورُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alghafuru alshshkuru

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Forgiver, the Ready to appreciate.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْحَمِيدُ ٱلْمَجِيدُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alhamidu almajidu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Praiseworthy, the All-glorious.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْغَنِيُّ ٱلْحَمِيدُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alghaniyyu alhamidu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Self-sufficient, the Praiseworthy.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلْوَدُودُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alghafuru alwadudu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Forgiver, the Loving.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْحَنَّانُ ٱلْمَنَّانُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alhannanu almannanu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the All-tender, the All-favorer.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْحَلِيمُ ٱلدَّيَّانُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alhalimu alddayyanu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Forbearing, the Requiter.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْجَوَادُ ٱلْمَاجِدُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta aljawadu almajidu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the All-magnanimous, the Glorious.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْوَاحِدُ ٱلأَحَدُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alwahidu al-ahadu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the One, the One and Only.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْغَائِبُ ٱلشَّاهِدُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta algha'ibu alshshahidu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Unseen, the Witness.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلظَّاهِرُ ٱلْبَاطِنُ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta alzzahiru albatinu

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You, the Evident, the Immanent.

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
segments.transliteration: wa anta allahu la ilaha illa anta bikulli shay'in `alimun

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god save You; You have knowledge of all things.
Perfect is Your brilliance; so, You have guided (to the true guidance).
Open is Your hand; so, You have given (liberally).
Our Lord, Your Face is the noblest of all faces,
Your side is the best of all sides,
and Your donations are the most favorable and most pleasant of all donations.
Our Lord, when You are obeyed, You thank

segments.transliteration: wa tu`sa rabbana fataghfiru liman shi'ta

translation.en: and when You, our Lord, is disobeyed, You forgive whomever You wish.
You respond to the distressed,
relieve from evil,
accept repentance,
and pardon the sins.
Your bestowals are too abundant to be recompensed,
Your graces are too many to be counted,

segments.transliteration: wa la yablughu midhataka qawlu qa'ilin

translation.en: and You are too praised to be expressed by any statement.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please) bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,
hasten their relief, comfort,
rest, and pleasure,
make me taste the flavor of their relief,

segments.transliteration: wa ahlik a`da'ahum min aljinni wal-insi

translation.en: annihilate their jinn and human enemies,
grant us good in this world
and good in the Hereafter,
and protect us against the chastisement of fire.

segments.arabic: وَٱجْعَلْنَا مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ لاَ خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلاَ هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
segments.transliteration: waj`alna min alladhina la khawfun `alayhim wa la hum yahzanuna

translation.en: Include us with those upon whom no fear shall come nor shall they grieve.
Include me with those who have acted patiently
and on their Lord do they rely.
Confirm me with the sure word

segments.transliteration: fi alhayati alddunya wa fi al-akhirati

translation.en: in this worldly life and in the Hereafter.

segments.transliteration: wa barik li fi almahya walmamati

translation.en: Bless me in my lifetime, in my death,
on the Situation, in the Resurrection,
in the Settling of account, in the Scale,
and at the horrors of the Resurrection Day,
keep me safe on the Discriminating Bridge, and make me pass it safely.
Provide me with useful knowledge,
true certitude,

segments.transliteration: wa tuqan wa birran wa wara`an

translation.en: piety, righteousness, devoutness,
fear of You, and dread of You
due to which I attain a nearer step towards You
and I avoid any remoter step from You.
Endear me (to people) and do not make the others incur the hatred of me.
Take care of me and do not disappoint me.

segments.arabic: وَأَعْطِنِي مِنْ جَمِيعِ خَيْرِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلآخِرَةِ
segments.transliteration: wa a`tini min jami`i khayri alddunya wal-akhirati

translation.en: Grant me a part of the whole welfare of this world and the Hereafter,

segments.arabic: مَا عَلِمْتُ مِنْهُ وَمَا لَمْ أَعْلَمْ
segments.transliteration: ma `alimtu minhu wa ma lam a`lam

translation.en: including what I know and what I do not.

segments.transliteration: wa ajirni min alssu'i kullihi bihadhafirihi

translation.en: Save me from the whole evil altogether,

segments.arabic: مَا عَلِمْتُ مِنْهُ وَمَا لَمْ أَعْلَمْ
segments.transliteration: ma `alimtu minhu wa ma lam a`lam

translation.en: including what I know and what I do not.

text.en: Sixteenth: Mu`awiyah ibn `Ammar has reported that he once asked Imam al-Sadiq (`a) to teach him exclusively a special supplicatory prayer. The Imam (`a) therefore taught him to say this:

translation.en: O One! O Glorious!
O One and Only! O Eternally besought of all!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ
segments.transliteration: ya man lam yalid wa lam yulad

translation.en: O He Who begets not nor is He begotten

segments.transliteration: wa lam yakun lahu kufwan ahadun

translation.en: and none is like Him!

segments.arabic: يَا عَزِيزُ يَا كَرِيمُ يَا حَنَّانُ

translation.en: O Almighty! O All-generous! O All-tender!
O Hearer of prayers!
O most magnanimous of all those who may donate
and best of all those who may give!

segments.arabic: يَا اللَّهُ يَا اللَّهُ يَا اللَّهُ
segments.transliteration: ya allahu ya allahu ya allahu

translation.en: O Allah! O Allah! O Allah!

segments.transliteration: qulta walaqad nadana nuhun falani`ma almujibuna

translation.en: You have said, “And Noah did certainly call upon Us, and most excellent answerer of prayer We are.”

text.en: Commenting on this sentence, Imam al-Sadiq (`a) said, “Upon hearing this verse, the Holy Prophet (s) used to say:

translation.en: Yes indeed! Most excellent answerer of prayer You are.
Most excellent besought One You are!
Most excellent prayed One You are!
I beseech You in the name of the brilliance of Your Face,

segments.transliteration: wa as'aluka bi`izzatika wa qudratika wa jabarutika

translation.en: I beseech You in the name of Your almightiness, omnipotence, and grandeur,

segments.transliteration: wa as'aluka bimalakutika wa dir`ika alhasinati

translation.en: and I beseech You in the name of Your sovereign power, Your invulnerable armor,
Your wholeness, all of Your supports,
in the name of Muhammad,
and in the name of the successors after Muhammad

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَنْ…
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin…

translation.en: to bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad and to…

text.en: You may now submit your requests.
Seventeenth: A man from al-Kufah city, named Abu-Ja`far, is reported to have asked Imam al-Sadiq (`a) to teach him a supplicatory prayer to say. The Imam (`a) thus taught him to say this:

translation.en: O He Whom alone I hope for all good things!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ آمَنُ سَخَطَهُ عِنْدَ كُلِّ عَثْرَةٍ
segments.transliteration: wa ya man amanu sakhatahu `inda kulli `athratin

translation.en: O He Whose wrath I do not expect whenever I commit a slip!

segments.transliteration: wa ya man yu`ti bilqalili alkathira

translation.en: O He Who recompenses the little deed with very much reward!
O He Who has answered one who begged Him
out of His kindness and mercifulness!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ أَعْطَىٰ مَنْ لَمْ يَسْأَلْهُ وَلَمْ يَعْرِفْهُ
segments.transliteration: ya man a`ta man lam yas'alhu wa lam ya`rifhu

translation.en: O He Who has given those who do not ask Him and do not even recognize Him!
(Please) bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.arabic: وَأَعْطِنِي بِمَسْأَلتِي مِنْ جَمِيعِ خَيْرِ ٱلدُّنْيَا
segments.transliteration: wa a`tini bimas'alti min jami`i khayri alddunya

translation.en: give me, for I pray You, the whole welfare of this world
and the whole welfare of the Hereafter.
Verily, all that which You give is unblemished.

segments.arabic: وَزِدْنِي مِنْ سَعَةِ فَضْلِكَ يَا كَرِيمُ
segments.transliteration: wa zidni min sa`ati fadlika ya karimu

translation.en: (Please do) increase Your favors upon me, O All-generous!

text.en: Eighteenth: Imam al-Baqir (`a) is reported to have instructed his brother, `Abdullah ibn `Ali, to say this supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: O Allah, (please) certify my expectation (of Your mercy) without disappointment,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ تُطْمِعْ فِيَّ عَدُوّاً وَلاَ حَاسِداً
segments.transliteration: wa la tutmi` fiyya `aduwwan wa la hasedan

translation.en: do not entice any enemy or envious one against me,
and safeguard me in my standing up, sitting,
waking, and sleeping.
O Allah, (please) forgive me, have mercy on me,
guide me to Your straightest path,
guard me against the heat of Hellfire,
release me from debts and sinning,
and make me of the best creatures.

text.en: Nineteenth: The following supplicatory prayer is reported to be the supplication of insistence:

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ ٱلسَّمَاوَاتِ ٱلسَّبْعِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُنَّ
segments.transliteration: allahumma rabba alssamawati alssab`i wa ma baynahunna

translation.en: O Allah, Lord of the seven skies and whatever is between them,
Lord of the Great Throne,

segments.transliteration: wa rabba jabra'ila wa mika'ila wa israfila

translation.en: Lord of Gabriel, Michael, and Seraph,
Lord of the Great Qur'an,
and Lord of Muhammad the Seal of the Prophets,

segments.arabic: إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِٱلَّذِي تَقُومُ بِهِ ٱلسَّمَاءُ
segments.transliteration: inni as'aluka billadhi taqumu bihi alssama'u

translation.en: I beseech You in the name of that by which the sky subsists erect,
the earth subsists,
the assemblage is scattered,
the scattering thing is assembled,
You have given the living (creatures) sustenance,
and You have counted the number of the pebbles,
the weight of the mountains,
and the measure of the waters of oceans…

text.en: You may now invoke Almighty Allah’s blessings upon the Holy Prophet and his Household and submit your requests, preferably insistently.
Twentieth: Ibn Abi-Ya`fur, the prominent and trustworthy narrator, is reported to have said that Imam al-Sadiq (`a) used to say this supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: O Allah, (please) fill in my heart with love for You,
fear of You,
belief, faith in You,

segments.transliteration: wa faraqan minka wa shawqan ilayka

translation.en: dread of You, and longing for You.
O Lord of majesty and honor!
O Allah, endear to myself my meeting with You,

segments.arabic: وَٱجْعَلْ لِي فِي لِقَائِكَ خَيْرَ ٱلرَّحْمَةِ وَٱلْبَرَكَةِ
segments.transliteration: waj`al li fi liqa'ika khayra alrrahmati walbarakati

translation.en: amass the best of mercy and blessings for me in my meeting with You,
add me to the righteous ones,
do not delay me with the evil ones,
include me with the past righteous ones,
line up me with the current righteous ones,
lead me to the path of the righteous ones,
help me against myself

segments.arabic: بِمَا تُعِينُ بِهِ ٱلصَّالِحِينَ عَلَىٰ أَنْفُسِهِمْ

translation.en: just as You help the righteous ones against themselves,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ تَرُدَّنِي فِي سُوءٍ ٱسْتَنْقَذْتَنِي مِنْهُ
segments.transliteration: wa la taruddani fi su'in istanqadhtani minhu

translation.en: and do not let me return to a wicked thing from which You have saved me,
O Lord of the worlds!

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ إِيمَاناً لاَ أَجَلَ لَهُ دُونَ لِقَائِكَ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka imanan la ajala lahu duna liqa'ika

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You for faith that does not stop until I meet You.
As long as You grant me lifetime and when You cause me to die, (please do) connect me to that faith
and resurrect me carrying it.

segments.transliteration: wa abri' qalbi min alrriya'i walssum`ati

translation.en: Release my heart from showing off, seeking for reputation,
and suspicion in the affairs of Your religion.
O Allah, grant me victory in attaining the faith You please,
capability to serve You,
discernment of Your creation,
and two folds of Your mercy.
And (please) make my face glitter on account of Your light,
make me desire for nothing but that which You hold,
and when I die, make me die following Your Path
on the faith You please and carrying the principles of Your Prophet.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ ٱلْكَسَلِ وَٱلْهَرَمِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni a`udhu bika min alkasali walharami

translation.en: O Allah, I pray Your protection against laziness, senility,
cowardice, stinginess,
inattentiveness, brutality,
spiritlessness, and poverty.

segments.arabic: وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ يَا رَبِّ مِنْ نَفْسٍ لاَ تَشْبَعُ
segments.transliteration: wa a`udhu bika ya rabbi min nafsin la tashba`u

translation.en: I also pray Your protection against having unsatisfied self,
fearless heart,
unanswered supplications,
and futile prayers.

segments.transliteration: wa u`idhu bika nafsi wa ahli wa dhurriyyati

translation.en: I also pray You to guard myself, my family members, and my progeny
against Satan the accursed.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنَّهُ لاَ يُجِيرُنِي مِنْكَ أَحَدٌ
segments.transliteration: allahumma innahu la yujiruni minka ahadun

translation.en: O Allah, none can ever save me against You

segments.arabic: وَلاَ أَجِدُ مِنْ دُو نِكَ مُلْتَحَداً
segments.transliteration: wa la ajidu min dunika multahadan

translation.en: and I can never find any shelter against You.

segments.arabic: فَلاَ تَخْذُلْنِي وَلاَ تَرُدَّنِي فِي هَلَكَةٍ
segments.transliteration: fala takhdhulni wa la taruddani fi halakatin

translation.en: So, (please) do not disappoint me, do not let me lead myself to perdition,
and do not make me deserve chastisement.
I beseech You for steadfastness on Your religion,
giving credence to Your Book,
and following Your Messenger.
O Allah, (please) refer to me with Your mercy
but do not refer to me with my sin.

segments.transliteration: wa taqabbal minni wa zidni min fadlika

translation.en: Accept from me and increase Your favors to me,
for I desire for You.
O Allah, (please) make the reward for my utterance
and the reward of my sessions
to be Your pleasure with me,
cause my deed and my prayers to be purely intended for You,
make Paradise to be my reward, out of Your mercy,
grant me all what I have asked You for entirely,
and endue me with even more favors,
for I desire for none save You.
O Allah, all stars sink
and all eyes sleep
while You are the Ever-living, the Self-subsisting.
Verily, no still night,
no sky displaying the zodiacal signs,
no widely expansive land,
no vast abysmal ocean,

segments.transliteration: wa la zulumatun ba`duha fawqa ba`din

translation.en: no depths of darkness one above another—none of these can ever hide itself or anyone from You.

segments.arabic: تُدْلِجُ ٱلرَّحْمَةَ عَلَىٰ مَنْ تَشَاءُ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ
segments.transliteration: tudliju alrrahmata `ala man tasha'u min khalqika

translation.en: You cover any of Your beings that You wish with mercy.

segments.arabic: تَعْلَمُ خَائِنَةَ ٱلأَعْيُنِ وَمَا تُخْفِي ٱلصُّدُورُ
segments.transliteration: ta`lamu kha'inata al-a`yuni wa ma tukhfi alssuduru

translation.en: You know the stealthy looks and that which the breasts conceal.

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ بِمَا شَهِدْتَ بِهِ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِكَ

translation.en: I bear witness for what You have borne witness for Yourself

segments.transliteration: wa shahidat mala'ikatuka wa ulu al`ilmi

translation.en: and what Your angels and possessors of knowledge have witnessed for You;

segments.arabic: لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta al`azizu alhakimu

translation.en: there is no god save You; You are the Almighty, the All-wise.

segments.arabic: وَمَنْ لَمْ يَشْهَدْ عَلَىٰ مَا شَهِدْتَ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa man lam yashhad `ala ma shahidta `ala nafsika

translation.en: If one who has not borne witness for what You have borne witness for Yourself

segments.transliteration: wa shahidat mala'ikatuka wa ulu al`ilmi

translation.en: and what Your angels and the possessors of knowledge have borne witness for You,
then (please) register my testimony instead of his testimony.
O Allah, You are the peace
and the source of peace.
I beseech You, O Lord of majesty and honor,
to release me from the Fire.

text.en: Shaykh al-Tusi, in Misbah al-Mutahajjid, has referred to the above-mentioned supplicatory prayer to be said immediately after the fourth unit of the Night Prayer. As for al-Majlisi (the compiler of Bihar al-Anwar), he reported Imam al-Sadiq (`a) to have recommended saying this prayer in the one-unit al-Witr Prayer (which is performed after the eight-unit Night Prayer and the two-unit al-Shaf` Prayer at the early dawn).

Twenty-first: The following supplicatory prayer is reportedly ascribed to Abu-Dharr. About it, Archangel Gabriel is reported to have said to the Holy Prophet (s), “This supplicatory prayer is well known by the inhabitants of the heavens:”

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You for security, true faith,
giving credence to Your Prophet,
safety from all sorts of tribulation,
showing gratitude for wellbeing,
and dispensing with the evil people.

text.en: Al-Du`a' al-Jami`the All-inclusive Supplicatory Prayer
Twenty-second: Abu-Hamzah is reported to have said: I have received this supplicatory prayer from Imam al-Baqir (`a) who used to describe it as the all-inclusive supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah; the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لاَ إِلٰهَ إلاَّ ٱللَّهُ
segments.transliteration: ashhadu an la ilaha illa allahu

translation.en: I bear witness that there is no god save Allah,
One and Only and having no associate.

segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu anna muhammadan `abduhu wa rasuluhu

translation.en: I also bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and messenger.
I believe in Allah, in all of His messengers,

segments.arabic: وَبِجَمِيعِ مَا أُنْزِلَ بِهِ عَلَىٰ جَمِيعِ ٱلرُّسُلِ
segments.transliteration: wa bijami`i ma unzila bihi `ala jami`i alrrusuli

translation.en: and in all what has been revealed to all of the messengers.
(I believe that) the promise of Allah is true,
His meeting is true,
Allah is always true,
the messengers have conveyed,
and all praise be to Allah the Lord of the worlds.

segments.arabic: وَسُبْحَانَ ٱللَّهِ كُلَّمَا سَبَّحَ ٱللَّهَ شَيْءٌ
segments.transliteration: wa subhana allahi kullama sabbaha allaha shay'un

translation.en: All glory be to Allah whenever a thing glorifies Allah

segments.transliteration: wa kama yuhibbu allahu an yusabbaha

translation.en: and as exactly as Allah likes to be glorified.

segments.arabic: وَٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ كُلَّمَا حَمِدَ ٱللَّهَ شَيْءٌ
segments.transliteration: walhamdu lillahi kullama hamida allaha shay'un

translation.en: All praise be to Allah whenever a thing praises Allah

segments.transliteration: wa kama yuhibbu allahu an yuhmada

translation.en: and as exactly as Allah likes to be praised.

segments.arabic: وَلاَ إِلٰهَ إلاَّ ٱللَّهُ كُلَّمَا هَلَّلَ ٱللَّهَ شَيْءٌ
segments.transliteration: wa la ilaha illa allahu kullama hallala allaha shay'un

translation.en: There is no god save Allah whenever a thing professes that there is no god save Allah

segments.transliteration: wa kama yuhibbu allahu an yuhallala

translation.en: and exactly as Allah likes to be professed that there is no god save Him.

segments.arabic: وَٱللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ كُلَّمَا كَبَّرَ ٱللَّهَ شَيْءٌ
segments.transliteration: wallahu akbaru kullama kabbara allaha shay'un

translation.en: Allah is the Most Great whenever a thing professes that Allah is the Most Great

segments.transliteration: wa kama yuhibbu allahu an yukabbara

translation.en: and as exactly as Allah likes to be professed that He is the Most Great.
O Allah, I beseech You for the keys to the good
as well as its seals, its abundant quantities,
its advantages, its blessings,
the scope that I know about it,
and the scope that is too much for me to retain.
O Allah, (please) make clear for me the elements of recognizing it (i.e. the good),
open before me the doors to it,
cover me with the blessings of Your mercy,

segments.arabic: وَمُنَّ عَلَيَّ بِعِصْمَةٍ عَنِ ٱلإِزَالَةِ عَنْ دِينِكَ

translation.en: bestow upon me with protection against divergence from Your religion,
and purify my heart from suspicion.
(Please) do not make my heart to be preoccupied by my worldly affairs
and my transient livelihood
against the everlasting reward in my other world;

segments.arabic: وَٱشْغَلْ قَلْبِي بِحِفْظِ مَا لاَ تَقْبَلُ مِنِّي جَهْلَهُ
segments.transliteration: washghal qalbi bihifzi ma la taqbalu minni jahlahu

translation.en: rather, (please) make my heart to be occupied by retaining that which You do not accept to be ignored.
And (please) make my tongue subservient to mentioning all virtuous items,
purify my heart from ostentation,
and do not allow ostentation to flow in my organs;
rather, make all my deeds to be purely intended for Your sake.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ ٱلشَّرِّ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni a`udhu bika min alshsharri

translation.en: O Allah, I pray Your protection against the evil
and all sorts of indecencies;
those of them which are apparent, those which are concealed, and those which are committed inattentively,

segments.arabic: وَجَمِيعِ مَا يُرِيدُنِي بِهِ ٱلشَّيْطَانُ ٱلرَّجِيمُ
segments.transliteration: wa jami`i ma yuriduni bihi alshshaytanu alrrajimu

translation.en: as well as whatever Satan the accursed

segments.transliteration: wa ma yuriduni bihi alssultanu al`anidu

translation.en: and the obstinate ruling authority want me to commit;
i.e. things that Your knowledge has encompassed.
You positively have the power to dispel all that from me.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ طَوَارِقِ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلإِنْسِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni a`udhu bika min tawariqi aljinni wal-insi

translation.en: O Allah, I pray Your protection against the striking of jinn and human beings

segments.transliteration: wa zawabi`ihim wa bawa'iqihim wa maka'idihim

translation.en: as well as against their violent commotions, calamities, conspiracies,

segments.transliteration: wa mashahidi alfasaqati min aljinni wal-insi

translation.en: and the fields used by the wicked jinn and human beings.
I also pray Your protection lest I am led astray from my religion
and thus my result will be worthless in the Hereafter,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ يَكُونَ ذٰلِكَ مِنْهُمْ ضَرَراً عَلَيَّ فِي مَعَاشِي
segments.transliteration: wa an yakuna dhalika minhum dararan `alayya fi ma`ashi

translation.en: or they (i.e. the wicked jinn and human beings) may cause damage to my livelihood,
or they may inflict me with tribulation
that I can neither tolerate
nor stand patiently.
So, O my God, I please You not to make me suffer from such things;
lest, they may stand against remembering You
or take my entire attention against serving You.
Yet, it is You Who can protect, guard,
defend, and preserve me from all that.

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ ٱللَّهُمَّ ٱلرَّفَاهِيَّةَ فِي مَعِيشَتِي عَلَىٰ مَا أَبْقَيْتَنِي
segments.transliteration: as'aluka allahumma alrrafahiyyata fi ma`ishati `ala ma abqaytani

translation.en: I beseech You, O Allah, for luxury in my life as long as You keep me alive;
such life by which I can be strong enough to obey You,
to attain Your pleasure,

segments.arabic: وَأَصِيرُ بِهَا إِلَىٰ دَارِ ٱلْحَيَوَانِ غَداً
segments.transliteration: wa asiru biha ila dari alhayawani ghadan

translation.en: and to guarantee my eternal other life in the morrow.
(Please) do not provide me with sustenance due to which I may become inordinate

segments.arabic: وَلاَ تَبْتَلِيَنِّي بِفَقْرٍ أَشْقَىٰ بِهِ مُضَيَّقاً عَلَيَّ
segments.transliteration: wa la tabtaliyanni bifaqrin ashqa bihi mudayyaqan `alayya

translation.en: and do not test me on poverty by which I may be miserable and straitened;
rather, (please) grant me abundant share in my otherworldly life

segments.arabic: وَمَعَاشاً وَاسِعاً هَنِيئاً مَرِيئاً فِي دُنْيَايَ
segments.transliteration: wa ma`ashan wasi`an hani'an mari'an fi dunyaya

translation.en: and expansive, pleasant, and enjoyable living in my worldly life.
Do not cause this world to be like prison for me
and do not cause my departure from it to be grief for me;
rather, protect me against its charms
and decide my deeds in it to be acceptable
and my efforts in it to be praiseworthy.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ وَمَنْ أَرَادَنِي بِسُوءٍ فَأَرِدْهُ بِمِثْلِهِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma wa man aradani bisu'in fa'arid-hu bimithlihi

translation.en: O Allah, thwart whoever desires evil for me with similar evil,
frustrate whoever intends to deceive me,

segments.arabic: وَٱصْرِفْ عَنِّي هَمَّ مَنْ أَدْخَلَ عَلَيَّ هَمَّهُ

translation.en: dispel from me the grief caused to me by any one,
plot against whoever wants to plot against me,
for You are the best of planners,
and gouge out on behalf of me the eyes of the disbelievers, the wrongdoers,
the tyrants, and the envious ones.
O Allah, (please) descend on me tranquility from You,
dress me Your invulnerable armor,
safeguard me with Your preventive covering,
expand over me Your useful wellbeing,
harmonize between my words and my deeds,

segments.arabic: وَبَارِكْ لِي فِي وَلَدِي وَأَهْلِي وَمَالِي
segments.transliteration: wa barik li fi waladi wa ahli wa mali

translation.en: and bless me in my children, family members (or my wife), and property.

segments.transliteration: allahumma ma qaddamtu wa ma akhkhartu

translation.en: O Allah, as for whatever I have done in advance, whatever I have put off,

segments.transliteration: wa ma aghfaltu wa ma ta`ammadtu

translation.en: whatever I have committed inattentively or deliberately,

segments.arabic: وَمَا تَوَانَيْتُ وَمَا أَعْلَنْتُ وَمَا أَسْرَرْتُ
segments.transliteration: wa ma tawanaytu wa ma a`lantu wa ma asrartu

translation.en: whatever I have neglected, and whatever I have done openly or secretly,
(please) forgive me all that, O most merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en: Twenty-third: Muhammad ibn Muslim is reported to have quoted Imam al-Baqir (`a) as advising of saying this supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: O Allah, (please) expand my sustenance for me,
increase my lifetime,
forgive my sin,
make me of those whom You use for backing Your religion,
and do not substitute another in my place.

text.en: Twenty-fourth: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have used to said this supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: O He Who accepts gratefully the little deed,
pardons the many sins,
and He is the Forgiving, the All-merciful,
(please) forgive my sins
whose pleasure has gone but whose consequences are still liable.

text.en: Twenty-fifth: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is also reported to have used to said this supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: O Light! O All-holy!
O Foremost of all first things!
O Last of all last things!
O All-beneficent! O All-merciful!

segments.arabic: ٱغْفِرْ لِيَ الذُّنُوبَ ٱلَّتِي تُغَيِّرُ ٱلنِّعَمَ
segments.transliteration: ighfir liya aldhdhunuba allati tughayyiru alnni`ama

translation.en: (Please) forgive me those sins that alter blessings!

segments.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِيَ ٱلذُّنُوبَ ٱلَّتِي تُحِلُّ ٱلنِّقَمَ
segments.transliteration: waghfir liya aldhdhunuba allati tuhillu alnniqama

translation.en: Forgive me those sins that make fall punishments!

segments.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِيَ ٱلذُّنُوبَ ٱلَّتِي تَهْتِكُ ٱلْعِصَمَ
segments.transliteration: waghfir liya aldhdhunuba allati tahtiku al`isama

translation.en: Forgive me those sins that tear apart safeguards!

segments.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِيَ ٱلذُّنُوبَ ٱلَّتِي تُنْزِلُ ٱلْبَلاَءَ
segments.transliteration: waghfir liya aldhdhunuba allati tunzilu albala'a

translation.en: Forgive me those sins that draw down tribulations!

segments.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِيَ ٱلذُّنُوبَ ٱلَّتِي تُدِيلُ ٱلأَعْدَاءَ
segments.transliteration: waghfir liya aldhdhunuba allati tudilu al-a`da'a

translation.en: Forgive me those sins that give ascendancy to the enemies!

segments.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِيَ ٱلذُّنُوبَ ٱلَّتِي تُعَجِّلُ ٱلْفَنَاءَ
segments.transliteration: waghfir liya aldhdhunuba allati tu`ajjilu alfana'a

translation.en: Forgive me those sins that hasten extinction!

segments.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِيَ ٱلذُّنُوبَ ٱلَّتِي تَقْطَعُ ٱلرَّجَاءَ
segments.transliteration: waghfir liya aldhdhunuba allati taqta`u alrraja'a

translation.en: Forgive me those sins that cut off hope!

segments.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِيَ ٱلذُّنُوبَ ٱلَّتِي تُظْلِمُ ٱلْهَوَاءَ
segments.transliteration: waghfir liya aldhdhunuba allati tuzlimu alhawa'a

translation.en: Forgive me those sins that darken atmospheres!

segments.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِيَ ٱلذُّنُوبَ ٱلَّتِي تَكْشِفُ ٱلْغِطَاءَ
segments.transliteration: waghfir liya aldhdhunuba allati takshifu alghita'a

translation.en: Forgive me those sins that uncover the covering!

segments.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِيَ ٱلذُّنُوبَ ٱلَّتِي تَرُدُّ ٱلدُّعَاءَ
segments.transliteration: waghfir liya aldhdhunuba allati taruddu alddu`a'a

translation.en: Forgive me those sins that cause prayers to be rejected!

segments.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِيَ ٱلذُّنُوبَ ٱلَّتِي تَرُدُّ غَيْثَ ٱلسَّمَاءِ
segments.transliteration: waghfir liya aldhdhunuba allati taruddu ghaytha alssama'i

translation.en: Forgive me those sins that preclude the water of the sky.

text.en: Twenty-sixth: This supplicatory prayer is also reported from Imam Ja`far al-Sadiq (`a):

translation.en: O my means in my agonies!
O my companion in my hardships!
O my benefactor in my amenities!
O my aide in my desires!

text.en: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) added: This is the supplicatory prayer of Imam `Ali Amir al-Mu'minin (`a):

translation.en: O Allah, You have recorded that which we leave behind,
known all news,
and come to know all secrets;
You have thus intervened between our hearts and us.
So, for You, a secret is not secret
and hearts are unbosomed before You.

segments.transliteration: wa innama amruka lishay'in idha aradtahu

translation.en: Your order for a thing when You intend it,
is only that You say to it, “Be,” and it is.
So, (please) in the name of Your mercy, tell the obedience to You

segments.arabic: أَنْ تَدْخُلَ فِي كُلِّ عُضْوٍ مِنْ أَعْضَائِي
segments.transliteration: an tadkhula fi kulli `udwin min a`da'i

translation.en: to enter all of my organs
and not to leave them until I meet You.
And, in the name of Your mercy, tell the disobedience to You

segments.arabic: أَنْ تَخْرُجَ مِنْ كُلِّ عُضْوٍ مِنْ أَعْضَائِي
segments.transliteration: an takhruja min kulli `udwin min a`da'i

translation.en: to depart all of my organs
and not to come close to me until I meet You.

segments.transliteration: warzuqni min alddunya wa zahhidni fiha

translation.en: And (please) provide me from the worldly sustenance, but induce me to renounce the world,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ تَزْوِهَا عَنِّي وَرَغْبَتِي فِيهَا يَا رَحْمٰنُ
segments.transliteration: wa la tazwiha `annai wa raghbati fiha ya rahmanu

translation.en: and do not turn it away from me while I have a desire in it, O All-beneficent!

text.en: Twenty-seventh: `Ali ibn Ibrahim has reported on the authority of his father on the authority of Ibn Mahbub on the authority of `Ala' ibn Ruzayn on the authority of `Abd al-Rahman ibn Sayyabah who said: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) gave me this supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: All praise be to Allah; the Patron of praise

segments.transliteration: wa ahlihi wa muntahahu wa mahallihi

translation.en: and its worthiest, utmost, and center.
Sincere is he who professes of His Oneness,
truly guided is he who serves Him,
triumphant is he who obeys Him,
and secured is he who takes shelter with Him.
O Allah, O Lord of magnanimity, glory,
excellent extolment, and praise,

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ مَسْأَلَةَ مَنْ خَضَعَ لَكَ بِرَقَبَتِهِ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka mas'alata man khada`a laka biraqabatihi

translation.en: I beseech You the beseeching of one who subjugated his neck to You,
made himself subordinate to You,
covered his face with soil for You,
humiliated himself to You,
shed his tears for fear of You,
sobbed for You,
and confessed of his sins;
one whose wrongdoing exposed him before You
and whose crime disgraced him before You;
therefore, his power diminished,
his stratagem failed,
the ways of his trickeries broke down,
all of his wrong things vanished,

segments.arabic: وَأَلْجَأَتْهُ ذُنُوبُهُ إِلَىٰ ذُلِّ مَقَامِهِ بَيْنَ يَدَيْكَ
segments.transliteration: wa alja'at-hu dhunubuhu ila dhulli maqamihi bayna yadayka

translation.en: and his sins compelled him to present himself in a humiliating situation in Your Presence,
to submit himself to You,
and to implore You earnestly.

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ ٱللَّهُمَّ سُؤَالَ مَنْ هُوَ بِمَنْزِلَتِهِ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka allahumma su'ala man huwa bimanzilatihi

translation.en: I thus beseech You the beseeching of such one,
desire for You the same desiring of him,
humiliate myself before You just as he does,
and implore You as earnestly as he does.
O Allah, (please) have mercy upon the humiliation in my words,
the meekness of my situation and condition,
and the subjugating of my neck to You.
I beseech You, O Allah, for guidance against deviation,
insight against blindness,
and orthodoxy against heterodoxy.

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُكَ ٱللَّهُمَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلْحَمْدِ عِنْدَ ٱلرَّخَاءِ
segments.transliteration: wa as'aluka allahumma akthara alhamdi `inda alrrakha'i

translation.en: I also beseech You, O Allah, for the most of praise in situations of luxurious wellbeing,
the comeliest patience in misfortunes,
the best gratitude in situations of thanking,
and submission in seditious situations.
I also beseech You for capability to obey You,
incapability to disobey You,
resorting to You from You,
seeking nearness to You, O my Lord, so that Your pleasure is obtained,
and inspection of whatever pleases You with me
although it may irate Your creatures,
for purpose of winning Your pleasure.

segments.arabic: رَبِّ مَنْ أَرْجُوهُ إِنْ لَمْ تَرْحَمْنِي
segments.transliteration: rabbi man arjuhu in lam tarhamni

translation.en: O my Lord, whom will I hope if You do not have mercy upon me?

segments.arabic: أَوْ مَنْ يَعُودُ عَلَيَّ إِنْ أَقْصَيْتَنِي

translation.en: Who will grant me graces if You put me aside?

segments.arabic: أَوْ مَنْ يَنْفَعُنِي عَفْوُهُ إِنْ عَاقَبْتَنِي

translation.en: Whose pardon will benefit me if You punish me?

segments.arabic: أَوْ مَنْ آمُلُ عَطَايَاهُ إِنْ حَرَمْتَنِي

translation.en: Whose donations will I expect if You deprive me?

segments.arabic: أَوْ مَنْ يَمْلِكُ كَرَامَتِي إِنْ أَهَنْتَنِي
segments.transliteration: aw man yamliku karamati in ahantani

translation.en: Who can honor me if You dishonor me?

segments.arabic: أَوْ مَنْ يَضُرُّنِي هَوَانُهُ إِنْ أَكْرَمْتَنِي
segments.transliteration: aw man yadurruni hawanuhu in akramtani

translation.en: Whose humiliation will I care for if You honor me?
O my Lord, how awful my deed is!
How hideous my acts are!
How hard my heart is!
How procrastinative my hope is!
How short my lifetime is!
How daring I am to disobey my Creator!
O my Lord, but how excellent Your favors to me are
and how manifest Your graces on me are!

segments.arabic: كَثُرَتْ عَلَيَّ مِنْكَ ٱلنِّعَمُ فَمَا أُحْصِيهَا

translation.en: Your blessings to me have been too many to be counted by me,

segments.transliteration: wa qalla minniya alshshukru fima awlaytanihi

translation.en: but my thanks to You have been too little if compared to what You have given me.
I therefore have despised Your blessings,
exposed myself to punishments,
neglected remembering You,
entered to ignorance after knowledge,

segments.transliteration: wa juztu min al`adli ila alzzulmi

translation.en: deviated from justice to injustice,
passed over virtue to sinning,

segments.arabic: وَصِرْتُ إِلَىٰ ٱللَّهْوِ مِنَ ٱلْخَوْفِ وَٱلْحُزْنِ
segments.transliteration: wa sirtu ila allahwi min alkhawfi walhuzni

translation.en: and shifted from fear and dread of You to heedless amusement.
How little and scarce my good deeds are
if compared to my numerous sins

segments.arabic: وَأَعْظَمَهَا عَلَىٰ قَدْرِ صِغَرِ خَلْقِي وَضَعْفِ رُكْنِي
segments.transliteration: wa a`zamaha `ala qadri sighari khalqi wa da`fi rukni

translation.en: and how grave my sins are if compared to my worthless form and my powerless support!

segments.arabic: رَبِّ وَمَا أَطْوَلَ أَمَلِي فِي قِصَرِ أَجَلِي
segments.transliteration: rabbi wa ma atwala amali fi qisari ajali

translation.en: O my Lord, how optimistic my hope is if compared to my fleeting lifetime!

segments.transliteration: wa aqsara ajali fi bu`di amali

translation.en: How fleeing my lifetime is if compared to my long hope!
How hideous my hidden self is if compared to my open manners!

segments.arabic: رَبِّ لاَ حُجَّةَ لِي إِنِ ٱحْتَجَجْتُ
segments.transliteration: rabbi la hujjata li in ihtajajtu

translation.en: O my Lord, I lack any argument if I try to provide a pretext,
I am unexcused if I try to apologize,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ شُكْرَ عِنْدِي إِنِ ا بْلِيتُ وَأُوْلِيتُ
segments.transliteration: wa la shukra `indi in ublitu wa ulitu

translation.en: and I have no gratitude in all what has been donated and bestowed to me

segments.arabic: إِنْ لَمْ تُعِنِّي عَلَىٰ شُكْرِ مَا أَوْلَيْتَ
segments.transliteration: in lam tu`inni `ala shukri ma awlayta

translation.en: unless You help me show gratitude to You for what You have endued me with.

segments.arabic: رَبِّي مَا أَخَفَّ مِيزَانِي غَداً إِنْ لَمْ تُرَجِّحْهُ
segments.transliteration: rabbi ma akhaffa mizani ghadan in lam turajjihhu

translation.en: O my Lord, the scale of my deeds in the morrow shall be really light if You do not give preponderance to it.

segments.transliteration: wa azalla lisani in lam tuthabbit-hu

translation.en: my tongue will slip terribly if You do not make it firm,

segments.transliteration: wa aswada wajhi in lam tubayyidhu

translation.en: and my face will be blackened totally, if You do not whiten it.

segments.arabic: رَبِّ كَيْفَ لِي بِذُنُوبِيَ ٱلَّتِي سَلَفَتْ مِنِّي
segments.transliteration: rabbi kayfa li bidhunubiya allati salafat minni

translation.en: O my Lord, what should I do about my past sins
that have demolished my posts?
O my Lord, how is it reasonable for me to seek the worldly lusts
and weep for my failure in gaining them,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ أَبْكِي وَتَشْتَدُّ حَسَرَاتِي عَلَىٰ عِصْيَانِي وَتَفْرِيطِي
segments.transliteration: wa la abki wa tashtaddu hasarati `ala `isyani wa tafriti

translation.en: while I should better weep with intense regret for my disobedience to You and my violation of Yourcommands?
O my Lord, when the worldly seductive matters called upon me,
I responded to them without delay
and I inclined to them obediently,
but when the affairs of the Hereafter called upon me,
I slowed down

segments.transliteration: wa abta'tu fi al-ijabati walmusara`ati ilayha

translation.en: and lagged in responding to them and in hurrying towards them
unlike my hurrying in responding to the seductive matters of this world
along with its extinct ruins,
dying chaff,
and vanishing mirage.
O my Lord, You have fascinated me, filled me with desire,
claimed against me with my servitude to You,
and guaranteed my sustenance;
therefore, I felt secured from You,
slowed down despite Your stimulating,
neglected paying attention to Your guaranteed sustenance,
and undervalued Your claim.
O Allah, (please) change my security from You
in this world into fear (of You)
and transform my slowing down into yearning for You
and my undervaluing of Your claim into dread of You.

segments.arabic: ثُمَّ رَضِّنِي بِمَا قَسَمْتَ لِي مِنْ رِزْقِكَ
segments.transliteration: thumma raddini bima qasamta li min rizqika

translation.en: Then, (please) make me satisfied with the sustenance You have decided for me.
O All-generous, I beseech You in the name of Your Great Name
to endue me with Your pleasure at situations that stimulate Your wrath,
to grant me relief at situations of agonies,
to grant me light in darkness,
and to grant me insight when seditious matters come to assimilate themselves to the truth.

segments.arabic: رَبِّ ٱجْعَلْ جُنَّتِي مِنْ خَطَايَايَ حَصِينَةً
segments.transliteration: rabbi ij`al junnati min khatayaya hasinatan

translation.en: O Allah, (please) make Your protection of me against my wrongdoings to be invulnerable,
my ranks in the gardens of Paradise to be elevated,
all of my deeds to be admitted,
and the rewards for my good deeds to be multiplied and increasingly doubled.
I pray Your protection against all seditious matters,

segments.transliteration: ma zahara minha wa ma batana

translation.en: both the apparent and the hidden,
against licentious food and drink,
against the evil of whatever I know

segments.transliteration: wa min sharri ma la a`lamu

translation.en: and against the evil of what I do not know.

segments.arabic: وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ أَنْ أَشْتَرِيَ ٱلْجَهْلَ بِٱلْعِلْمِ
segments.transliteration: wa a`udhu bika min an ashtariya aljahla bil`ilmi

translation.en: I also pray Your protection against that I may exchange knowledge for ignorance,
forbearance for alienation,
justice for bias,
piety for rupture of relations,
tolerance for impatience,
true guidance for deviation,
and belief for disbelief.

text.en: In fact, this supplicatory prayer comprises highly considerable contents. Its reporter is `Abd al-Rahman in Sayyabah whom was given a noteworthy advice by Imam al-Sadiq (`a). Let us now refer to this event:

`Abd al-Rahman ibn Sayyabah: When my father died, one of his friends came to us and knocked the door. When I opened the door, he expressed his sympathy to me and then asked whether my father had left any financial inheritance with us.
When I answered in the negative, the man handed me over a bag of one thousand dirhams and advised me to keep them up, use them in commerce, and gain my living from their profits.

Rejoicing at this, I hurried to my mother and informed her about what had just happened. Later on, I went to one of my father’s friends and asked him to teach me what kind of business I would do with the money. He therefore advised me to trade in a sort of fine cloth. He also purchased a quantity of this cloth and I chose a shop to work in. In fact, Almighty Allah provided me with very much profit in this business. In the season of the hajj pilgrimage, I intended to go on hajj. I therefore informed my mother about this intention of mine and she advised me to give back the amount of money to its owner.

When I did, the man became very happy as if I would donate him the money for nothing. Nevertheless, he offered to me more money, but I thanked him and informed that I would go on the hajj pilgrimage. I then left for Makkah and then returned to al-Madinah. With a group of people, I intended to visit Imam al-Sadiq (`a) who used to permit all people to visit him at that time. Within the group that visited the Imam (`a), I sat in the last raw, since I was young. People began to ask the Imam (`a), he would answer them all, and they would then leave. When a few people only remained, the Imam (`a) pointed to me to come close to him. “Do you need anything?” asked the Imam (`a). “May Allah accept me as ransom for you! I am `Abd al-Rahman the son of Sayyabah,” answered I. The Imam (`a) asked about my father and I told him that he had died.

Expressing sympathy to me, the Imam (`a) asked, “Did he leave any financial inheritance with you?” “No, he did not,” answered I. “How could you then afford this journey of hajj pilgrimage?” asked the Imam (`a). I then told him about the story of these dirhams. Interrupting me, the Imam (`a) asked, “What have you done with these dirhams before you came on hajj pilgrimage?” I answered that I had given back the amount to its owner. “Well done,” said the Imam (`a), “May I advise you of something?” “Yes, you may,” answered I. He thus said, “If you adhere to telling the truth and fulfilling your trusts, you will have a binding share in the properties of all people.”

To explain, the Imam (`a) advised this man to keep up telling the truth in all of his words, to avoid telling lies, to fulfill his promises, to give back the debts to their owners in their limited times, to avoid devouring the others’ dues wrongfully. If one does all that to people, they will give him whenever you ask. If he does so, he will be as if he has a share in their properties.

Back to the event, `Abd al-Rahman added: As I committed myself with this advice of Imam al-Sadiq (`a), I gained so many profits that the religious tax of which was three hundred thousand dirhams one year.
According to another tradition, the aforementioned supplicatory prayer is ascribed to Imam `Ali ibn al-Husayn Zayn al-`ªbidin (`a). However, it ends up with this sentence:

translation.en: (Please) respond, O Lord of the worlds.

text.en: Twenty-eighth: Ibn Mahbub is reported to have said: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) taught a man this supplicatory prayer to say it in his supplications

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You for Your mercy

segments.arabic: ٱلَّتِي لاَ تُنَالُ مِنْكَ إِلاَّ بِرِضَاكَ
segments.transliteration: allati la tunalu minka illa biridaka

translation.en: that cannot be obtained from You except through Your pleasure,
for departure from all acts of disobedience to You,
for entering into all that which achieves Your pleasure,
for redemption from all troubles,
and for a way out from every grave matter
that I have committed deliberately,
I have committed wrongly,

segments.arabic: أَوْ خَطَرَ بِهَا عَلَيَّ خَطَرَاتُ ٱلشَّيْطَانِ

translation.en: or I have committed owing to an evil inspiration of Satan.

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ خَوْفاً تُوقِفُنِي بِهِ عَلَىٰ حُدُودِ رِضَاكَ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka khawfan tuqifuni bihi `ala hududi ridaka

translation.en: I beseech You for fear (of You) by which You stop me at the limits of Your pleasure

segments.arabic: وَتَشَعَّبَ بِهِ عَنِّي كُلُّ شَهْوَةٍ خَطَرَ بِهَا هَوَايَ
segments.transliteration: wa tasha``aba bihi `anni kullu shahwatin khatara biha hawaya

translation.en: and by which You strew about all lusts that come to my mind because of my whims

segments.arabic: وَٱسْتُزِلَّ بِهَا رَأْيي لِيُجَاوِزَ حَدَّ حَلاَلِكَ
segments.transliteration: wastuzilla biha ra'yi liyujawiza hadda halalika

translation.en: or because of the slips of my personal view that violates the bounds of what You have deemed lawful.

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ ٱللَّهُمَّ ٱلأَخْذَ بِأَحْسَنِ مَا تَعْلَمُ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka allahumma al-akhdha bi-ahsani ma ta`lamu

translation.en: I beseech You, O Allah, to lead me to take in the best of what You know,

segments.transliteration: wa tarka sayyi'i kulli ma ta`lamu

translation.en: to shun the evil of all what You know,

segments.arabic: أَوْ أُخْطِئَ مِنْ حَيْثُ لاَ أَعْلَمُ
segments.transliteration: aw ukhti'a min haythu la a`lamu

translation.en: and to avoid committing a wrongdoing from whence I know
and from whence I do not know.
I beseech You for expansion in my sustenance,
abstinence with the minimum necessary sustenance,
finding a way out from all seditious matters with clear demonstration,
hitting the mark in all arguments,
telling the truth under all circumstances,

segments.arabic: وَإِنْصَافَ ٱلنَّاسِ مِنْ نَفْسِي فِيمَا عَلَيَّ وَلِي
segments.transliteration: wa insafa alnnasi min nafsi fima `alayya wa li

translation.en: treating all people fairly whether for my good or the otherwise,
acting humbly in giving people fairness against myself
in all situations of dissatisfaction or satisfaction,

segments.transliteration: wa tarka qalili albaghyi wa kathirihi

translation.en: eschewing any amount of transgression, be it grave or insignificance
and be it in words or in deeds,

segments.transliteration: wa tamama ni`amika fi jami`i al-ashya'i

translation.en: winning Your blessings perfectly in all things,

segments.arabic: وَٱلشُّكْرَ لَكَ عَلَيْهَا لِكَيْ تَرْضَىٰ وَبَعْدَ ٱلرِّضَا
segments.transliteration: walshshukra laka `alayha likay tarda wa ba`da alrrida

translation.en: and showing You gratitude for all such blessings so that You will be pleased with me and even beyond Your pleasure.

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُكَ ٱلْخِيَرَةَ فِي كُلِّ مَا يَكُونُ فِيهِ ٱلْخِيَرَةُ
segments.transliteration: wa as'aluka alkhiyarata fi kulli ma yakunu fihi alkhiyaratu

translation.en: I also beseech You to lead me to the best of all matters that have more than one option,
in the easiest way of all matters, but not in a difficult manner.

segments.arabic: يَا كَرِيمُ يَا كَرِيمُ يَا كَرِيمُ
segments.transliteration: ya karimu ya karimu ya karimu

translation.en: O All-generous! O All-generous! O All-generous!
(Please) open before me the door to attain the mattes
that achieve my wellbeing and my relief,
open before me the door to all such matters,
and make easy for me to find a way out against the opposite.

segments.arabic: وَمَنْ قَدَّرْتَ لَهُ عَلَيَّ مَقْدِرَةً مِنْ خَلْقِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa man qaddarta lahu `alayya maqdiratan min khalqika

translation.en: As for any one of Your creatures whom You have given power over me,
(please) take away from me his hearing, sight,
tongue (i.e. word), and hand (i.e. deed),
and take him away from his right side, his left side,

segments.transliteration: wa min khalfihi wa min quddamihi

translation.en: his back, and his front,
and stop him lest he may pass evil on me.
Strong is he who is in Your vicinity,
glorious is Your praise,
and there is no god but You.
You are my Lord and I am Your servant.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَجَائِي فِي كُلِّ كُرْبَةٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma anta raja'i fi kulli kurbatin

translation.en: You are my hope in all hard times,

segments.transliteration: wa anta thiqati fi kulli shiddatin

translation.en: in You alone I put my trust in all ordeals,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ لِي فِي كُلِّ أَمْرٍ نَزَلَ بِي ثِقَةٌ وَعُدَّةٌ
segments.transliteration: wa anta li fi kulli amrin nazala bi thiqatun wa `uddatun

translation.en: and You alone are my reliance and support in every matter that afflicts me.

segments.arabic: فَكَمْ مِنْ كَرْبٍ يَضْعُفُ عَنْهُ ٱلْفُؤَادُ

translation.en: Too many are the agonies, which the heart is too weak to undergo,
which all stratagems fail to manage,
at which the enemy gloat,
and in which I lose any way out,
but when I referred them to You and complained about them to You,
abandoning all others than You,
You have relieved and saved me from them.
So, You are the patron of all graces,
the One Who stands for all requests,
and the utmost of all desires.
So, very much praise be to You
and abundant gratitude be to You.

text.en: Twenty-ninth: Through a considerable chain of authority, Imam Ja`far al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have taught this supplicatory prayer to Abu-Basir to say it in his supplications::

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ قَوْلَ ٱلتَّوَّابِينَ وَعَمَلَهُمْ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka qawla alttawwabina wa `amalahum

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You for the words of those who turn constantly to You and for their deeds,
the light of the prophets and their honesty,
the salvation of the strivers and their rewards,
the gratitude of the chosen ones and their advice,
the deed of the rememberers and their certitude,
the faith of the scholars and their knowledgeability,
the worship of the dutiful ones and their modesty,
the wisdom of the well-versed scholars and their way,
the fear of the pious ones and their desires,
the belief of the believers and their trust (in You),
and the hope of the good-doers and their devoutness.
O Allah, I beseech You for the reward of the thankful ones,
the rank of the ones drawn near to You,
and the companionship of the prophets.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَوْفَ ٱلْعَامِلِينَ لَكَ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka khawfa al`amilina laka

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You for the fear of those who act for Your sake constantly,
the deed of those who fear You,
the submission of those who worship You,
the certitude of those who put their trusts in You,
and the reliance on You of those who believe in You.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ بِحَاجَتِي عَالِمٌ غَيْرُ مُعَلَّمٍ

translation.en: O Allah, about my request, You know it well and You do not need to be informed about it

segments.transliteration: wa anta laha wasi`un ghayru mutakallafin

translation.en: and You are fully competent to grant it and it does not require You to exert any effort.

segments.transliteration: wa anta alladhi la yuhfika sa'ilun

translation.en: It is You Whom is never disturbed by any suppliant;
and Whose kingdom is never decreased owing to donations;

segments.transliteration: wa la yablughu midhataka qawlu qa'ilin

translation.en: and You are too praised to be expressed by any statement.
You are as exactly as You describe Yourself
but more elevated than what we say about You.
O Allah, (please) grant me close relief,
great reward,
and nice covering.
O Allah, You do know

segments.arabic: أَنِّي عَلَىٰ ظُلْمِي لِنَفْسِي وَإِسْرَافِي عَلَيْهَا

translation.en: that although I have wronged myself and violated it excessively,

segments.arabic: لَمْ أَتَّخِذْ لَكَ ضِدّاً وَلاَ نِدّاً
segments.transliteration: lam attakhidh laka diddan wa la niddan

translation.en: I have never claimed that You have a contrary, a peer,

segments.transliteration: wa la sahibatan wa la waladan

translation.en: a wife, or a son.
O He Who is never confused by requests!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ لاَ يَشْغَلُهُ شَيْءٌ عَنْ شَيْءٍ
segments.transliteration: wa ya man la yashghaluhu shay'un `an shay'in

translation.en: O He Who is never preoccupied by a thing against another thing,
a hearing against another hearing,
or a sight against another sight!
O Who is never annoyed by the insistence of the earnest petitioners,

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تُفَرِّجَ عَنِّي فِي سَاعَتِي هٰذِهِ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka an tufarrija `anni fi sa`ati hadhihi

translation.en: I beseech You to grant me relief at this very moment

segments.arabic: مِنْ حَيْثُ أَحْتَسِبُ وَمِنْ حَيْثُ لاَ أَحْتَسِبُ
segments.transliteration: min haythu ahtasibu wa min haythu la ahtasibu

translation.en: from whence I expect and from whence I do not.
Verily, You give life to bones when they have rotted away.
You verily have power over all things.

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ قَلَّ شُكْرِي لَهُ فَلَمْ يَحْرِمْنِي
segments.transliteration: ya man qalla shukri lahu falam yahrimni

translation.en: O He to Whom I rarely turned thankful; yet, He did not deprive me,
against Whom I committed grand sins; yet, He did not disclose me,
Who saw me insisting on disobeying Him; yet, He did not encountered me,
and Who created me for the purpose He has created me for,
but I committed things purposed for other than the aim for which He has created me.
You are the most excellent Lord, my Master,
but You have found me the most miserable servant.
Most excellent intender You are, my Lord,
but You have found me the most miserable intended one.
I, Your servant and the son of Your two servants,
am before You;
You can do to me whatever You want.
O Allah, all sounds have calmed down,
all motions have been standstill,
and every lover has withdrawn with his beloved;

segments.transliteration: wa khalawtu bika anta almahbubu ilayya

translation.en: so, I am withdrawing with You, my most beloved;

segments.arabic: فَٱجْعَلْ خَلْوَتِي مِنْكَ ٱللَّيْلَةَ ٱلْعِتْقَ مِنَ ٱلنَّارِ
segments.transliteration: faj`al khalwati minka allaylata al`itqa min alnnari

translation.en: therefore, make the reward of my withdrawing with You this night to be release from Hellfire.

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ لَيْسَتْ لِعَالِمٍ فَوْقَهُ صِفَةٌ
segments.transliteration: ya man laysat li`alimin fawqahu sifatun

translation.en: O He above Whose description, there is no knowledgeable one may enjoy a description!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ لَيْسَ لِمَخْلُوقٍ دُونَهُ مَنْعَةٌ
segments.transliteration: ya man laysa limakhluqin dunahu man`atun

translation.en: O He against Whom, there is no creature to prevent itself!
O Foremost before all things!

segments.transliteration: wa ya akhiran ba`da kulli shay'in

translation.en: O Everlasting after all things!
O He Who has no starting point!

segments.transliteration: wa ya man laysa li'akhirihi fana'un

translation.en: O He Who has no final point!
O most perfect of all described things!
O most liberal of all donors!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ يَفْقَهُ بِكُلِّ لُغَةٍ يُدْعَىٰ بِهَا
segments.transliteration: wa ya man yafqahu bikulli lughatin yud`a biha

translation.en: O He Who understands all languages used for praying Him!
O He Whose pardon is eternal,
Whose grip is strong,
and Whose kingdom is straight!

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ بِٱسْمِكَ ٱلَّذِي شَافَهْتَ بِهِ مُوسَىٰ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka bismika alladhi shafahta bihi musa

translation.en: I beseech You in the name of Your Name that You have used in Your speaking with Moses.

segments.arabic: يَا اللَّهُ يَا رَحْمٰنُ يَا رَحِيمُ
segments.transliteration: ya allahu ya rahmanu ya rahimu

translation.en: O Allah! O All-beneficent! O All-merciful!
O He save Whom there is no god!
O Allah, You are verily the eternally besought of all.

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: I beseech You to bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
and to allow me to Paradise out of Your mercy.

text.en: Thirtieth: Yunus is reported to have asked Imam al-Rida (`a) for a brief supplicatory prayer; so, the Imam (`a) taught him to say this:

translation.en: O He Who has guided me to Himself
and humiliated my heart by testifying to Him,
I beseech You for security and faith.

text.en: Salats & Aamal for Hajaat - Baqiyatus salehat
1.Prayer for Settling the Needs

In al-Rawandi’s book of al-Da`awat, it is reported that Imam Zayn al-`ªbidin (`a) passed by a man sitting at the doorstep of another man’s house. “What for are you sitting at the door of this lavish tyrant?” the Imam (`a) asked.“It is because of ordeal,” the man answered. “Stand up, “ the Imam (`a) said, “I will guide you to a door better than this tyrant’s door and to a Lord who is better than this man.”

Hence, the Imam (`a) took the man from the hand to the Holy Prophet’s Mosque where he instructed him to turn his face towards the kiblah direction, offer a two-unit prayer, raise the hands towards Almighty Allah to praise Him, invoke His blessings upon the Holy Prophet and his Household, pray to Him by reciting the last verses of Surah al-Hashr (No. 59), the first six verses of Surah al-Hadid (No. 57), and the two verses in Surah ªl-`Imran (either verses 81-19 according to al-Rawandi, or 26-27 according to al-Majlisi), and then pray to Him for the personal needs. Any prayer that is said in this situation will be definitely granted.

It is worth mentioning that Imam `Ali Amir al-Mu'minin (`a) said, “If you need something to be settled, you may go for seeking it on early Thursdays and you may recite the last verses of Surah ªAle Imran, Ayah al-Kursi, Surah al-Qadr, and Surah al-Fatihah before you leave your house, because these holy verses and Surahs contribute to solving all the needs for this life and the life to come.”
2. A Prayer for Settling Urgent Needs

You may offer a four-unit prayer with precise Qunut and other pillars (i.e. genuflection, prostration, and the other parts of prayers). In the first unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah one time and repeat the following verse seven times:
In the second unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah once and the following verse seven times:

segments.arabic: مَا شَاءَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِٱللَّهِ إِنْ تَرَنِ أَنَٱ أَقَلَّ مِنْكَ مَالاً وَوَلَداً.
