segments.transliteration: ma sha'a allahu la quwwata illa billahi in tarani ana aqalla minka malan wa waladan

text.en: In the third unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah once and the following verse seven times:

segments.arabic: لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ ٱلظَّالِمِينَ.
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu min alzzalimina

text.en: In the fourth unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah once and the following verse seven times:

segments.arabic: وَأُفَوِّضُ أَمْرِي إِلَىٰ اللَّهِ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بَصِيرٌ بِٱلْعِبَادِ.
segments.transliteration: wa ufawwidu amri ila allahi inna allaha basirun bil`ibadi

translation.en: You may then mention your need.

text.en: 3.Prayer for Solving Difficulties

Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have said: When you face a difficulty, you may offer a two-unit prayer at midday. In the first unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah, Surah al-Tawhid, and the first three verses of Surah al-Fath (No. 48):

translation.en: Surely, We have given to you a clear victor,

segments.arabic: لِيَغْفِرَ لَكَ ٱللَّهُ مَا تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِكَ وَمَا تَأَخَّرَ
segments.transliteration: liyaghfira laka allahu ma taqaddama min dhanbika wa ma ta'akhkhara

translation.en: that Allah may forgive your community their past faults and those to follow
and complete His favor to you
and keep you on a right way,
and that Allah might help you with a mighty help.

text.en: In the second unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah, Surah al-Tawhid, and <ref=1:94>Surah Al-Sharh</ref> . This prayer has been personally experienced.
4.Prayer for Settling the Needs
The following is quoted from the book of Makarim al-Akhlaq:

At midnight, you may bathe yourself and offer a two-unit prayer. In both units, you should recite Surah al-Fatihah once and repeat Surah al-Tawhid five hundred times. After repeating this Surah in the second unit, you may recite <ref=1:59:21-24>last verses of Surah al-Hashr</ref>
You may then recite the first six verses of Surah al-Hadid. While standing up, you may then repeat the following holy verse one thousand times:

translation.en: You do we serve and You do we beseech for help.

text.en: You may then accomplish your prayer and praise Almighty Allah. If your need is not settled, you may repeat this prayer. If it is still not settled, you may repeat it once more and your need will be settled, Allah willing.
5.Another Prayer for Settling the Needs

The trustworthy Shaykh al-Kulayni, may Allah have mercy upon him, reports in his book of al-Kafi that `Abd al-Rahim al-Qasir said: I once visited Imam al-Sadiq (`a) and said, “May Allah accept me as ransom for you! I have invented a formula of supplicatory prayer.”

The Imam (`a) answered, “Save me from your inventions! Whenever a disaster befalls you, you should have recourse to the Messenger of Allah and offer a two-unit prayer and dedicate it to him, peace be upon him and his Household.” “How can I do that?” I asked.

The Imam (`a) answered, “You should bathe yourself and offer a two-unit prayer, beginning it with the prefatory part of obligatory prayers and say the tashahhud statement of obligatory prayers. When you finish saying the tashahhud and taslim statements, you should say the following: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ ٱلسَّلاَمُ

translation.en: allahumma anta alssalamu

text.en: O Allah, You are the (source of) peace;

translation.en: from You does peace come;
and to You does peace return.

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.transliteration: wa balligh ruha muhammadin minni alssalama

translation.en: convey my greetings to the soul of Muhammad
and the souls of the veracious Imams,
and convey to me their response of my greetings.

segments.transliteration: walssalamu `alayhim wa rahmatu allah wa barakatuhu

translation.en: Peace and Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon them.
O Allah, these two units of prayer
are present from me to Allah’s Messenger,
peace and greetings of Allah be upon him and his Household.
S0, (please) decide Your reward to be that You grant me what I have hoped

segments.transliteration: wa rajawtu fika wa fi rasulika

translation.en: and expected from You and Your Messenger.
O Guardian of the believers.

text.en: You may then prostrate and repeat the following forty times:

translation.en: O Ever-living! O Self-Subsisting!
O Ever-living One Who never dies!

segments.arabic: يَا حَيّاً لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ
segments.transliteration: ya hayyan la ilaha illa anta

translation.en: O Ever-living One; there is no god save You!
O Lord of majesty and honoring!
O most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en: You may then put your right cheek on the ground and repeat the aforesaid supplication forty times. You may then put your left cheek on the ground and repeat the supplication more forty times.

You may then raise your head from prostration, extend your hands (towards the sky) and repeat the supplication forty times. You may then pull your hands to your neck, point out with your forefinger, and repeat the supplication forty times. You may then hold your beard with your left hand and weep, or try to weep, and say the following:

translation.en: O Muhammad! O Allah’s Messenger!

segments.transliteration: ashku ila allah wa ilayka hajati

translation.en: I complain to Allah and to you about my need

segments.transliteration: wa ila ahli baytika alrrashidina hajati

translation.en: and I complain to your Household, the rightly guided, about my need,

segments.arabic: وَبِكُمْ أَتَوَجَّهُ إِلَىٰ ٱللَّهِ فِي حَاجَتِي
segments.transliteration: wa bikum atawajjahu ila allahi fi hajati

translation.en: and through you all I turn my face towards Allah to grant me my need.

text.en: You should then prostrate and repeat the following word as much as one breath can take:

translation.en: O Allah! O Allah!

text.en: You may then say the following:

translation.en: Please, send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad, and...

text.en: You may now mention your need. If you do so, Imam al-Sadiq (`a) added, I guarantee that Allah the Almighty and Majestic will settle your need before you leave your place.”
6.In al-Balad al-Amin, al-Kaf`ami states: For urgent needs, the following words may be written on a sheet of paper and then thrown in (current) water:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah; the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
From the submissive servant
to the All-majestic Lord.
O Lord: Harm has afflicted me,
and You are the most Merciful of the merciful.
In the name of Muhammad and his Household,
(please) bless Muhammad and his Household,
take off my distress and relieve me from my grief,
out of Your mercy, O most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en: 7.Another Prayer for Settling the Needs

Sayyid ibn Tawus, in his book of al-Mazar, Chapter: Rites of al-Kufah Mosque, after mentioning the recommended acts at Imam `Ali’s Niche (mihrab), has mentioned a special prayer for settling the needs to be offered there. This prayer consists of four units, each two alone; i.e. the taslim statement is uttered after each two units.

In the first unit, Surah al-Fatihah is recited once and Surah al-Tawhid is repeated ten times. In the second unit, Surah al-Fatihah is recited once and Surah al-Tawhid is repeated twenty-one times. In the third unit, Surah al-Fatihah is recited once and Surah al-Tawhid is repeated thirty-one times. In the fourth unit, Surah al-Fatihah is recited once and Surah al-Tawhid is repeated forty-one times. Upon uttering the taslim statement and saying the glorification statements, you may do the following:
(1) Recite Surah al-Tawhid fifty-one times,
(2) utter the imploration for forgiveness (i.e. istighfar) fifty times:

translation.en: I pray Allah for forgiveness.

text.en: (3) invoke blessings upon the Holy Prophet and his Household (i.e. Salawat) fifty times:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

text.en: (4) repeat the following statement fifty times:

segments.arabic: لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلعَلِيِّ ٱلعَظِيمِ
segments.transliteration: la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah al`aliyyi al`azimi

translation.en: There is neither might nor power except with Allah.

text.en: and (5) say the following supplication:

translation.en: O Allah Who stops His power (to punish) from His creatures,
Who possesses His authority through it,

segments.arabic: وَٱلْمُتَسَلِّطُ بِمَا فِي يَدَيْهِ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ مَوْجُودٍ
segments.transliteration: walmutasallitu bima fi yadayhi `ala kulli mawjudin

translation.en: and Who has full power over all existent beings through what He possesses!
While all others disappoint those who put hope in them,
he who puts hope in You is pleased and never disappointed.
I beseech You in the name of all items that please You,
all things in which You are present,

segments.arabic: وَبِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ تُحِبُّ أَنْ تُذْكَرَ بِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa bikulli shay'in tuhibbu an tudhkara bihi

translation.en: all things in which You like to be mentioned,
and in Your Name, O Allah,
for there is nothing to be equal to You,

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.transliteration: wa tahfazani wa waladi wa ahli wa mali

translation.en: safeguard me, my sons, my wife, and my possessions,
safeguard me with Your safeguarding,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَقْضِيَ حَاجَتِي فِي… كَذا وَكَذا

translation.en: and settle my need, which is...

text.en: 8.Another Prayer for Settling the Needs

It is reported that one who has a request to be granted by Almighty Allah may offer a four-unit prayer, reciting Surah al-Fatihah and <ref=1:6>Surah Al-An'am</ref> in each unit. Upon accomplishment, one may say the following supplication:

segments.arabic: يَا كَرِيمُ يَا كَرِيمُ يَا كَرِيمُ
segments.transliteration: ya karimu ya karimu ya karimu

translation.en: O All-generous! O All-generous! O All-generous!

segments.arabic: يَا عَظِيمُ يَا عَظِيمُ يَا عَظِيمُ

translation.en: O All-great! O All-great! O All-great!
O Greater than all great ones!
O Hearer of prayers!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ لاَ تُغَيِّرُهُ ٱللَّيَالِي وَٱلأَيَّامُ
segments.transliteration: ya man la tughayyiruhu allayali wal-ayyamu

translation.en: O Unaffected by nights and days!
(Please) send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household
and have mercy on my feebleness, neediness,
destitution, and poverty,
because You know them better than I do
and You know my need better than I do.
O He Who had mercy upon Jacob, the old man,

segments.arabic: حِينَ رَدَّ عَلَيْهِ يُوسُفَ قُرَّةَ عَيْنِهِ

translation.en: when He returned Joseph, the delight of his eyes, to him!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ رَحِمَ أَيُّوْبَ بَعْدَ طُولِ بَلاَئِهِ
segments.transliteration: ya man rahima ayyuba ba`da tuli bala'ihi

translation.en: O He Who had mercy upon Job after a long time of tribulation!
O He Who had mercy upon Muhammad,
Allah’s peace and blessings be upon him,
Who gave shelter to him against orphanhood,

segments.transliteration: wa nasarahu `ala jababirati qurayshin wa tawaghitiha

translation.en: Who gave him victory against the tyrants and despots of Quraysh
and Who gave him control over them!

segments.arabic: يَا مُغِيثُ يَا مُغِيثُ يَا مُغِيثُ…
segments.transliteration: ya mughithu ya mughithu ya mughithu…

translation.en: O Bringer of aid! O Bringer of aid! O Bringer of aid!…

text.en: One may repeat the last statement frequently and then ask Almighty Allah for one’s need, for Almighty Allah will grant so.
9.Another Prayer for Settling the Needs

Sayyid Ibn Tawus, may Allah have mercy upon him, has reported the following: At Friday nights and the night before the Eidal-Adha Day (i.e. the tenth of Dhu’l-Hijjah), you may offer a two-unit prayer. In each unit, recite Surah al-Fatihah, but when you reach the following verse, you should repeated it one hundred times:

translation.en: You do we serve and You do we beseech for help.

text.en: You may then recite the next verses of Surah al-Fatihah and then recite Surah al-Tawhid two hundred times. Upon accomplishment of the prayer, you may repeat the following statement seventy times:

segments.arabic: لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلعَلِيِّ ٱلعَظِيمِ
segments.transliteration: la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah al`aliyyi al`azimi

translation.en: There is neither might nor power except with Allah.

text.en: You may then prostrate and repeat the following word of supplication two hundred times:

translation.en: O my Lord!

text.en: Then, you may pray Almighty Allah for whatever you want, and all your requests will be granted, Allah willing.
10.Another Prayer for Settling the Needs

This prayer has been reported by a big number of scholars, such as Shaykh al-Mufid, al-Tusi, Sayyid Ibn Tawus, and many others, from Imam al-Sadiq (`a). However, according to the report of Sayyid Ibn Tawus, the way of offering this prayer is as follows:

When you have an urgent need and you wish Almighty Allah to grant it for you, you may observe fasting on three successive days; Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday. On Friday, you may bathe yourself, dress yourself with a new, clean costume, go up to the highest point in your house, and offer a two-unit prayer. Then, you may raise your hands towards the sky and say the following supplication:

translation.en: O Allah, I have resided in Your court
due to my full acquaintance with Your Oneness, Self-independence, and

segments.arabic: وَأَنَّهُ لاَ قَادِراً عَلَىٰ قَضَاءِ حَاجَتِي غَيْرُكَ
segments.transliteration: wa annahu la qadiran `ala qada'i hajati ghayruka

translation.en: that there is none to settle my need save You.
I have known, O my Lord,
that whenever Your graces are poured down on me in hoards,
my need to You becomes more urgent.
I have been inflicted by the distress of…

text.en: You should now mention your problem and then keep on, saying:

translation.en: About relieving me from this distress, You have full knowledge and You need no direction
and You are Limitless and You do not have to think about it.

segments.arabic: فَأَسْأَلُكَ بِٱسْمِكَ ٱلَّذِي وَضَعْتَهُ عَلَىٰ ٱلْجِبَالِ فَنُسِفَتْ
segments.transliteration: fa'as'aluka bismika alladhi wa da`tahu `ala aljibali fanusifat

translation.en: I therefore beseech You in the name of Your Name that You put on the mountains; so, they were carried away from the roots,
You put on the heavens; so, they were rent asunder,
on the stars; so, they were spread,
and on the earth; so, it was made a vast expanse.

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلْتَهُ عِنْدَ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: wa as'aluka bilhaqqi alladhi ja`altahu `inda muhammadin

translation.en: I also beseech You in the name of the Right that You deposited with Muhammad,
may Allah bless him and his Household,
and with `Ali, al-Hasan, al-Husayn,
`Ali, Muhammad, Ja`far,
Musa, `Ali, Muhammad,
`Ali, al-Hasan, and the Argument,
peace be upon them,

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ahli baytihi

translation.en: [I beseech You] t0 send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household,
settle my need for me,
make easy what is difficult in it,
and save me from the intricate part of it.
If you do, then all praise be to You.

segments.transliteration: wa in lam taf`al falaka alhamdu

translation.en: If you do it not, then all praise be to You,
because You are neither biased in Your judgment,
nor accused in Your decree,
nor prejudicing in Your justice.

text.en: You may then put your face on the ground and say the following supplication:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّ يُونُسَ بْنَ مَتَّىٰ عَبْدَكَ

translation.en: O Allah, Jonah the son of Amittai, Your servant,
prayed to You from the belly of the big fish
and he is Your servant, and You responded to him;
and I am Your servant too; so, (please) respond to me.

text.en: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) said, “Many times, I had many needs to be settled by Almighty Allah. I therefore said this supplication and came back having my need settled for me.”
Etiquettes of Praying for Settling the Needs

In Jamal al-Usbu`, Sayyid Ibn Tawus has mentioned an essay regarding the etiquettes of praying for granting one’s requests, which can be summed up as follows:While trying to beseech the All-knowing Lord to grant you your request, you must be at the most modest manner. When you intend to present yourself before a human king to ask him for granting you a request, you naturally try to attain his pleasure through all possible means and efforts. Likewise, you must exert all possible efforts to attain the pleasure of the Almighty and All-majestic Lord when you want Him to grant you your request. Thus, do not beseech Him in a way less earnest than appealing from human kings; lest, you will fail to win your request because you have been of the scoffers. How is it then reasonable that you care for the pleasure of the All-majestic Lord less than your care for winning the pleasure of human kings who are no more than slaves of the Master of the Worlds? Such being the case, you will be regarded as belittling, scoffing, disdaining, and deriding the grandeur of the All-majestic Lord, and, as a result, you will never win your request no matter how many prayers you would offer and how many days you would fast.

Moreover, when you fast on a day or offer a prayer for purpose of winning your request from Almighty God, you must not behave as if you are testing Him; rather, you must have full confidence of His response. As a rule, we, human beings, try others only when we are suspicious about their ability to do what they are tried to do. About those who suspect Him, Almighty Allah says, “…the entertainers of evil thoughts about Allah. On them is the evil turn. (48/6)”

Significantly, when you beseech the Lord for granting your request, you must have full confidence in His all-encompassing mercy, His all-inclusive magnanimity, and His fulfillment of all of His promises.

When you turn your face towards the most generous of all human beings to ask him for an insignificant thing, you will most certainly be sure that he will grant you that thing after you have asked it from him in the most appropriate way. Similarly, you must be surer that Almighty Allah Who is the All-generous and the most generous of all those who act generously that He will grant you your request, which is, in His sight and as compared to His omnipotence, less insignificant than all insignificant things. Hence, beware that you put confidence in any mortal more than you do with Almighty Allah.

It is also advisable that when you offer a prayer and fast on a day for purpose of winning your request from God, you must arrange your worldly requests according to their importance. In fact, the most important of all your requests, no matter how urgent they be, are the requests of the one under whose shade you are living and whose hospitality you are experiencing; namely, the Patron of the Age, Imam al-Mahdi, may Allah’s blessings be upon him.
Then come your requests with regard to your religious faith. Only then do come your personal requests involved.

To explain, when you intend to beseech Almighty Allah to save you from perdition, you may observe fasting with the intention of praying Him to grant you this very request. In fact, you should have known that winning Almighty Allah’s amnesty, pleasure, acceptance, and approval is more important than being saved from perdition, because you will eventually perish whether you like it or not, but if you miss Almighty Allah’s majesty, pleasure, and acceptance, you will lose this worldly life and the eternal life thus exposing yourself to unbearable and unimaginable misfortunes.

Similarly, as I have advised you to give the requests of the Patron of the Age priority to your personal requests, I only meant to inform you that the survival of this world and the survival of its inhabitants are contingent upon the existence of the Patron in this world. Hence, how is it reasonable to give precedence to your personal requests over the requests of the one without whom you do not live a single moment?

Let it be known to you that the Patron of the Age is in no need for your implorations for him; rather, if you do what I have just advised you to do, you will then have fulfilled your pledge towards him.
Finally, it is highly recommended to commence your supplicatory prayers with invoking Almighty Allah’s blessings upon the Holy Infallibles (`a).

NAHJUL BALAGHA by Imam Ali (as) on SUSTENANCE :Allah (swt) has guaranteed your livelihood and has commanded you to act. Therefore, the pursuit of that which has been guaranteed to you should not get preference over that whose performance has been enjoined upon you. But by Allah, most certainly the position is that doubt has overtaken and certainty has been shattered and it seems as if what has been guaranteed to you is obligatory on you and what was made obligatory on you has been taken away from you..
Baqiyatussalehat- Five in number, the supplicatory prayers for sustenance are as follows:

First: Mu`awiyah ibn `Ammar has reported the following: I once asked Imam al-Sadiq (`a) to teach me a supplicatory prayer for more sustenance. He therefore taught me a supplicatory prayer that I have never seen more effective than it. It is the following:

translation.en: O Allah, (please) provide me out of Your all-inclusive,
legal, and pleasant sustenance
with sustenance that is expansive,
legally gotten, pleasant,
covering all this-worldly and other-worldly demands,
pouring, torrential,
enjoyable, and wholesome,
without exhaustive affairs

segments.arabic: وَلاَ مَنٍّ مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa la mannin min ahadin min khalqika

translation.en: and without the favor of any of Your creatures;
rather, with a favor out of Your all-inclusive favors.

segments.transliteration: fa'innaka qulta was'alu allaha min fadlihi

translation.en: You have said, “Ask Allah of His grace.”
Thus, from Your grace only do I ask,
from Your gift only do I ask,
and from Your abounding donation do I ask.

text.en: Second: Imam al-Baqir (`a) is reported to have said to Zayd al-Shahham: For sustenance, you may say the following supplicatory prayer in the obligatory prayer while you are in the position of prostration:

translation.en: O best of all those whom may be besought
and O best of all those who may give,
(please) provide me and my dependants with sustenance out of Your grace,
for You verily the Lord of great favoring.

text.en: Third: Abu-Basir is reported to have said: I complained to Imam al-Sadiq (`a) about poverty and asked him to teach me a supplicatory prayer for sustenance. He therefore taught me a supplicatory prayer after which I have never needed anything. Precisely, the Imam (`a) instructed me to say the following supplicatory prayer in the Night Prayer while I am in the position of prostration:

translation.en: O best of all those whom may be besought,
O best of all those whom may be begged,
O most ample-giver of all those who may give,
and O best of all those whom may be pleased,
(please) provide me with sustenance, grant me liberal sustenance,

segments.transliteration: wa sabbib li rizqan min qibalika

translation.en: and cause me to receive sustenance from Your Presence,
for You have power over all things.

text.en: Referring to the same supplicatory prayer, Shaykh al-Tusi, in his book of Misbah al-Mutahajjid, has mentioned that this supplicatory prayer may be said in the second prostration of the eighth unit of the Night Prayer.
Fourth: The Holy Prophet (s) is reported to have instructed that this supplicatory prayer might be said for asking for sustenance:

translation.en: O Sustainer of the impoverished,
O Compassionate to the poor,
O Patron of the believers,
and O Lord of power and strong,
(please) send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household,
provide me with sustenance, grant me wellbeing,
and save me from what has distressed me.

text.en: Fifth: Reporting this supplicatory prayer for seeking sustenance from Imam al-Sadiq (`a), Abu-Basir added that Imam al-Sadiq (`a) ascribed this prayer to Imam `Ali ibn al-Husayn (`a):

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You for excellent livelihood;

segments.arabic: مَعِيشَةً أَتَقَوَّىٰ بِهَا عَلَىٰ جَمِيعِ حَوَائِجِي

translation.en: such livelihood by which I can be competent enough to settle all of my needs

segments.arabic: وَأَتَوَصَّلُ بِهَا فِي ٱلْحَيَاةِ إِلَىٰ آخِرَتِي
segments.transliteration: wa atawassalu biha fi alhayati ila akhirati

translation.en: and by which I can pave my way to my otherworldly life by means of this life,

segments.arabic: مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ تُتْرِفَنِي فِيهَا فَأَطْغَىٰ
segments.transliteration: min ghayri an tutrifani fiha fa'atgha

translation.en: but do not make me so luxurious that I may be inordinate
and do not straiten my means of life that I may be miserable.
Rather, You may grant me expansive and legally gotten sustenance
and endue me kindly with the torrential favors of You,
such pleasant graces,
and enduring gifts.

segments.arabic: ثُمَّ لاَ تَشْغَلْنِي عَنْ شُكْرِ نِعْمَتِكَ

translation.en: Then, (please) do not cause me to be distracted from thanking Your graces
through granting me excessive sustenance whose joy may divert me
and whose vainglory may lead me astray
or through giving me very little amount of sustenance
due to which my painstaking efforts fail to meet my needs
and due to which my heart is filled with grief.
(Please) give me, O my God, an amount
that makes me dispense with Your evil creatures
and makes me attain Your pleasure.

segments.arabic: وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ يَا إِلٰهِي مِنْ شَرِّ ٱلدُّنْيَا
segments.transliteration: wa a`udhu bika ya ilahi min sharri alddunya

translation.en: I pray Your protection, O my God, against the evil of this world
and the evil therein.
Do not cause this world to be like prison for me
and do not cause my departure from it to be grief for me.
(Please) take me out of it while You are pleased with me
and my deeds are accepted by You

segments.transliteration: ila dari alhayawani wa masakini al-akhyari

translation.en: to the habitat of true life and the abode of the virtuous ones.
Give me in exchange for this terminating life
the bliss of the everlasting abode.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ أَزْلِهَا وَزِلْزَالِهَا
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni a`udhu bika min azliha wa zilzaliha

translation.en: O Allah, I pray for Your protection against this world’s hardships and tribulations

segments.transliteration: wa satawati shayatiniha wa salatiniha wa nakaliha

translation.en: and against the sways of its devils, rulers, and chastisement

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ بَغْيِ مَنْ بَغَىٰ عَلَيَّ فِيهَا
segments.transliteration: wa min baghyi man bagha `alayya fiha

translation.en: as well as against the oppression of whoever has oppressed me in this world.
O Allah, (please) frustrate whoever intends to deceive me,
thwart whoever intends evil to me,

segments.arabic: وَفُلَّ عَنِّي حَدَّ مَنْ نَصَبَ لِي حَدَّهُ
segments.transliteration: wa fulla `anni hadda man nasaba li haddahu

translation.en: break off the sharp edge of whoever has targeted at me,

segments.arabic: وَأَطْفِئْ عَنِّي نَارَ مَنْ شَبَّ لِي وَقُودَهُ
segments.transliteration: wa atfi' `anni nara man shabba li waqudahu

translation.en: extinguish the fire of whoever has collected its wood against me,
save me from the trickeries of the cunning ones,
gouge out the eyes of the disbelievers,

segments.arabic: وَٱكْفِنِي هَمَّ مَنْ أَدْخَلَ عَلَيَّ هَمَّهُ

translation.en: save me from the grief of whoever has grieved me,
defend me against the evil of the envious ones,
protect me against all that through tranquility,
dress me Your invulnerable armor,
cause me to live under the shade of Your protective covering,
set aright all of my affairs,
make congruous my words and my deeds,
and bless my family members for me.

text.en: Prayer for More Sustenance -Baqeyatus salehat

It is reported that a man came to the Holy Prophet (s) and said, “I have many dependants and I am suffering too much because of debts. Therefore, instruct me a supplication by which Almighty Allah will provide me sustenance so that I can settle my debts and cover the needs of my dependants.” The Holy Prophet (s) taught him to do the following: You may do the ritual ablution very accurately and then offer a two unit prayer with accurate genuflection and prostration. Upon accomplishment, you may say the following supplication:

segments.arabic: يَا مَاجِدُ يَا وَاحِدُ يَا كَرِيمُ
segments.transliteration: ya majidu ya wahidu ya karimu

translation.en: O Glorious! O One an Only! O All-generous!

segments.arabic: أَتَوَجَّهُ إِلَيْكَ بِمُحَمَّدٍ نَبِيِّكَ نَبِيِّ ٱلرَّحْمَةِ
segments.transliteration: atawajjahu ilayka bimuhammadin nabiyyika nabiyyi alrrahmati

translation.en: I turn my face towards You in the name of Muhammad, Your Prophet, the Prophet of Mercy,
Allah’s blessings be upon him and his Household;
O Muhammad! O Allah’s Messenger!
I turn my face in your name towards Allah,

segments.transliteration: rabbi wa rabbika wa rabbi kulli shay'in

translation.en: my and your Lord and the Lord of all things.

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُكَ ٱللَّهُمَّ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa as'aluka allahumma an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ahli baytihi

translation.en: I thus beseech You, O Allah, to send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household,

segments.transliteration: wa as'aluka nafhatan karimatan min nafahatika

translation.en: and I beseech You to grant me one of Your generous gifts,
an easy opening,
and expansive sustenance
by which I can put together my scattered things,
settle my debts,
and have the ability to cover the needs of my dependants.

text.en: Another Prayer for More Sustenance -Baqeyatus salehat
Before you go to your shop (or place of work), you may go to the mosque to offer two or four units of prayer after which you may say the following supplication:

translation.en: I begin this day by Allah’s might and power.

segments.arabic: وَغَدَوْتُ بِلاَ حَوْلٍ مِنِّي وَلاَ قُوَّةٍ
segments.transliteration: wa ghadawtu bila hawlin minni wa la quwwatin

translation.en: I begin it by no might or power of mine;

segments.transliteration: wa lakin bihawlika wa quwwatika ya rabbi

translation.en: rather, by Your might and power, O my Lord.
O Allah, I am verily your servant.
I am seeking Your favor, as You have ordered me to do.

segments.arabic: فَيَسِّرْ لِي ذٰلِكَ وَأَنَا خَافِضٌ فِي عَافِيَتِكَ
segments.transliteration: fayassir li dhalika wa ana khafidun fi `afiyatika

translation.en: So, (please) make it easy for me while I am enjoying wellbeing that You confer upon me.

text.en: Another Salat Prayer for More Sustenance -Baqeyatus salehat

You may offer a two unit prayer; in the first unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah once and repeat Surah al-Kawthar (No. 108) three times. In the second unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah once and repeat Surah al-Falaq and Surah al-Nas each three times.
Tasbih of Fatima Zehra (sa)

Once the Prophet's (saw) daughter Fatima Zehra (as) asked the prophet for a helper. The Prophet(saw) advised her to recite the following Tasbih which is now known as Tasbih e Zehra. This Tasbih is usually recited after each prayer. However it also has great value for solving the problems of Rizq, Sustenance, Business, Job etc. The person should recite the tasbih with the niyat (intention) of seeking rizq (sustenance), before leaving the house ,before commencing business or before starting work at office. 34 times Allah o Akbar,33 times Alhumdolillah & 33 times Subhanallah Inshallah rizq problems can be overcome with this. For an indepth look at this particular 'tasbih', have a view of this particular compilation http://www.scribd.com/doc/2296632/The-Tasbih-of-Fatima-Zahra
Tawasul of lady Khadija
Also the Tawasul of lady Khadija can be used to overcome Rizq problems as her wealth made the Prophet (saws) wealthy
The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said:
“Adopt the fear of Allah and make your inner self sincere and read this Dua:
Dua from Lady Fatima (sa )
Recite ...The 5 sentences to be regularly

translation.en: Ya Rabbal Awwaleen wal Aakhereen
Ya Khairal Awwaleen wal Aakhereen
Ya Zal Quwwatil Mateen
Ya Rahemal Masaakeen
Ya Arhamar Raahemeen

text.en: They were gifted to lady Sayyeda fatima( sa) as by the Holy Prophet saws when his daughter had mentioned to him that the kitchen fires in their home had not been lit since a month (signifying gurbah)
Rizq Dua by Imam Mahdi (atfs)

This dua is taken from a book called Sahifa of the Mahdi and its a tawassul through the 9th Imam. Shaykh 'Abbas Qummi also in his biography of the 9th Imam (muntahal aamaal) mentions something on these lines that asking Allah (swt) by the 9th Imam for increase in sustenance is a tested and proved method that really works.

segments.arabic: وَ أَسْأَلُكَ اَللّٰهُمَّ بِحَقِّ وَلِيِّكَ أَبِيْ جَعْفَرٍ الْجَوَادِ ؑ إِلَّا جُدْتَ عَلَيَّ بِهٖ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ وَ تَفَضَّلْتَ عَلَيَّ بِهٖ مِنْ وُسْعِكَ مَا أَسْتَغْنِي بِهٖ عَمَّا فِي أَيْدِيْ خَلْقِكَ وَ خَاصَّةً يَا رَبِّ لِئَامَهُمْ وَ بَارِكْ لِيْ فِيْهِ وَ فِيْمَا لَكَ عِنْدِيْ مِنْ نِعَمِكَ وَ فَضْلِكَ وَ رِزْقِكَ إِلٰهِي انْقَطَعَ الرَّجَاءُ إِلَّا مِنْكَ وَ خَابَتِ الْآمَالُ إِلَّا فِيْكَ يَا ذَا الْجَلَالِ وَ الْإِكْرَامِ أَسْأَلُكَ بِحَقِّ مَنْ حَقُّهٗ عَلَيْكَ وَاجِبٌ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلٰى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ أَهْلِ بَيْتِهٖ وَ أَنْ تَبْسُطَ عَلَيَّ مَا حَظَرْتَهٗ مِنْ رِزْقِكَ وَ أَنْ تُسَهِّلَ ذٰلِكَ وَ تُيَسِّرَهٗ فِي خَيْرٍ مِنْكَ وَ عَافِيَةٍ وَ أَنَا فِي‏خَفْضِ عَيْشٍ وَ دَعَةٍ يَا أَرْحَمَ الرَّاحِمِيْنَ.
segments.transliteration: wa as'alukallahumma bi haqqi waliyyika abi ja'farinil jawaad, muhammadibni 'aliyyin 'alayhis salaam, illa judta 'alayya bihi min fadhlik, wa tafadh-dhalta 'alayya bihi min wus'ik, maa astaghni bihi 'ammaa fi aydi khalqik, wa khaassatan ya rabbi li'aamihim wa baarik lee feehi wa fee ma laka 'indee min ni'amika wa fadhlika wa rizqik. ilaahi anqata'ar rajaa'u illa mink, wa khaabatil aamaalu illa feek, ya dhal jalaali wal ikraam, as'aluka bihaqqi man haqquhu 'alayka waajibun an tusalliya 'ala muhammadin wa ahli baytihi wa an tabsuta 'alayya maa khatartahu min rizqik wa an tusahhila dhaalika wa tuyassirahu fee khayrin minka wa 'aafiyatin wa ana fee khaffadhi 'aiyshin wa da'a ya arhamar rahimeen

translation.en: Rizq Duas From the Book Tohfatul Hashmiya:

text.en: From Amirul Momineen Ali bin Abi Talib, Allah’s blessings be upon him said: One who is having problems regarding his livelihood and the ways of earning are closed on him, he should write the following on deer skin or a piece of leather and hang it or put it in some clothes that he wears. Then he will not take it off but that Allah will widen his sustenance and open for him doors of earnings from where he has least expected and it is as follows:

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ لاَ طَاقَةَ لِفُلاَنِ بْنِ فُلاَنٍ بِالْجُهدِ وَ لاَ صَبْرَ لَهُ عَلَی الْبَلاَءِ وَ لاَ قُوَّةَ لَهُ عَلَی الْفَقْرِ وَ الفَاقَةِ اَللَّهُمَّ فَصَلِّ عَلٰی مُحَمَّدٍ وَّ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَّ لاَ تَحْظُرْ عَلٰی فُلاَنِ بْنِ فُلاَنٍ رِزْقَكَ وَ لاَ تُقَتِّرَ عَلَيْهِ سِعَةَ مَا عِنْدَكَ وَ لاَ تَحْرِمْهُ فَضْلَكَ وَ لاَ تَحْسِمْهُ مِنْ جَزِيْلِ قِسَمِكَ وَ لاَ تَكِلْهُ إِلٰى خَلقِكَ وَ لاَ إِلٰی نَفْسِهِ…
segments.transliteration: Allaahumma laa t’aaqata lifulaanIbnI fulaaniM bil juhdi wa laa s’abra lahu a’lal balaa-i wa laa quwwata lahu a’lal faqri wal faaqati allaahumma fas’alli a’laa muh’ammadinw wa aali muh’ammad. Wa laa tah’z’ur a’laa fulaanibnI fulaaniR rizqAka wa laa tuqattira a’laihi sia’ta maA i’ndaka wa laa tah’rimhu faz”laka wa laa tah’simhu min jazeeli qIsamika wa laatAkilhu ilaa khalqika wa laa ilaa nafsihi…

translation.en: O Allah so and so, son of so and so, has no strength to struggle and no patience to bear the hardships and no power for him in poverty and hunger. O Allah then bless Muhammad and the progeny of Muhammad and do not hinder upon so and so, son of so and so, Your sustenance and do not decrease for him the widening of sustenance that is with You and do not deprive him of Your Grace and do not prevent from his share of sustenance and do not entrust him to Your creatures and neither to his ownself…

segments.arabic: فَيَعْجَزَ عَنْهَا وَ يَضْعُفُ عَنِ الْقِيَامِ فِيْمَا يُصْلِحُهُ وَ يُصْلِحُ مَا قَبْلَهُ بَلْ تَنْفَرِدُ بِلَمِّ شَعْثِهِ وَ تَوَلِّىْ كِفَايَتِهِ وَ انْظُرْ إِلَيْهِ فِىْ جَمِيْعِ اُمُوْرِهِ إِنَّكَ إنْ وَكَلْتَهُ إِلٰی خَلْقِكَ لَمْ يَنْفَعُوْهُ وَ إنْ اَلْجاَتَهُ إِلٰی اَقْرِبَائِهِ حَرَمُوْهُ وَ إنْ اَعْطُوْهُ اَعْطُوْهُ قَلِيْلاً نَكِدًا وَ إِنْ مَنَعُوْهُ مَنَعُوْهُ كَثِيْراً وَ إِنْ بَخِلُوْا بَخِلُوْا فَهُمْ لِلْبُخْلِ اَهْلِ، اَللَّهُمَّ اَغْنِ فُلاَنَ بْنَ فُلاَنٍ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ وَ لاَ تُخْلِهِ مِنْهُ فَإِنَّهُ مُضْطَرٌّ إِلَيْكَ فَقِيْرٌ إِلٰی مَا فِىْ يَدَيْكَ وَ اَنْتَ غَنِيٌّ عَنْهُ وَ اَنْتَ بِهِ خَبِيْرٌ عَلِيْمٌ…
segments.transliteration: fa ya’-jaza a’nhaa wa yaz”-u’fu a’nil qiyaami feemaa yus’lih’uhu wa yus’lih’u maa qAblahu bal tanfaridu bIlamMi shA’thihi wa tuwallee kifaayatihi wanz’ur ilaihi fee jameei’ umoorihi innaka in wakaltahu ilaa khalqika lam yanfao’o’hu wa in aljaa-tahu ilaa aqribaa-ihi h’aramoohu wa in aa’t’oohu aa’t’oohu qaleelan nakIdanw wa in manao’o’hu manao’o’hu katheeranw wa im bakhilu bakhilu fahum lil bukhli ahlI. Allaahumma aghni fulaanabna fulaanim min faz”lika wa laa tukhlihi minhu fa-innahu muz”t’arrun ilaika faqeerun ilaa maa fee yadaika wa anta ghaneeyun a’nhu wa anta bihi khabeeruM a’leemu…

translation.en: So that he becomes helpless and is weak to rise up in that in which lies his betterment and what was before him. But single him out for straightening his muddled affairs and take the responsibility of the fulfillment of his needs and keep an eye on all his affairs. Indeed You, if you entrust him to Your creatures he will not benefit and if you leave him upon his relatives he would be deprived. And if they give him they would give little and less. And if they prevent from him they will restrain much. And if they are miserly they would be miserly, for they are the miserly ones. O Allah, make so and so, son of so and so, needless by Your Grace and do not keep him bereft of it. For he is distressed towards You, needful of what is with You and You are needless of him. And You are informed and knowing about him.

segments.arabic: وَ مَنْ يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَی اللهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ إِنَّ اللهَ بَالِغُ اَمْرِهِ قَدْ جَعَلَ اللهُ لِكُلِّ شَیْءٍ قَدْرًا إِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا وَ مَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا وَ يَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لاَ يَحْتَسِبُ.
segments.transliteration: wa maYn yatawakkal a’lallaahi fahuwa h’asbuhu innallaaha baalighu amrihi qad ja-a’-lallaahU likulli shai-in qadran inna ma-a’l u’sri yusra. wa maYn yattaqillaahA yaj-a’l lahu makhrajanw wa yarzuqhu min h’aithu laa yah’tasib.

translation.en: And whoever trusts in Allah, He is sufficient for him, surely Allah attains His purpose, Allah indeed has appointed a measure for everything. Surely with difficulty is ease. And whoever is careful of (his duty to) Allah, He will make for him an outlet, and give him sustenance from whence he thinks not.[1] From Ali (a.s.): One who begins his day and does not utters these words, it is feared that his sustenance would be destroyed.

segments.arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ ِللهِ الَّذِىْ عَرَّفَنِيْ نَفْسَهُ، يَتْرُكْنِيْ عَمِىَّ الْقَلْبِ، اَلْحَمْدُ ِللهِ الَّذِىْ جَعَلَنِيْ مِنْ اُمَّةِ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّی اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ، اَلْحَمْدُ ِللهِ الَّذِىْ جَعَلَ رِزْقِىْ فِىْ يَدِهِ، وَ لَمْ يَجْعَلْهُ فِىْ اَيْدِىْ النَّاسِ، اَلْحَمْدُ ِللهِ الَّذِىْ سَتَرَ عَوْرَتِىْ وَ لَمْ يَفْضَحْنِيْ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ.
segments.transliteration: Alh’amdu lillaahilladhee a’rrafanee nafsahu, wa lam yataruknee a’meeyal qalb. Alh’amdu lillaahilladhee ja-a’lanee min ummati muh’ammadin s’allallaahu a’laihi wa aalih. Alh’amdu lillaahilladhee ja-a’la rizqee fee yadih. Wa lam yaj-a’lhu fee aidinnaas. Alh’amdu lillaahilladhee satara a’uratee wa lam yafz”ah’nee bainan naas.

translation.en: Praise be to Allah, the One Who has introduced me His self, and did not leave me blind by the heart. Praise be to Allah Who made me from the nation of Muhammad, may Allah bless him and his family. Praise be to Allah Who kept my sustenance in His hands and did not put it in the control of people. Praise be to Allah Who concealed my defects and did not expose me among the people. Also recite the following for increase of sustenance:

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ ارْزُقْنِيْ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ الْوَاسِعِ، الْحَلاَلِ الطَّيِّبِ، رِزْقًا حَلاَلاً طَيِّبًا، بَلاَغًا لِلدُّنْيَا وَ اْلآخِرَةِ، صَبًّا صَبًّا هَنِيْئًا مَرِيْئًا، مِنْ غَيْرِ كَدٍّ وَ لاَ مَنٍّ مِنْ اَحَدٍ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ، إِلاَّ سَعَةً مِنْ فَضْلِكَ الْوَاسِعِ، فَإِنَّكَ قُلْتَ. وَ اسْاَلُوْا اللهَ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ. فَمِنْ فَضْلِكَ اَسْاَلَ، وَ مِنْ عَطَيْتِكَ اَسْاَلَ، وَ مِنْ يَدِكَ الْمَلاَیٰ اَسْاَلَ.
segments.transliteration: Allaahummar zuqnee min faz”likal wasii’l h’alaalit’ t’iyyibi rizqan h’alaalan t’ayyibaM balaaghaL LIDdunyaa wal aakhirah. S’abban s’abban hanee-am maree-aa. Min ghairi kadinW wa laa mannim min ah’adim min khalqika illaa sAa’tam min faz”likal waasii’ fa-innaka qulta was alullaaha min faz”lihi famin faz”lika asa-ala wa min a’t’iatika asa-ala wa miyn yakal malaayaa as-al.

translation.en: O Allah, give me from Your wide Grace, and the permissible and the pure, a sustenance that is permissible and pure, sufficient for the world and the Hereafter, in continuous outpour, pleasing to eat and drink without undue struggle and without being under an obligation of one of Your creatures. Except that You widen it by Your wide favor; for indeed You have said: “And ask Allah through His Grace”. Then from Your Grace I ask. And I ask you through Your bestowal and I ask You from Your hands that are full of bounties.

text.en: Duas increase in sustenance and sufficiency in important matters- Verses
Among the formulae for increase in sustenance and sufficing for important affairs is the reading of the following verses:

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ. وَ مَن يَتَّقِ اللهَ يَجْعَلْ لَّهُ مَخْرَجًا. وَ يَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لاَ يَحْتَسِبُ وَ مَن يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ إِنَّ اللهَ بَالِغُ أَمْرِهِ قَدْ جَعَلَ اللهُ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدْرًا.
Recite Salawat on the Prophet (s.a.w.s.) three times before and after reciting the above verses. Perform this ritual every Wednesday.
Verse of sustenanceFrom the proven supplications of Shaykh Mufid for seeking sustenance is reciting seven times:
اَللهُ لَطِيْفٌ بِعِبَادِهِ يَرْزُقُ مَنْ يَشَآءُ وَ هُوَ الْقَوِيُّ الْعَزِيْزُ.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ اَدِمْ نِعْمَتَكَ وَ الْطُفْ بِنَا فِيْمَا قَدَّرْتَهُ.
segments.transliteration: Allaahumma adim ni’mataka walt’uf binaa feemaa qaddartah. O Allah make permanent Your bounty and Your Grace for us in what You have ordained.

translation.en: For sustenance and repayment of debts

text.en: Maaz complained to the Prophet (s.a.w.s.) when he was under a debt. He (the Prophet) said: Do you want that Allah should liquidate your debts? He said: Yes, O Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.). He (the Prophet) said:

segments.arabic: قُلِ اللَّهُمَّ مَالِكَ الْمُلْكِ تُؤْتِيْ الْمُلْكَ مَنْ تَشَآءُ وَتَنْزِعُ الْمُلْكَ مِمَّن تَشَآءُ وَ تُعِزُّ مَن تَشَآءُ وَ تُذِلُّ مَنْ تَشَآءُ بِيَدِكَ الْخَيْرُ إِنَّكَ عَلٰى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ. تُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِيْ الْنَّهَارِ وَ تُوْلِجُ النَّهَارَ فِيْ اللَّيْلِ وَ تُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَ تُخْرِجُ الْمَيَّتَ مِنَ الْحَيِّ وَ تَرْزُقُ مَنْ تَشَآءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ. رَحْمٰنَ الدُّنْيَا وَ اْلآخِرَةِ وَ رَحِيْمَهُمَا تُعْطِىْ مِنْهُمَا مَا تَشَآءُ وَ تَمْنَعُ مِنْهُمَا مَا تَشَآءُ إِقْضِی عَنِّیْ دَيْنِيْ.
segments.transliteration: Qulillaahumma maalikal mulki tu-til mulka man tashaa-u wa tanziu’l mulka mimman tashaa-u wa tu-i’zzu man tashaa-u wa tudhillu man tashaa-u biyadikal khairu innaka a’laa kulli shai-in qadeer. Toolijul Laila finnahaari wa toolijun nahaara fil laili wa tukhrijul h’ayya minal mayyiti wa tukhrijul mayyita minal h’ayy. Wa tarzuqu man tashaa-u bighairi h’isaab. Rah’maanad dunyaa wal aakhirati wa rah’eemahumaa tu’t’ee minhumaa maa tashaa-u wa tamnau’ minhumaa maa tashaa-u iqz”ee a’nnee dainee.

translation.en: Say: O Allah, Master of the Kingdom! Thou givest the kingdom to whomsoever Thou pleasest and takest away the kingdom from whomsoever Thou pleasest, and Thou exaltest whom Thou pleasest and abasest whom Thou pleasest; in Thine hand is the good; surely, Thou hast power over all things. Thou makest the night to pass into the day and Thou makest the day to pass into the night, and Thou bringest forth the living from the dead and Thou bringest forth the dead from the living, and Thou givest sustenance to whom Thou pleasest without measure. The Beneficent of the world and the hereafter and their Merciful, You bestow from the two what You like and prevent from the two what You like, repay for me my debt. Then even if your debts equal the whole world full of gold, Allah would cause it to be liquidated.
For repayment of debt and removal of poverty

text.en: From Sahl Sadi that he said: A person came to the Prophet (s.a.w.s.) and complained of poverty and straitening of sustenance. The Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) told him: When you enter your house, say Salam (salute) whether there is someone therein or there is none. The recite “Qul huwallaaho Ahad (Surah Tawheed)”, once. The man did as advised and Allah gave him so much that he even passed some of it to his neighbors.[8]

From ar-Reza (a.s.) from his forefathers from Ali (a.s.) that he said: The Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) said: One who recites the following a hundred times everyday will acquire wealth by it, and poverty will be removed and the door of poverty will be closed while the door of Paradise shall be opened for him:
There is no god except Allah, the True and Manifest Ruler.
It is said in traditions to recite the above thirty times for removing poverty and getting prosperity.[9]
For seeking sustenance

Allamah Amili (r.a.) says: We have found in some books of supplication that one who writes these verses on the last Friday of the month of Ramadan and keeps it in his purse or his money box, Allah the High, will cause increase and the two will not be empty of it till he keeps it.
For sustenance Write:

segments.arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ فَتَقَبَّلَهَا رَبُّهَا بِقَبُوْلٍ حَسَنٍ وَ أَنْبَتَهَا نَبَاتًا حَسَنًا وَ كَفَّلَهَا زَكَرِيَّا كُلَّمَا دَخَلَ عَلَيْهَا زَكَرِيَّا الْمِحْرَابَ وَجَدَ عِندَهَا رِزْقاً قَالَ يَا مَرْيَمُ أَنّٰى لَكِ هٰذَا قَالَتْ هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ اللهِ إِنَّ اللهَ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَآءَ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ صَدَقَ اللهُ الْعَظِيْمُ.
segments.transliteration: Bismillaahir rah’maanir rah’eem. Fataqbbalahaa rabbuhaa biqaboolin h’asaninw wa ambatahaa nabaatan h’asananw wa kaffalahaa zakareeyaa. Kullamaa dakhala a’laihaa zakareeyal mih’raaba wajada i’ndahaa rizqaa. Qaala yaa maryamu annaa laki haadhaa qaalat huwa min i’ndillaahi innallaaha yarzuqu mayn yashaa-u bighairi h’isaab. s’adaq allaahul a’z’eem.

translation.en: In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful. So her Lord accepted her with a good acceptance and made her grow up a good growing, and gave her into the charge of Zakaria; whenever Zakaria entered the sanctuary to (see) her, he found with her food. He said: O Maryam! whence comes this to you? She said: It is from Allah. Surely Allah gives to whom He pleases without measure.[10] Allah the Mighty, has said the truth.

text.en: Another supplication for sustenance
In the History of Ali bin Anjab, known as Ibne Sayyi, he says: One who follows this supplication, his sustenance would become easy and their ways would become easy for him.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ يَا سَبَبَ مَنْ لاَ سَبَبَ لَهُ يَا سَبَبَ كُلِّ ذِیْ سَبَبٍ يَا مُسَبِّبُ اْلاَسْبَابِ بِغَيْرِ سَبَبٍ صَلِّ عَلٰی مُحَمَّدٍ وَّ اٰلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَّ اَغْنِنِیْ بِحَلاَلِكَ عَنْ حَرَامِكَ وَ بِطَاعَتِكَ عَنْ مَعْصِيَتِكَ وَ بِفَضْلِكَ عَمَّنْ سِوَاك يَا حَیُّ يَا قَيُّوْمُ.
segments.transliteration: Allaahumma yaa sababa mallaa sababa lah. Yaa sababa kulli dhee sabab. Yaa musabbilal asbaabi fee ghairi sabab. S’alli a’alaa muh’ammadinw wa aali muh’ammadinw wa-aghninee bih’alaalika an h’araamika wa bit’aa-a’tika a’m ma’s’iyatika wa bifaz”lika a’mman siwaaka yaa h’ayyu yaa qayyoom.

translation.en: O Allah, O the cause of one for whom there is no cause, O cause of all that have cause. O cause of causes in that where there is no cause. Bless Muhammad and the progeny of Muhammad. Make me needless through Your permissible from Your prohibited. And through Your obedience from Your disobedience and by Your Grace from others than You. O the Living, O the Everlasting.[11] Another Supplication

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَسْاَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ مَا يَلِيْقُ بِفَضْلِكَ كَمَا يَلِيْقُ بِفَضْلِكَ، وَ زِيَادَةً مِنْ فَضْلِكَ بِفَضْلِكَ، يَا ذَا الْفَضْلِ، اُرْزُقْنِيْ رِزْقًا وَاسِعًا يَا كَرِيْمُ.
segments.transliteration: Allaahumma innaa nas-aluka min faz”lika maa yaleequ bifaz”lika kamaa yaleequ bifaz”lik. Wa ziyaadatam min faz”lika bifaz”lik yaa dhal faz”li urzuqnee rizqanw wasia’yn yaa kareem.

translation.en: O Allah, we ask You from Your Grace that which befits Your Grace, like it befits Your Grace. And more than Your Grace by Your Grace. O one with Grace, give me a wide sustenance, O Munificent.[12]

text.en: [1] Muhajjut Dawat, Ibne Tawus, Pg. 160
[2] As-Sahifa as-Sajjadiya al-Kamilah, Al-Imam Zainul Abideen (a.s.) Pg. 136, Manshuratil A’lami
[3] As-Sahifa Alawiya, Pg.152, India
[4] Makarim al-Akhlaq, Ash-Shaykh Tabarsi, Pg. 337
[5] Surah Talaq 65:2-3
[6] Surah Shura 42:19
[7] Mujarribat al-Imamiya, Pg. 159
[8] Tibb al-Aaimma, As-Sayyid Shubbar, Pg. 524
[9] Tibb al-Aaimma, As-Sayyid Shubbar, Pg. 524
[10] Surah Aale Imran 3:37
[11] Al-Misbah, Vol. 1, Pg. 337
[12] Al-Mukhla, al-Bahai, Pg. 186
Extract From Manifestations book Ensuring Sustenance

segments.arabic: وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ الْقُرَى آمَنُوا وَاتَّقَوْا لَفَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ بَرَكَاتٍ مِنَ السَّمَآءِ وَالأَرْضِ...

translation.en: 1. Chapter al-Ā‘rāf - 7:96: ) (“And if the people of the towns were to believe and observe piety, surely we would have opened to them the blessings of the heaven and the earth…”

segments.arabic: وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللٌّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا. وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لاَ يَحْتَسِبُ

translation.en: 2. Chapter al-Talāq - 65:2-3: ) (“And whosoever is God-wary (observes piety) He would make for him a way out [from difficulties] and Bestow on him sustenance from whence he expects not…” 3. Chapter Nūh - 71:10-12:)

segments.arabic: فَقُلْتُ اسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ إِنَّهُ كَانَ غَفَّارًا. يُرْسِلِ السَّمَآءَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِدْرَارًا. وَيُمْدِدْكُمْ بِأَمْوَالٍ وَبَنِينَ وَيَجْعَلْ لَكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ وَيَجْعَلْ لَكُمْ أَنْهَارًا

translation.en: ( “Then I said: seek forgiveness from your Lord, [for] indeed He is the Most-forgiving; He would send down abundance of rain upon you; and aid you with wealth and sons; and make for you gardens and make for you rivers.”

text.en: These verses inform the human being about the best path of ensuring sustenance and avoiding the calamity of hunger. If the culture of taqwā and istighfār (in its various manifestations and dimensions] were to prevail, hunger would no more be a predicament save in cases of Divine trial.) (“And We would certainly try You with somewhat of fear and hunger…”[27] We also have many traditions that guide the hungry and the destitute, as well those who would like to disseminate the culture of independence, about the ways of achieving sustenance. Following are some examples:
1. Imām al-Sādiq (as) is reported to have said[28]:إِنَّ الْبِرَّ يَزِيدُ فِي الرِّزْقِ. “Indeed, virtuousness increases sustenance.”
2. Imām al-Sādiq (as) is reported to have said[29]:حُسْنُ الْخُلْقِ يَزِيدُ فِي الرِّزْقِ.“Good behaviour (akhlāq) increases sustenance.”
3. The Holy Prophet (s) is reported to have said[30]:دُمْ عَلى الطَّهَارَةِ، يُوَسَّعُ عَلَيْكَ فِي الرِّزْقِ. “Always be in the state of tahāra (purity) and your sustenance shall be increased.”
Therefore, the duty of those who sincerely utter this verse of the supplication, besides actively supporting others, should be to educate them to realize the direct causes of hunger.
Salat of Isteghfar

translation.en: The Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) said:

If your sustenance reduces and you have difficulties in your affairs you must remove them by asking your needs from the Almighty with the help of Namaz and Isteghfar. Namaz-e-Isteghfar is two rakats. In each rakat after Surah Hamd recite Surah Qadr once and then say Astaghfirullaah 15 times. Then go into the ruku and after the recitation of the ruku say Astaghfirullaah 10 times. Rise up from the ruku and again say it 10 times. Go into the first Sajdah and after the recitation of the Sajdah say Astaghfirullaah 10 times. Arise from the first Sajdah and again say Astaghfirullaah 10 times. Go into the second Sajdah and repeat like the first Sajdah. Get up from the second Sajdah and say Astaghfirullaah 10 times. In the similar manner complete the second rakat and conclude the prayer. Insha Allah everything would be all right.

text.en: Ailments duas- Mafatih Baqiyatus Salehat
Sayyid Ibn Tawus (may Allah have mercy upon him) in his book of Muhaj al-Da`awat has reported Sa`id ibn Abi’l-Fath of Wasit as saying:

I was once inflicted by a serious disease that overtired all physicians. Therefore, my father took me to the hospital where all physicians, headed by the Christian doctor of medicine, tried to find me treatment, but failed. Finally, they had to confess, “This disease cannot be healed except by God the All-exalted.” With breaking heart and distress, I had to go back home. Browsing one of my father’s books, I found the following inscribed on the back of the book:

Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have said: Any diseased person who repeats these statements forty times immediately after the Dawn Prayer and then rubs the painful part with his hand, Almighty Allah will free him from the disease and restore him to health:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
All praise be to Allah, the Lord of the Worlds.
Sufficient to us is Allah, and most excellent is the Protector.
Blessed be Allah, the Best of all creators.

segments.arabic: وَلاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاََّ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَلِيِّ ٱلْعَظِيمِ
segments.transliteration: wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billahi al`aliyyi al`azimi

translation.en: There is neither might nor power save with Allah, the Most High and All-great.

text.en: I, the patient, waited for dawn, performed the obligatory prayer, and sat in my place repeating these words while I was fearful lest the pains would return to me. Keeping on this state for three days, the disease was gone. My father, after I had told him about this, thanked Almighty Allah. When he informed one of the physicians about the restoration of my health, the physician, who was non-Muslim, visited and examined me. When he was sure that my disease had gone, he converted to Islam, bore witness that Muhammad is the messenger of God, and committed himself to the teachings of Islam.
Second:Al-Kaf`ami

Al-Kaf`ami, in his book entitled al-Misbah, has mentioned that whoever is diseased, may rub over the place of his prostration (in prayers) with his hand and then rub the ailed organ after each obligatory prayer seven times. He may then say the following supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ كَبَسَ ٱلأَرْضَ عَلَىٰ ٱلْمَاءِ

translation.en: O He Who has pressed the earth on water,
blocked the air with the sky,
and chose for Himself the most excellent of all names,
(please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,
do to me…
grant me sustenance, and cure me of… [Instead of the three dots, you may submit your requests and mention your ailed organ.]

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ أَعِنِّي عَلَىٰ هَوْلِ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَامَةِ

translation.en: O Allah, (please) help me encounter the horror of the Resurrection Day,
take me out of this world soundly,
give me in marriage the Paradisiacal women,
save me from procuring the provisions of myself, my family members,
and people,
and include me, in the name of Your mercy, with Your righteous servants.

text.en: Third:Prayer for restoration of Health
Quoting Misbah al-Mutahajjid, al-Kaf`ami has reported that whoever is ailed, may say the following supplicatory prayer in the second prostration of the first two units of the Night Prayer:

translation.en: O Most High! O All-great!
O All-beneficent! O All-merciful!
O Hearer of all prayers!
O Giver of the good things!
(Please) bless Muhammad and his Household,

segments.arabic: وَأَعْطِنِي مِنْ خَيْرِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلآخِرَةِ مَا أَنْتَ أَهْلُهُ
segments.transliteration: wa a`tini min khayri alddunya wal-akhirati ma anta ahluhu

translation.en: give me from the good of this world and the Next World in the amount that fits You,

segments.arabic: وَٱصْرِفْ عَنِّي مِنْ شَرِّ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلآخِرَةِ مَا أَنْتَ أَهْلُهُ
segments.transliteration: wasrif `anni min sharri alddunya wal-akhirati ma anta ahluhu

translation.en: dispel from me the evils of this world and the Next World in the way that fits You,
and remove from me this ailment…The disease or ailment may be now mentioned…
for it has enraged and depressed me.

text.en: It is recommended to supplicate earnestly, because the more earnest the sooner in response.
Fourth: Quoted from `Uddat al-Da`i, Imam al-Sadiq (`a)
When you are ailed, go outdoors, raise your hands towards the sky, and say the following supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ عَيَّرْتَ أَقْوَاماً فِي كِتَابِكَ

translation.en: O Allah! Having rebuked some people in Your Book,

segments.arabic: فَقُلْتَ: ﴿قُلِ ٱدْعُوٱ ٱلَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ
segments.transliteration: faqulta qul id`u alladhina za`amtum min dunihi

translation.en: You said, “Say, ‘Call on those whom you assert besides Him.

segments.arabic: فَلاَ يَمْلِكُونَ كَشْفَ الضُّرِّ عَنْكُمْ وَلاَ تَحْوِيلاًَ﴾
segments.transliteration: fala yamlikuna kashfa alddurri `ankum wa la tahwilan

translation.en: They shall not control the removal of distress from you nor its transference.’”

segments.arabic: فَيَا مَنْ لاَ يَمْلِكُ كَشْفَ ضُرِّي وَلاَ تَحْوِيلَهُ عَنِّي أَحَدٌ غَيْرُهُ
segments.transliteration: fayaman la yamliku kashfa durri wa la tahwilahu `anni ahadun ghayruhu

translation.en: O He save Whom no one controls the removal of distress nor its transference,
(please) bless Muhammad and his Household,
remove my distress,

segments.arabic: وَحَوِّلْهُ إِلَىٰ مَنْ يَدْعُو مَعَكَ إِلٰهاً آخَرَ
segments.transliteration: wa hawwilhu ila man yad`u ma`aka ilahan akhara

translation.en: and transfer it to one who calls on another deity besides You,

segments.arabic: فَإِنِّي أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لاَ إِلٰهَ غَيْرُكَ
segments.transliteration: fa'inni ashhadu an la ilaha ghayruka

translation.en: for I bear witness that there is no god save You.

text.en: Fifth:
It is also reported that any believer who is diseased or ailed, should rub the ailed organ with his hand and repeat the following (holy verse) with sincerity:

segments.arabic: وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ ٱلْقُرْآنِ مَا هُوَ شِفَاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
segments.transliteration: wa nunazzilu min alqur'ani ma huwa shifa'un wa rahmatun lilmu'minina

translation.en: “[And] We reveal of the Qur'an that which is a healing and a mercy for believers

segments.transliteration: wa la yazidu alzzalimina illa khasaran

translation.en: though it increase the evil-doers in naught save ruin.”

text.en: If he does, he will be restored to health no matter what his disease would be. Confirming this, the holy verse corroborates that it carries healing and mercy to the believers.
Sixth:
An ailed person is recommended to purchase one sa`([1]) of wheat, lie on his back, disperse the wheat grains on his chest, and say the following supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You in the name of Your Name—

segments.arabic: ٱلَّذِي إِذَا سَأَلَكَ بِهِ ٱلْمُضْطَرُّ كَشَفْتَ مَا بِهِ مِنْ ضُرٍّ
segments.transliteration: alladhi idha sa'alaka bihi almudtarra kashafta ma bihi min durrin

translation.en: if the distressed asks You by Your Name, You shall remove whatever harm he suffers,
give him power in the land,
and make him Your representative among Your creatures

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَىٰ أَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa `ala ahli baytihi

translation.en: to bless Muhammad and his Household
and to cure me of my ailment.

text.en: He may then sit, gather the wheat grains around him, repeat the same supplicatory prayer, divide the wheat grains into four shares, give each share to a poor man, and repeat the same supplicatory prayer. Once an ailed person does all that, he will be restored to health, Allah willing.
Seventh:Imam `Ali (`a) is reported
Imam `Ali (`a) is reported to have said: Put your hand on the ailed organ and repeat the following supplicatory prayer three times:

translation.en: Allah, Allah, Allah is my Lord indeed.
I do not associate with Him anything.

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ لَهَا وَلِكُلِّ عَظِيمَةٍ فَفَرِّجْهَا
segments.transliteration: allahumma anta laha wa likulli `azimatin fafarrijha

translation.en: O Allah, You are the only besought for this and for every terrible thing; so (please) relieve it.

text.en: Eighth:
Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have said in this regard: You may put your hand on the ailed organ and say:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah.

text.en: You may then rub the ailed organ with your hand and repeat the following evil-repelling prayer (`udhah) seven times:

translation.en: I pray for the protection of Allah’s almightiness,
I pray for the protection of Allah’s omnipotence,
I pray for the protection of Allah’s majesty,
I pray for the protection of Allah’s greatness,
I pray for the protection of Allah’s wholeness,

segments.arabic: وَأَعُوذُ بِرَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa a`udhu birasuli allahi salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi

translation.en: I pray for the protection of Allah’s Messenger, may Allah’s blessings be upon him and hisHousehold,
and I pray for the protection of Allah’s Names
against the evil of what I concern

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَخَافُ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِي
segments.transliteration: wa min sharri ma akhafu `ala nafsi

translation.en: and the evil of what I fear for myself.

text.en: Ninth:for child

It is reported that when a child is ailed, the mother may ascend to a roof, throw the head cover away, expose the hair of her head under the sky, prostrate herself, and say the following supplication:

translation.en: O Allah, O my Lord! It is You Who have given him to me
and it is You Who have endued me with Him.
O Allah, so (please) renew Your donation to me on this day.
You are verily all-powerful and omnipotent.

text.en: The mother will not raise her head from the prostration but that her child is restored to health.

Tenth: Dawud al-Raqqi has said: Whenever he insisted in prayers, Imam al-Sadiq (`a) would pray Almighty Allah in the name of the Five Persons: the Holy Prophet, Imam `Ali, Lady Fatimah, Imam al-Hasan, and Imam al-Husayn, peace be upon them.
Eleventh

Al-Shahid, may Allah have mercy upon him, has reported that whoever is tensely ailed may recite Surah al-Fatihah forty times on a glass of water. He may then pour the water on his body. He may have a measure of wheat near him and hand over each beggar a measure and ask him to pray Almighty Allah to cure him. If he does so, he will be restored to health, Allah willing.
“Medicate your patients with alms.” This saying is reported through considerable chains of authority.

With reference to removal of diseases, al-Shahid has also reported that the right arm of a patient may be held while reciting Surah al-Fatihah seven times. After that, the following supplicatory prayer may be said:

translation.en: O Allah, (please) remove ailments and maladies from him,
restore him to health and cure,
supply him with excellent protection,
return him to excellent wellbeing,

segments.arabic: وَٱجْعَلْ مَا نَالَهُ فِي مَرَضِهِ هٰذَا مَادَّةً لِحَيَاتِهِ
segments.transliteration: waj`al ma nalahu fi maradihi hadha maddatan lihayatihi

translation.en: and cause what has afflicted him in this ailment of him to be material of his life
and remission of his evildoings.

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ وَصَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma wa salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please) bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad.

text.en: If this does not work, Surah al-Fatihah may be repeated seventy times, and it will be effective, Allah willing.

translation.en: O Allah, endue me with reliance on You,
entrusting (all my affairs) with You,
satisfaction with Your decree,
and submission to Your ordinance

segments.arabic: حَتَّىٰ لاَ أُحِبَّ تَعْجِيلَ مَا أَخَّرْتَ
segments.transliteration: hatta la uhibba ta`jila ma akhkharta

translation.en: so that I would not long for hastening what You delay
and delaying what You hasten.
O Lord of the worlds!

text.en: Twelfth:
Imam al-Baqir (`a) is reported to have said, “Whoever is not healed by Surah al-Fatihah and Surah al-Tawhid, nothing else will heal him. Verily, these two Surahs cure of all ailments.”
Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have said: Allah will certainly cure any ailed believer who says the following verse sincerely while rubbing his ailed organ with his hand:

segments.arabic: وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ ٱلْقُرْآنِ مَا هُوَ شِفَاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
segments.transliteration: wa nunazzilu min alqur'ani ma huwa shifa'un wa rahmatun lilmu'minina

translation.en: “[And] We reveal of the Qur’an that which is a healing and a mercy for believers.”

text.en: Imam al-Rida (`a) is reported to have said: For all ailments, you may say the following supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: O revealer of cure and remover of remedy,
(please) bless Muhammad and his Household
and reveal cure upon my ailment.

text.en: Thirteenth:
Sayyid Ibn Tawus, may Allah have mercy upon him, has mentioned the following narration in his book entitled Muhaj al-Da`awat:

Ibn `Abbas is reported to have said that he was once present with Imam `Ali (`a) when a faint-faced man visited him and said, “O Commander of the Faithful, I am diseased person and many ailments and aches have afflicted me. So, please teach me a supplicatory prayer that helps me endure my ailments.” Answering him, Imam `Ali (`a) said, “I will lead you to a supplicatory prayer that was taught by Archangel Gabriel to the Holy Prophet (s) when al-Hasan and al-Husayn fell ill. This is it:

translation.en: O my God, whenever You bestow upon me a favor,
I am too short to thank it properly.
Whenever You test me with a tribulation,
I am too short to endure it.

segments.arabic: فَيَا مَنْ قَلَّ شُكْرِي عِنْدَ نِعَمِهِ فَلَمْ يَحْرِمْنِي
segments.transliteration: faya man qalla shukri `inda ni`amihi falam yahrimni

translation.en: O He Whose favors have not been thanked by me properly; yet, He has not deprived me of them!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ قَلَّ صَبْرِي عِنْدَ بَلاَئِهِ فَلَمْ يَخْذُلْنِي
segments.transliteration: wa yaman qalla sabri `inda bala'ihi falam yakhdhulni

translation.en: O He Whose tribulations have not been endured by me properly; yet, He has not disappointed me!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ رَآنِي عَلَىٰ ٱلْمَعَاصِي فَلَمْ يَفْضَحْنِي
segments.transliteration: wa ya man ra'ani `ala alma`asi falam yafdahni

translation.en: O He Who has seen me committing acts of disobedience to Him; yet, He has not exposed me!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ رَآنِي عَلَىٰ ٱلْخَطَايَا فَلَمْ يُعَاقِبْنِي عَلَيْهَا
segments.transliteration: wa ya man ra'ani `ala alkhataya falam yu`aqibni `alayha

translation.en: O He Who has seen me committing wrongdoings; yet, He has not punished me for them!
(Please) bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,
forgive my sin
and cure me of my ailment.
Verily, You have power over all things.”

text.en: Ibn `Abbas added: One year later, I saw the man fresh-faced and glowing. He said, “Whenever I said this supplicatory prayer in sickness, I would be soon restored to health, in ailment, I would be soon cured, and before a despotic authority, I would be protected against him.”
Fourteenth:

It is reported that al-Najashi, an ancient king of Abyssinia, inherited from his fathers a four hundred year old bonnet that appeases any pain that it touches. When the bonnet was untied to inspect its secret, the following supplicatory prayer was seen written therein:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah; the King and the Manifest Truth.

segments.arabic: شَهِدَ ٱللَّهُ أَنَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاََّ هُوَ
segments.transliteration: shahida allahu annahu la ilaha illa huwa

translation.en: Allah is witness that there is no god save Him.
The angels and the men of learning too are witness.
Maintaining His creation in justice,

segments.arabic: لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاََّ هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa huwa al`azizu alhakimu

translation.en: there is no god save Him; the Almighty and Wise.
Lo! The religion with Allah is Islam.
To Allah there are light, wisdom,
might, power,

segments.transliteration: wa qudratun wa sultanun wa burhanun

translation.en: omnipotence, authority, and clear evidence.
There is no god save Allah.
Adam is the choice of Allah.
There is no god save Allah.
Abraham is the friend of Allah.
There is no god save Allah.
Moses is the spoken by Allah.
There is no god save Allah.
Muhammad the Arab is the messenger of Allah,

segments.transliteration: wa habibuhu wa khiyaratuhu min khalqihi

translation.en: as well as His most beloved and best of all His creatures.
Calm down, O all pains,
illnesses, diseases,
all ailments,
and all fevers.

segments.arabic: سَكَّنْتُكَ بِٱلَّذِي سَكَنَ لَهُ مَا فِي ٱللَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ
segments.transliteration: sakkantuka billadhi sakana lahu ma fi allayli walnnahari

translation.en: I make you calm down in the name of the One to Whom whatsoever in the night and daylight rests
and He is the All-hearing and All-knowing.
May Allah bless the best of His creatures;
Muhammad, and his entire Household.

text.en: Fifteenth: Headache

According to Makarim al-Akhlaq, al-Najashi, having had headache, wrote a message to the Holy Prophet (s) complaining about his ache. In response, the Holy Prophet (s) sent him a supplicatory amulet written in a patch. When al-Najashi put the patch in his bonnet, his headache was relieved.This is the supplicatory amulet:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

segments.arabic: لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاََّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱلْحَقُّ ٱلْمُبِينُ
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa allahu almaliku alhaqq almubinu

translation.en: There is no god save Allah, the King and the manifest truth.

segments.arabic: شَهِدَ ٱللَّهُ أَنَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ
segments.transliteration: shahida allahu annahu la ilaha illa huwa

translation.en: Allah bears witness that there is no god but He
and so do the angels and those possessed of knowledge
maintaining His creation with justice.

segments.arabic: لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa huwa al`azizu alhakimu

translation.en: There is no god but Him, the Mighty, the Wise.
To Allah there are light, wisdom,
might, power,
clear argument, omnipotence,
authority, and mercy.
O He Who never sleeps!
There is no god save Allah.
Abraham is the friend of Allah.
There is no god save Allah.
Moses is the spoken by Allah.
There is no god save Allah.
Jesus and Allah’s spirit and word.
There is no god save Allah.
Muhammad is Allah’s messenger,
choice, and select.

segments.transliteration: salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi wa sallama

translation.en: May Allah send blessings and peace upon him and his Household.
Calm down!

segments.arabic: سَكَّنْتُكَ بِمَنْ يَسْكُنُ لَهُ مَا فِي ٱلسَّمَوَاتِ وَٱلأَرْضِ
segments.transliteration: sakkantuka biman yaskunu lahu ma fi alssamawati wal-ardi

translation.en: I make you calm down in the name of the One before Whom whatever is in the heavens and theearth rests

segments.arabic: وَبِمَنْ سَكَنَ لَهُ مَا فِي ٱللَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ
segments.transliteration: wa biman sakana lahu ma fi allayli walnnahari

translation.en: and in the name of the One to Whom whatsoever in the night and daylight rests
and He is the All-hearing and All-knowing.
Then, We made the wind subservient to him; it made his command
to run gently wherever he desired
and the devils, every builder and diver.
Verily, all things return to Allah.

text.en: Imam al-Baqir (`a) is reported to have said: Whoever suffers from headache may rub his head with his hand and repeat the following statement seven times:

segments.arabic: أَعُوذُ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِي سَكَنَ لَهُ مَا فِي ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ
segments.transliteration: a`udhu billahi alladhi sakana lahu ma fi albarri walbahri

translation.en: I beseech the protection of the One to Whom whatsoever in the lands, in the sea,
in the heavens, and in the earth rests
and He is the All-hearing and All-knowing.

text.en: Sixteenth:Headache
Supplicatory Amulet against Headache
Recite the following holy verse on a glass of water and then drink it:

translation.en: “Do not those who disbelieve
see that the heavens and the earth
were closed up, but We have opened them

segments.arabic: وَجَعَلْنَا مِنَ ٱلْمَاءِ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ حَيٍّ
segments.transliteration: wa ja`alna min alma'i kulla shay'in hayyin

translation.en: and We have made of water everything living?
Will they not then believe?”

text.en: It is reported that whenever the Holy Prophet (s) was ailed or suffered from headache, he would open his two hands, recite Surah al-Fatihah, Surah al-Falaq, and Surah al-Nas, and then rub his face with his hands. Then, the ache would disappear.
For those who suffer from headache, while rubbing their heads with the hands, the following holy verse should be recited:

segments.arabic: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُمْسِكُ ٱلسَّمَاوَاتِ وَٱلأَرْضَ أَنْ تَزُولاَ
segments.transliteration: inna allaha yumsiku alssamawati wal-arda an tazula

translation.en: Surely, Allah upholds the heavens and the earth lest they come to naught;

segments.arabic: وَلَئِنْ زَالَتَا إِنْ أَمْسَكَهُمَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa la'in zalata in amsakahuma min ahadin min ba`dihi

translation.en: and if they should come to naught, there is none who can uphold them after Him.
Surely, He is the Forbearing, the Forgiving.

text.en: The following incident is quoted from the book of Rabi` al-Abrar:

In Turtus (a city western Syria), Al-Ma'mun, the `Abbasid ruler, was once affected by headache that none could treat. Having heard about this, the Roman emperor sent him a bonnet and a letter in which he wrote, “Having been informed about your headache, I sent you this bonnet so that you would put it on your head and your ache would vanish.” Anticipating that the bonnet would be poisoned, al-Ma'mun ordered the messenger to put it on his head. However, no harm happened to the messenger. Al-Ma'mun then ordered the bonnet to be put on the head of another man who suffered from headache. Therefore, his pain vanished. Only then did he put it on his head and his headache vanished. Surprised, al-Ma'mun untied the bonnet and found the following inscription written therein:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

segments.arabic: كَمْ مِنْ نِعْمَةٍ لِلَّهِ فِي عِرْقٍ سَاكِنٍ
segments.transliteration: kam min ni`matin lillahi fi `irqin sakinin

translation.en: Too many graces of Allah are in dormant vein.
ha-mim `ayn-sin-qaf
They shall not be affected with headache thereby nor shall they get exhausted.
Due to the words of the All-beneficent Lord, fires are extinguished.

segments.arabic: وَلاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاََّ بِٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billahi

translation.en: There is neither might nor power save with Allah.
Let the benefit of medicine pervade in you

segments.arabic: كَمَا يَجُولُ مَاءُ ٱلرَّبِيعِ فِي ٱلْغُصْنِ
segments.transliteration: kama yajulu ma'u alrrabi`i fi alghusni

translation.en: just as the spring water pervades in twigs.

text.en: Supplicatory Amulet against Migraine
Put your hand on the ached half of your head and repeat the following supplicatory prayer three times:

translation.en: O Manifestly Existent,
O never missing although immanent!

segments.arabic: أُرْدُدْ عَلَىٰ عَبْدِكَ ٱلضَّعِيفِ أَيَادِيَكَ ٱلْجَمِيلَةَ عِنْدَهُ

translation.en: Confer back Your excellent favors upon your weak servant

segments.arabic: وَأَذْهِبْ عَنْهُ مَا بِهِ مِنْ أَذىٰ
segments.transliteration: wa adhhib `anhu ma bihi min adhan

translation.en: and remove from him the harm from which he is suffering.
Verily, You are All-merciful and Omnipotent.

text.en: Seventeenth:Ear /Deafness
Supplicatory Amulet against Earache
Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have advised the same supplicatory amulet to be repeated seven times by those who suffer from earache.
Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have advised those who suffer from earache to do the following:
Bring a piece of very old cheese, crush it, add milk to it, boil it on fire, and pour some drops of it in the ached ear.
Supplicatory Amulet against Deafness
Imam al-Baqir (`a) is reported to have said: Put your hand on the ailed organ and recite the following holy verses [the last four verses of Surah al-Hashr]:

segments.arabic: لَوْ أَنْزَلْنَا هٰذَا ٱلْقُرْآنَ عَلَى جَبَلٍ…

translation.en: Had We sent down this Qur'an on a mountain…

text.en: These holy verses have been previously cited in this book.
Eighteenth: Mouth Ache
Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have said: As for those who suffer from mouth ache, they may put their hands on the ached organ and say the following:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

segments.arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِي لاَ يَضُرُّ مَعَ ٱسْمِهِ دَاءٌ
segments.transliteration: bismi allah alladhi la yadurru ma`a ismihi da'un

translation.en: In the Name of Allah with Whose Name no malady will harm.

segments.arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّتِي لاَ يَضُرُّ مَعَهَا شَيْءٌ
segments.transliteration: a`udhu bikalimati allahi allati la yadurru ma`aha shay'un

translation.en: I pray for the protection of Allah’s Words with which nothing will harm.
All-holy. All-holy. All-holy.
I beseech You, O my Lord, in Your Name,
the all-pure, the sacred, the all-blessed;
whoever beseeches You in Your Name, You will give him
and whoever implores You in Your Name, You will respond to him.

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ يَا اللَّهُ يَا اللَّهُ يَا اللَّهُ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka ya allahu ya allahu ya allahu

translation.en: I beseech you, O Allah, O Allah, O Allah,

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ ٱلنَّبِيِّ وَأَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin alnnabiyyi wa ahli baytihi

translation.en: to bless Muhammad, the Prophet, and his Household
