segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تُعَافِيَنِي مِمَّا أَجِدُ فِي فَمِي وَفِي رَأْسِي
segments.transliteration: wa an tu`afiyani mimma ajidu fi fami wa fi ra'si

translation.en: and to cure me of the ache from which I am suffering in my mouth, my head,

segments.transliteration: wa fi sam`i wa fi basari

translation.en: my hearing, my sight,

segments.transliteration: wa fi batni wa fi zahri

translation.en: my stomach, my back,

segments.transliteration: wa fi yadi wa fi rijli

translation.en: my hand, my leg,
and all of my organs.

text.en: Nineteenth: Toothache

Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have said: As for those who suffer from toothache, they may put their hands on the ached organ and recite Surah al-Fatihah, Surah al-Tawhid, Surah al-Qadr, and the following holy verse:

translation.en: And you see the mountains, you think them to be solid,
and they shall pass away as the passing away of the cloud--

segments.arabic: صُنْعَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِي أَتْقَنَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ
segments.transliteration: sun`a allahi alladhi atqana kulla shay'in

translation.en: the handiwork of Allah Who has made every thing thoroughly.
Surely, He is Aware of what you do.

text.en: Likewise, Imam `Ali (`a) is reported to have said: You may rub the place of your prostration (in ritual prayers) with your hand, rub the ached tooth with it, and say the following:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah.
The (true) Healer is Allah.

segments.arabic: وَلاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاََّ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَلِيِّ ٱلْعَظِيمِ
segments.transliteration: wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billahi al`aliyyi al`azimi

translation.en: There is neither might nor power save with Allah, the Most High and All-great.

text.en: Another supplicatory amulet against toothache
You may recite Surah al-Fatihah and Surah al-Tawhid. With each Surah, you may recite:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

text.en: After Surah al-Tawhid, you may recite the following holy verses:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

segments.arabic: وَلَهُ مَا سَكَنَ فِي ٱللَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ
segments.transliteration: wa lahu ma sakana fi allayli walnnahari

translation.en: And to Him belongs whatever dwells in the night and the day
and He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

segments.arabic: قُلْنَا يَا نَارُ كُونِي بَرْدًا وَسَلاَمًا عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ
segments.transliteration: qulna ya naru kuni bardan wa salaman `ala ibrahima

translation.en: We said: O fire! Be a comfort and peace to Abraham.

segments.transliteration: wa aradu bihi kaydan faja`alnahumu al-akhsarina

translation.en: They desired a war on him but We made them the greatest losers.

segments.arabic: نُودِيَ أَنْ بُورِكَ مَنْ فِي ٱلنَّارِ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهَا
segments.transliteration: nudiya an burika man fi alnnari wa man hawlaha

translation.en: Blessed is Whoever is in the fire and whatever is about it
and glory be to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

text.en: You may then say the following supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ يَا كَافِياً مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma ya kafiyan min kulli shay'in

translation.en: O Allah, O He Who saves from all things
while nothing can save from You,
(please) save Your servant and the son of Your she-servant

segments.transliteration: min sharri ma yakhafu wa yahdharu

translation.en: from the evil of what he fears and anticipates

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ شَرِّ ٱلْوَجَعِ ٱلَّذِي يَشْكُوهُ إِلَيْكَ
segments.transliteration: wa min sharri alwaja`i alladhi yashkuhu ilayka

translation.en: and from the evil of the ache about which he is complaining to You.

text.en: It is also reported that one who suffers from toothache may take a blade or a leaf of a date-palm tree, rub the ached place with it, and repeat the following statement seven times:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
In the Name of Allah (I begin) and in Allah (I trust).
Muhammad is the messenger of Allah
and Abraham is the friend of Allah.

segments.arabic: أُسْكُنْ بِٱلَّذِي سَكَنَ لَهُ مَا فِي ٱللَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ بِإِذْنِهِ
segments.transliteration: uskun billadhi sakana lahu ma fi allayli walnnahari bi'idhnihi

translation.en: Calm down in the name of the One to Whom whatsoever is in the night and day rests by His permission
and He has power over all things.

text.en: It is also reported that one who suffers from toothache may put a stick or a piece of iron on the tooth and use this supplicatory amulet from the side of the tooth by repeating it seven times:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
How wonderful! It is the wonder of wonders
that a worm in the mouth
eats the bone
and causes blood to come out.
I use this amulet
but Allah is the healer and the savior.
There is no god save Allah.
All praise be to Allah, the Lord of the Worlds.

segments.transliteration: wa idh qataltum nafsan faddara'tum fiha

translation.en: “And when you killed a man, then you disagreed with respect to that,
and Allah was to bring forth that which you were going to hide.
So, We said: Strike the dead body with part of the sacrificed cow.
Thus Allah brings the dead to life
and He shows you His signs so that you may understand.”

text.en: The previous method may be repeated seven times.
Twentieth:chest Pains:
It is also reported that the previously mentioned verse (i.e. “and when you killed…”) may be recited by those who suffer from chest pains.

A tradition reads, “Seek healing from the Holy Qur'an, for Almighty Allah says, ‘O men! There has come to you indeed an admonition from your Lord and a healing for what is in the breasts.<ref=1:10:57-57>Surah Yunus</ref>
Twenty-first:Cure of Coughing ”

An all-inclusive supplicatory prayer is reported for curing of coughing. This lengthy supplicatory prayer, which is quoted from Bihar al-Anwar, Book of Supplicatory Prayers, begins with the following statement:

translation.en: O Allah, You are my hope
and You are my trust and reliance…

text.en: Twenty-second:Stomach pains:

The Holy Prophet (s) is reported to have advised those who suffer from colic to mix honey with hot water and recite Surah al-Fatihah seven times on the mixture so as to seek Almighty Allah’s protection against the pains.
As to Imam `Ali (`a), he is reported to have directed those who suffer from stomach pains to drink hot water and say the following supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: يَا اللَّهُ يَا اللَّهُ يَا اللَّهُ
segments.transliteration: ya allahu ya allahu ya allahu

translation.en: O Allah! O Allah! O Allah!
O All-beneficent! O All-merciful!
O Lord of all lords!
O God of all gods!
O King of all kings!
O Master of all masters!

segments.arabic: إِشْفِنِي بِشِفَائِكَ مِنْ كُلِّ دَاءٍ وَسُقْمٍ
segments.transliteration: ishfini bishifa'ika min kulli da'in wa suqmin

translation.en: (Please) cure me, with Your cure, of all maladies and illnesses,
for I am Your servant and the son of Your two servants
and I turn living under Your control.

text.en: For a stomach pain or any other ailment, the sick is recommended to put his/her hand on the ailed place and repeat the following evil-repelling prayer:

translation.en: I pray the protection of Allah’s almightiness and majesty
against the evil of what I feel.

text.en: The sick may then put his/her right hand on the ailed place and repeat the following three times:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah.

text.en: Twenty-third: Amulet against Colitis
For those who suffer from colitis, they are required to write Surah al-Fatihah, Surah al-Falaq, and Surah al-Nas on a paper or a sheet and then adds the following statement thereunder:

translation.en: I pray the protection of the Face of the All-great Allah,
His almightiness that is invulnerable,

segments.arabic: وَبِقُدْرَتِهِ ٱلَّتِي لاَ يَمْتَنِعُ مِنْهَا شَيْءٌ
segments.transliteration: wa biqudratihi allati la yamtani`u minha shay'un

translation.en: and His omnipotence against which nothing can stop
against the evil of this pain,
the evil of whatever is in it,

segments.transliteration: wa min sharri ma ajidu minhu

translation.en: and the evil of what I feel because of it.

text.en: Then, the sheet may be washed with rainwater and drunk in the beginning of the day before eating anything and before going to sleep. This is blessed and useful way.

It is narrated that a man complained to the Holy Prophet (s) about colitis from which his brother had suffered. The Holy Prophet (s) instructed, “Tell your brother to drink a mixture of honey and hot water.” The next day, the man returned to the Holy Prophet (s) and informed that the prescription was not useful. The Holy Prophet (s) commented, “Allah is truthful, but your brother’s colon is not. Go back, give him the same drink, and recite on it Surah al-Fatihah seven times as prayer for God’s protection.” When the man left, the Holy Prophet (s) said to Imam `Ali (`a), “The brother of this man is hypocrite; therefore, the drink was not effective with him.”
Twenty-fourth:Amulet Against warts

To treat warts—small, benign, and hard excrescence on the skin or on the hand occasionally—for each wart, you may take seven barley seeds. Recite the verses 1-6 of Surah al-Waqi`ah (No. 56) seven times over each seed. Then, recite the following verse seven times over each seed, too:

segments.arabic: وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنْ ٱلْجِبَالِ فَقُلْ يَنسِفُهَا رَبِّي نَسْفًا.
segments.transliteration: wa yas'alunaka `an aljibali faqul yansifuha rabbi nasfan

translation.en: They ask thee concerning the Mountains, say, ‘My Lord will uproot them and scatter them as dust;
He will leave them as plains smooth and level;

segments.arabic: لاَ تَرَىٰ فِيهَا عِوَجًا وَلاَ أَمْتًا.
segments.transliteration: la tara fiha `iwajan wa la amtan

translation.en: Nothing crooked or curved wilt thou see in their place.

text.en: You may then blow over it. Take one seed at a time and rub each seed on one wart. Then, put all of the seeds in a piece of cloth, tie up a rock on the piece of cloth, and throw it in a well.
This act is said to be advisably done in the waning of the moon.
A warty person is recommended to rub the warts with a piece of salt and recite three times the last four verses of Surah al-Hashr:

segments.arabic: لَوْ أَنْزَلْنَا هٰذَا ٱلْقُرْآنَ عَلَىٰ جَبَلٍ…

translation.en: Had We sent down this Qur'an on a mountain…

text.en: The piece of salt may be then thrown in an oven and the warty person may pass over it hurriedly. Then, the warts will vanish, Allah willing.
The author of the book of al-Khaza'in has mentioned that warts may vanish when they are overlaid with the hair-removing powder.
Twenty-fifth: Amulet against all Tumors
Recite the last four verses of Surah al-Hashr with ponderation while you are ceremonially pure after you have performed the ritual ablution (wudu') before and after an obligatory prayer:

segments.arabic: لَوْ أَنْزَلْنَا هٰذَا ٱلْقُرْآنَ عَلَىٰ جَبَلٍ…

translation.en: Had We sent down this Qur'an on a mountain…

text.en: The swelling will then vanish, Allah willing.
Twenty-sixth:Amulet Against Dystocia /Child birth :

For women suffering from dystocia (i.e. difficult or abnormally painful childbirth), the following holy verses may be written on a parchment and tied to their right thigh. It must be untied after they give birth:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
On the day that they shall see what they are promised,

segments.arabic: لَمْ يَلْبَثُوٱ إِلاََّ سَاعَةً مِنْ نَهَارٍ
segments.transliteration: lam yalbathu illa sa`atan min naharin

translation.en: they shall be as if they had not tarried save an hour of the day.
On the day that they see it,

segments.arabic: لَمْ يَلْبَثُوٱ إِلاََّ عَشِيَّةً أَوْ ضُحَاهَا

translation.en: it will be as though they had not tarried but the latter part of a day or the early part of it.
When a woman of `Imran said:

segments.arabic: رَبِّ إِنِّي نَذَرْتُ لَكَ مَا فِي بَطْنِي مُحَرَّراً
segments.transliteration: rabbi inni nadhartu laka ma fi batni muharraran

translation.en: My Lord! Surely, I vow to You what is in my womb, to be devoted (to Your service);

segments.arabic: فَتَقَبَّلْ مِنِّي إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ
segments.transliteration: fataqabbal minni innaka anta alssami`u al`alimu

translation.en: accept therefore from me. Surely, You are the Hearing, the Knowing.

text.en: It is also reported that the following holy verse should be recited over women suffering from dystocia:

translation.en: And the throes (of childbirth) compelled her to betake herself to the trunk of a palm tree.

segments.arabic: قَالَتْ يَا لَيْتَنِي مِتُّ قَبْلَ هٰذَا
segments.transliteration: qalat ya laytani mittu qabla hadaa

translation.en: She said: Oh, would that I had died before this,
and had been a thing quite forgotten.
Then (the child) called out to her from beneath her: Grieve not!
Surely, your Lord has made a stream to flow beneath you.
And shake towards you the trunk of the palm tree,
it will drop on you fresh ripe dates.

text.en: The reciter should then raise the voice with the following holy verse and the statement following it:

translation.en: “And Allah has brought you forth from the wombs of your mothers;
you did not know anything;

segments.transliteration: wa ja`ala lakumu alssam`a wal-absara wal-af'idata

translation.en: and He gave you hearing, sight, and hearts
that you may give thanks.”
So, come out O throes.
Come out, by the permission of Allah.

text.en: For easy childbirth, Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have advised of writing the following supplicatory prayer on a sheet of paper or a parchment:

translation.en: O Allah, O reliever of agonies,
dispeller of distresses,

segments.transliteration: wa rahmana alddunya wal-akhirati wa rahimahuma

translation.en: and beneficent and merciful of this world and the Hereafter,
(please) have mercy upon…

text.en: You may now mention the woman’s name and her mother’s:

segments.arabic: رَحْمَةً تُغْنِيهَا بِهَا عَنْ رَحْمَةِ جَمِيعِ خَلْقِكَ
segments.transliteration: rahmatan tughniha biha `an rahmati jami`i khalqika

translation.en: with such mercy that makes her dispense with the mercy of all of Your creatures,
relieve her anguish,
repel her distress,
and makes easy her childbirth.

segments.transliteration: wa qudiya baynahum bilhaqqi wa hum la yuzlamuna

translation.en: “And judgment shall be given between them with justice,

segments.transliteration: wa qila alhamdu lillahi rabbi al`alamina

translation.en: and it shall be said: All praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.”

text.en: Twenty-seventh: FEVER
(1) It is recommended to say this supplicatory amulet, which the Holy Prophet (s) taught to Imam `Ali (`a) against fever:

translation.en: O Allah, (please) have mercy upon my soft skin
and my fragile bone.

segments.transliteration: wa a`udhu bika min fawrati alhariqi

translation.en: I pray Your protection against the outburst of fire.
O Ummu-Mildam (i.e. fever),
if you believe in Allah,
then do not eat the flesh,
do not drink the blood,
do not boil in the mouth,

segments.arabic: وَٱنْتَقِلِي إِلىٰ مَنْ يَزْعَمُ أَنَّ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهاً آخَرَ
segments.transliteration: wantaqili ila man yaz`amu anna ma`a allahi ilahan akhara

translation.en: and move to one who claims that there is another god with Allah,

segments.arabic: فَإِنِّي أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ ٱللَّهُ
segments.transliteration: fa'inni ashhadu an la ilaha illa allahu

translation.en: for I bear witness that there is no god but Allah,
One and Only and having no associate,

segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu anna muhammadan `abduhu wa rasuluhu

translation.en: and I bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and messenger.

text.en: (2) A feverish person is recommended to say repeatedly in mornings and evenings the supplicatory prayer known as du`a' al-nur—a supplicatory prayer that Lady Fatimah al-Zahra' (`a) taught to Salman. This supplicatory prayer can be found in Mafatih al-Jinan.

(3) The Holy Imams (`a) are reported to have used to treated themselves against fever with cold water. Successively, they used to wet their clothes by putting one on and leaving the other in water. When the dressed one dried out, they would put on the other wet one.
(4) Handwritten by Imam al-Rida (`a), a feverish person may write the following inscriptions on three sheets of paper as follows:
On the first sheet, the following may be written down:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
“Fear not! Surely, you shall be the uppermost.”

text.en: On the second sheet, the following may be written down:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

segments.arabic: لاَ تَخَفْ نَجَوْتَ مِنْ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّالِمِينَ
segments.transliteration: la takhaf najawta min alqawmi alzzalimina

translation.en: “Fear not! You are secure from the unjust people.”

text.en: On the third sheet, the following may be written down:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
“Surely, His is the creation and the command.
Blessed is Allah, the Lord of the worlds.”

text.en: On each of these sheets, Surah al-Tawhid may be recited three times. After that, the feverish person may swallow the sheets in three days; each one on a day. If this is practiced, the fever will be healed, Allah willing.

(5) The feverish person my untie the buttons of his shirt, hang his head down to his chest, say the adhan and the iqamah statements, and recite Surah al-Fatihah seven times. If this is practiced, the fever will be healed, Allah willing.
(6) The Holy Imams (`a) are reported to have instructed that the following supplicatory prayer should be written on a parchment and put on to the feverish person:

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You in the name of Your almightiness,
omnipotence, authority,
and whatever is comprehended by Your knowledge

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: to bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ لاَ تُسَلِّطَ عَلَىٰ… شَيْئاً مِمَّا خَلَقْتَ بِسُوءٍ
segments.transliteration: wa an la tusallta `ala… ibni… shay'an mimma khalaqta bisu'in

translation.en: not to overload over [so-and-so] the son of [so-and-so] any thing evil that You have created,
and to have mercy upon his soft skin
and his fragile bone
against the outbursts of fire.
Come out, O Ummu-Mildam (i.e. fever),
eater of flesh,
and drinker of blood.
Its heat and coldness are from Hell.
If you have believed in Allah the Greatest,
then do not eat any thing of the flesh of [so-and-so] the son of [so-and-so],
do not suck any amount of his blood,
do not weaken any part of his bones,
do not stir up distress over him,
do not excite headache over him,
and move away from his hair, skin,
flesh, and blood

segments.arabic: إِلىٰ مَنْ زَعَمَ أَنَّ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهاً آخَرَ
segments.transliteration: ila man za`ama anna ma`a allahi ilahan akhara

translation.en: to one who claims that there is another god with Allah
save Whom there is no god.
Glory be to Him and Exalted be Him against whatever they associate with Him.

text.en: Then, it is recommended to write down the name of a non-Muslim or an enemy of Almighty Allah.
(7) On the right arm of the feverish person, it is advised to hang a sheet of paper (or the like) on which the following inscription has been written down:

translation.en: “In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
All praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the Worlds.
The Beneficent, the Merciful.
Master of the Day of Judgment.
Thee do we serve and Thee do we beseech for help.
Keep us on the right path—
the path of those upon whom You have bestowed favors.
Not (the path) of those upon whom Your wrath is brought down,
nor of those who go astray.”
In the Name of Allah (I begin) and in Allah (I trust).
I pray the protection of all the perfect words of Allah,

segments.arabic: ٱلَّتِي لاَ يُجَاوِزُهُنَّ بَرٌّ وَلاَ فَاجِرٌ
segments.transliteration: allati la yujawizuhunna barrun wa la fajirun

translation.en: beyond which neither a pious nor an impious can go,

segments.arabic: مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ وَذَرَأَ وَبَرَأَ
segments.transliteration: min sharri ma khalaqa wa dhara'a wa bara'a

translation.en: against the evil of whatsoever He has created, fashioned, and made,
against the evil of the pestles, the poisonous,
the general (evils), and the ill sights,

segments.transliteration: wa min sharri tawariqi allayli walnnahari

translation.en: against the evil of the comers at nights and in days,

segments.transliteration: wa min sharri fussaqi al`arabi wal`ajami

translation.en: against the evil of the wicked Arabs and non-Arabs,

segments.transliteration: wa min sharri fasaqati aljinni wal-insi

translation.en: against the evil of the wicked jinn and men,

segments.transliteration: wa min sharri alshshaytani wa shirkihi

translation.en: against the evil of Satan and his snares,

segments.transliteration: wa min sharri kulli dhi sharrin

translation.en: against the evil of all evil things,

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ دَابَّةٍ هُوَ آخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهَا
segments.transliteration: wa min sharri kulli dabbatin huwa akhidhun binasiyatiha

translation.en: and against the evil of every living creature that He holds it by its forelock.
Surely, my Lord is on the right path.
“Our Lord, on You do we rely,
to You do we turn,
and to You is the eventual coming.”

segments.arabic: يَا نَارُ كُونِي بَرْداً وَسَلاَماً عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ
segments.transliteration: ya naru kuni bardan wa salaman `ala ibrahima

translation.en: “O fire, be a comfort and peace to Abraham.

segments.transliteration: wa aradu bihi kaydan faja`alnahum al-akhsarina

translation.en: They desired a war on him, but We made them the greatest losers.”
Let it be comfort and peace to [so-and-so] the son of [so-and-so (his mother’s name)].

segments.arabic: رَبَّنَا لاَ تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِنْ نَسِينَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا
segments.transliteration: rabbana la tu'akhidhna in nasina aw akhta'na

translation.en: “Our Lord, do not punish us if we forget or make a mistake.
Our Lord, do not lay on us a burden

segments.arabic: كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِنَا

translation.en: as You did lay on those before us.

segments.arabic: رَبَّنَا وَلاَ تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لاَ طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ
segments.transliteration: rabbana wa la tuhammilna ma la taqata lana bihi

translation.en: Our Lord, do not impose upon us that which we have not the strength to bear.
Pardon us, forgive us, and have mercy on us.
You are our Patron;
so, help us against the unbelieving people.”
“Sufficient to me is Allah.
There is no god save Him.
So, take Him for a protector.”

segments.arabic: وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَىٰ ٱلْحَيِّ ٱلَّذِي لاَ يَمُوتُ
segments.transliteration: wa tawakkal `ala alhayyi alladhi la yamutu

translation.en: “Rely on the Ever-living Who dies not
and celebrate His praise.

segments.arabic: وَكَفىٰ بِهِ بِذُنُوبِ عِبَادِهِ خَبِيراً بَصِيراً
segments.transliteration: wa kafa bihi bidhunubi `ibadihi khabiran basiran

translation.en: And Sufficient is He as being aware of the faults of His servants.”
There is no god but Allah,
One and Only and having no associate.
He has truly fulfilled His promise,
given victory to His servant,
and defeated the allies alone.
Only what Allah wills shall come to pass.
“There is no power save with Allah.”

segments.transliteration: kataba allahu la'aghlibanna ana wa rusuli

translation.en: “Allah has written down: I will most certainly prevail, I and My messengers.
Surely, Allah is Strong, Mighty.”
“Surely, the party of Allah are they that shall be triumphant.”
“And whoever holds fast to Allah,
he indeed is guided to the right path.”
May Allah bless Muhammad
and his pure and immaculate Household.

text.en: (8) On three pieces of sugar, the following three phrases may be written down and the feverish person may eat them in the mornings of three days as the first meal, each piece a day:
The inscription on the first piece of sugar is this:

translation.en: I will be tied up by the permission of Allah.

text.en: The inscription on the second piece of sugar is this:

translation.en: I have fled by the permission of Allah.

text.en: The inscription on the third piece of sugar is this:

translation.en: I will be calmed down by the permission of Allah.

text.en: Twenty-eighth:against Tenesmus

It is reported that a man complained to Imam Musa al-Kazim (`a) about suffering from continuous tenesmus (i.e. a frequent, excessive, and painful desire to defecate). As treatment, the Imam (`a) taught the man to say the following supplicatory prayer after the Night Prayer:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ مَا كَانَ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَمِنْكَ
segments.transliteration: allahumma ma kana min khayrin faminka

translation.en: O Allah, whatsoever good thing I have ever experienced has been from You
and I have no praiseworthy thing about it.

segments.arabic: وَمَا عَمِلْتُ مِنْ سُوءٍ فَقَدْ حَذَّرْتَنِيهِ
segments.transliteration: wa ma `amiltu min su'in faqad hadhdhartanihi

translation.en: As for whatsoever evil I have done, You have already warned me against it;
therefore, I am not excused in doing it at all.

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أَتَّكِلَ عَلَىٰ مَا لاَ حَمْدَ لِي فِيهِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni a`udhu bika an attakila `ala ma la hamda li fihi

translation.en: O Allah, I thus pray Your protection against that I rely upon things that I have nopraiseworthy interference it them

segments.arabic: أَوْ آمَنَ مِمَّا لاَ عُذْرَ لِي فِيهِ
segments.transliteration: aw amana mimma la `udhra li fihi

translation.en: or that I feel secure against things that I am unexcused in committing them.

text.en: Twenty-ninth:Stomach Gurgling

Imam Musa al-Kazim (`a) is also reported to have advised the same previous supplicatory prayer to be said after the Night Prayer by those who suffer from stomach gurgling. [A man said to him, “I suffer from stomach gurgling that never stops. Therefore, I feel embarrassed when people to whom I speak hear the sounds of such gurgling. So, please pray Allah to cure me of it.]
Imam al-Sadiq (`a), advising those who suffer from stomach gurgling, said, “Black cumin may be mixed with honey and then eaten.”
Thirtieth:Leprosy

Yunus has reported that whitish scales between the eyes affected him. He therefore complained about this disease to Imam al-Sadiq (`a) who, leading him to a treatment, said: You may pure yourself ceremonially, offer a two-unit prayer, and say this (supplicatory prayer):

segments.arabic: يَا اللَّهُ يَا رَحْمَٰنُ يَا رَحِيمُ
segments.transliteration: ya allahu ya rahmanu ya rahimu

translation.en: O Allah! O All-beneficent! O All-merciful!
O Hearer of prayers!
O Donor of the good!

segments.transliteration: a`tini khayra alddunya wa khayra al-akhirati

translation.en: (Please) give me the good of this world and the good of the Hereafter,

segments.transliteration: wa qini sharra alddunya wa sharra al-akhirati

translation.en: protect me against the evil of this world and the evil of the Hereafter,
and remove from me what I am facing,
for this matter has enraged and saddened me.

text.en: Reporting the same narration, the author of `Uddat al-Da`i has reported Imam al-Sadiq (`a) as saying:

In the beginning of the last third of night, you may perform the ritual ablution (wudu') and offer the prayer you usually offer at this time. In the second prostration of the first two units of the prayer, you may say the following (supplicatory prayer) while you are in a state of prostration:

translation.en: O Most High! O All-great!
O All-beneficent! O All-merciful!
O Hearer of prayers!
O Donor of the good!
(Please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.transliteration: wa a`tini min khayri alddunya wal-akhirati ma anta ahluhu

translation.en: give me from the good of this world and the Hereafter

segments.transliteration: wasrif `anni min sharri alddunya wal-akhirati ma anta ahluhu

translation.en: in a way that fits You,

segments.arabic: وَٱصْرِفْ عَنِّي مِنْ شَرِّ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلآخِرَةِ

translation.en: repel from me the evil of this world and the Hereafter
in a way that fits You,
and remove from me this pain,
for it has enraged and saddened me.

text.en: You may pray Almighty Allah so persistently.
Yunus added, “Before I returned to al-Kufah, Almighty Allah has removed from me all the traces of that disease.”
For this disease as well as hemorrhoids, it is reported to write down Surah Yasin with honey in a cup that must be then washed and drunk.
It is also reported to have the soil of Imam al-Husayn’s tomb after being kneaded with rainwater.

It is also reported to overlay the ailed organ with henna and hair-removing powder for those suffering from mange, furuncle, and herpes. For the same diseases, it is reported to recite, write down, and hang on the diseased person the following holy verses and supplicatory statement:

translation.en: “In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
And the parable of an evil word
is as an evil tree
pulled up from the earth's surface;
it has no stability.”
“From it, We created you
and into it, We shall send you back,
and from it, will We raise you a second time.”

segments.transliteration: allahu akbaru wa anta la tukabbiru

translation.en: Allah is the Most Great, but you do not glorify His greatness.

segments.transliteration: allahu yabqa wa anta la tabqa

translation.en: Allah shall remain forever, but you do not remain forever.
Allah has power over all things.

text.en: Thirtyfirst Private Parts Pains

One of the companions of the Holy Imams (`a) is reported to have uncovered his private parts in a situation where it is improper to uncover one’s private parts; he was therefore affected by an ailment at that place. When he complained about this to Imam al-Sadiq (`a), he was taught to do this supplicatory amulet:
Put your left hand on the ailed part and repeat the following supplicatory prayer three times. If you do, you will be restored to health, Allah willing:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah (I begin) and in Allah (I trust).

segments.arabic: بَلىٰ مَنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ
segments.transliteration: bala man aslama wajhahu lillahi wa huwa muhsinun

translation.en: “Yes! Whoever submits himself entirely to Allah and he is the doer of good (to others),
he has his reward from his Lord,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلاَ هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
segments.transliteration: wa la khawfun `alayhim wa la hum yahzanuna

translation.en: and there is no fear for him nor shall he grieve.”
O Allah, I have submitted myself to You
and entrusted my affair to You.

segments.arabic: لاَ مَلْجَأَ وَلاَ مَنْجَىٰ مِنْكَ إِلاَّ إِلَيْكَ
segments.transliteration: la malja'a wa la manja minka illa ilayka

translation.en: There is neither shelter nor refuge against You except with You.

text.en: Thirty two:Knee Pains
Quoted from the book of Tibb al-A'immah (Medicine of the Imams), Jabir al-Ju`fi reported Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (`a) as saying:

I was present with al-Husayn ibn `Ali (`a) when a man from the descendants of Umayyah, yet he was loyalist to us, came to him and said, “O son of Allah’s Messenger, I could hardly come to you walking because of a pain from which I am suffering in my leg.”
The Imam (`a) asked, “How are you turned back from the supplicatory amulet of al-Hasan ibn `Ali (`a)?”
“What is that, son of Allah’s Messenger?” asked the man.
The Imam (`a) explained, “You may recite the first seven verses of Surah al-Fath.”
Afterwards, the man said, “As I had carried out this direction, I felt no pain at all.”
It is also reported that one who suffers from knee pains may say the following supplicatory prayer after the ritual prayers:

translation.en: O most magnanimous of all those who may donate!
O best of all those who may be besought!
O most merciful of all those whose mercy is asked!
(Please) have mercy upon my weakness and my confusion
and release me from my pain.

text.en: For those suffering from pains in the legs, it is reportedly recommended to recite the following holy verse seven times over the legs:

segments.arabic: وَٱتْلُ مَا أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ كِتَابِ رَبِّكَ
segments.transliteration: watlu ma uhiya ilayka min kitabi rabbika

translation.en: “And recite what has been revealed to you of the Book of your Lord.
There is none who can alter His words

segments.transliteration: wa lan tajida min dunihi multahadan

translation.en: and you shall not find any refuge besides Him.”

text.en: Thirty three: Eye Aches
For those suffering from eye aches, many traditions have recommended saying the following prayer after the ritual obligatory prayers of dawn (subh) and sunset (maghrib):

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِحَقِّ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَلَيْكَ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka bihaqqi muhammadin wa ali muhammadin `alayka

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You in the name of the right of Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad that is incumbent upon You

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: to bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad

segments.transliteration: wa an taj`ala alnnura fi basari

translation.en: and to place light in my sight,
insight in my faith,
certitude in my heart,
sincerity in my deeds,
safety in my self,
opulence in my sustenance,
and thanks for You forever as long as You keep me alive.

text.en: Al-Bizanti reported Yunus ibn Zabyan to have said: One day, we visited Imam al-Sadiq (`a) and found him tensely sore-eyed; therefore, we were depressed. The next morning, we visited him while he was completely healed. Surprised by this, we asked him, “May Allah accept us as ransoms for you! Have you treated your eyes with a certain medicine?” The Imam answered, “Yes, I have. I have treated them with the best of all medicines.” “What is that?” we asked. The Imam (`a) answered, “It is a supplicatory amulet.” We therefore wrote down that supplicatory amulet, which is as follows:

translation.en: I pray the protection of Allah’s almightiness,
I pray the protection of Allah’s all-powerfulness,
I pray the protection of Allah’s omnipotence,
I pray the protection of Allah’s light,
I pray the protection of Allah’s grandeur,
I pray the protection of Allah’s majesty,
I pray the protection of Allah’s beauty,
I pray the protection of Allah’s splendor,
I pray the protection of Allah’s wholeness,
I pray the protection of Allah’s pardon,
I pray the protection of Allah’s forgiveness,
I pray the protection of Allah’s Messenger
I pray the protection of the Imams:
`Ali ibn Abi-Talib,
al-Hasan ibn `Ali,
al-Husayn ibn `Ali,
`Ali ibn al-Husayn,
Muhammad ibn `Ali,
Ja`far ibn Muhammad,
Musa ibn Ja`far,
`Ali ibn Musa,
Muhammad ibn `Ali,
`Ali ibn Muhammad,
al-Hasan ibn `Ali,
and al-Hujjah (the Argument-person) ibn al-Hasan,
over whatever we wish
against the evil of what I am feeling.
O Allah! O Lord of the obedient!

text.en: When we asked the Imam (`a) about the meaning of jam` allah, he said, “It means the wholeness of Allah.”
It is also reported that one who suffers from eye pains may recite Ayah al-Kursi and believe in his inner self that the reciting of this holy verse will cure him of that pain.
Before reciting this holy verse, one suffering from eye pains is advised to put his hand on his eye and say the following:

translation.en: For the light of my sight, I pray
the protection of Allah’s light that is inextinguishable.

text.en: A Supplicatory Amulet against Nyctalopia

For those who suffer from nyctalopia (i.e. reduction or loss of vision in dim light), they are recommended to write down ayah al-Nur several times (on a sheet of paper) in a cup, wash it, put it in a flask, and use it as eye ointment.Ayah al-Nur is the following:

translation.en: Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth.
The similitude of His light is as a niche wherein a lamp is.
The lamp is in a glass.
The glass is as it were a shining star.
(This lamp is) kindled from a blessed tree, an olive
neither of the east nor of the west,
whose oil would almost glow forth (of itself)
though no fire touched it.
Light upon light.
Allah guides to His light whom He will.
And Allah speaks to mankind in allegories,
for Allah is Knower of all things.

text.en: It is also reported that whoever recites the Holy Qur'an from copies of the Holy Qur'an, will enjoy strong sight.
It is also reported that whoever repeats the following holy verse every day, his eyes will be saved from all optical troubles:

translation.en: “So, We make him hearing, seeing.”

text.en: Al-Kaf`ami says: According to experiments, to seek the advocacy of Imam Musa al-Kazim (`a) is useful for cure of eye pains as well as the troubles of the other organs.x
For those suffering from nose bleed, it is recommended to pour cold water on their heads and foreheads.
Thirty four:Harmel

The Holy Prophet (s) is also reported to have said, “For each tree, leaf, and fruit of harmel (also harmala; a plant having aromatic seeds containing various alkaloids and used locally for medicines, oil, and dye),([2]) there is an angel entrusted with them until they are wrecked. Verily, in the root and branches of these trees is an amulet against distress and sorcery. In the grains of this plant, there is remedy of seventy-two maladies. So, treat yourselves with this plant and with olibanum.”
Thirty five Epilepsy

It is reported that when Imam al-Rida (`a) once passed by an epileptic person, he ordered a cup of water to be brought to him. The Imam (`a) then recited Surah al-Fatihah, Surah al-Falaq, and Surah al-Nas on the cup and expectorated in it. He then ordered them to pour that water on the head and face of the epileptic person. Immediately, the man restored consciousness. The Imam (`a) then said to him, “This will never affect you again.”
Diabetes & Coronary heart disease
After Fajr recite this Ayat 7 times on water & drink it for Diabetes & 11 times by keeping hand on chest  And say:

segments.arabic: وَ قُلْ رَبِّ أَدْخِلْنِي مُدْخَلَ صِدْقٍ وَ أَخْرِجْنِي مُخْرَجَ صِدْقٍ وَ اجْعَلْ لِي مِنْ لَدُنْكَ سُلْطاناً نَصِيراً

translation.en: ‘My Lord! cause me to enter a goodly entrance and cause me to go out (of) a goodly exit, and grant me from Your part a (powerful) authority to assist (me)’. Al-Israa (الإسراء)‎ - 80

text.en: following is recommended for compulsive disorders like continuous washing
1- Wipe your hand on your chest then say:

segments.arabic: بسم الله و بالله محمد رسول الله ، و لا حول و لا قوة إلا بالله العلي العظيم ، اللهم امسح عني ما أحذر

text.en: Then wipe your hand on your stomach and say the same prayer 3 times.
2- When you start your salat, with your right index finger, strike your left thigh, then say:

segments.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَ بِاللَّهِ، تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللَّهِ، أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ السَّمِيعِ الْعَلِيمِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ"

text.en: Duas to overcome; Worries, Anxiety Hardship.
Worries
Hardships
Sahifa Sajjadia 54

translation.en: O Allah, drive the misfortune away from me.

segments.arabic: يَا كَائِنًا قَبْلَ كُلِّ شَيْءٍوَ يَا مُكَوِّنَ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَ يَا بَاقِي بَعْدَ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ صَلِّ عَلٰى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ افْعَلْ بِيْ
segments.transliteration: yaa kaaa-inan qabla kulli shay -in wa yaa mukawwina kulli shay -in wa yaa baaqiya ba'da kulli shay -in s'alli ala muh'ammadin wa ahli baytihee waf-a'l bee

translation.en: O the (ever) existing who is a priori to all existence; O the creator (who says 'be' and it becomes) of all things, O the everlasting eternal even when everything (created) shall perish:

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللهِ الْعَظِيْمِ وَ بِحَمْدِهِ اَسْتَغْفِرُ اللهَ وَاَسْألَهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ
segments.transliteration: subh'aanallaahil a'z'eemi wa bih'amdihee astaghfirullaaahaa wa as-aluhoo min faz'lihee

translation.en: Glory be to Allah and praise be to Him. I seek protection of Allah and I beseech Him to give me His bounties generously.

segments.arabic: اَعْدَدْتُ لِكُلِّ عَظِيْمَةٍ لَآ اِله اِلاَّ اللهُ وَ لِكُلِّ هَمٍٍّ وَ غَمِّ لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ اِلاَّ باِللهِ مُحَمَّدٌ نُوْرُ الْاوَّلُ وَ عَلِىٌّ نُوْرُ الثَّانِىْ وَ الْاَءِمَّةُ الْاَبْرَارُ عُدَّةٌ لِلِقَآءِ اللهِ وَ حِجَابٌ مِنْ اَعْدَآءِ اللهِ ذَلَّ كُلُّ شَىْءٍ لِعَظَمَةِ اللهِ وَأَسْأَلُ اللهَ عَزَّوَجَلَّ الْكِفَايَةَ
segments.transliteration: a'-dadtu likulli a'z'eematin laa ilaaha illallaahu wa likulli hammin wa ghammin laa h'awla wa laa quwwata illaa billaah muh'ammadun noorul awwalu wa a'liyaun nooruth thaanee wal a-immatul abraaru u'ddatun liliqaaa-illaahi wa h'ijaabun min a-daaa-illaahi d'alla kulli shay-in li-a'z'amatillaahi wa as-alullaaha a'zza wa jallal kifaayah

translation.en: I enumerate the merits of all that which are glorious and magnificent (to know that) there is no god save Allah; and for (removal of) pains and sorrows there is no power nor strength (in any) save in Allah. Muhammad is the first light, Ali the second light; and all the pious Imams (of the Ahlul Bayt) are a means to reach the presence of Allah and a protection from the enemies of Allah. All things are dwarfed in presence of the greatness of Allah. I seek sufficient (support) from Allah, the mighty, the glorious.

segments.arabic: اَلَّلهُمَّ اِنْ كَانَتْ ذَنُوْبِىْ قَدْ اَخْلَقَتْ وَجْهِيْ عِنْدَكَ فَاِنِّيْ اَتَوَجَّهُ اِلَيْكَ نَبِىِّ الرَّحْمَةِ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ اَلِهِ وَ عَلِىِّ وَ فَاطِمَةَ وَالْحَسَنِ وَ الْحُسَيْنِ وَ الْأِمَّةِ عَلَيْهِمْ السَلَامُ.
segments.transliteration: Allaahumma in kaanat d'unoobee qad akhlaqat wajhee i'ndaka fa innee atawajjahu ilayka bi nabiyyika nabiyyir rah'mati muhammadin s'allallaahu a'layhi wa aalihee wa a'liyyin wa faat'imata walah'asani wal h'usayni wal a-immati a'layhimus salaam

translation.en: O Allah, if my sins have blackened my face, I turn to You through Your prophet, Muhammad, the prophet of mercy, blessings of Allah be on him and on his children, (and through) Ali, Fatimah, Hasan, Husayn and the (rest of the) Imams (of the Ahl ul Bayt), peace be on all of them.

segments.arabic: حَسْبِىَ اللهُ لَآ اٍلَهَ اِلاَّ هُوَ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَهُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيْمِ-
segments.transliteration: h'asbiyallaahu laaa ilaaha illaa huw a'layhi tawakkaltu wa huwa rabbul a'rshil a'z'eem.

translation.en: Allah suffices me. There is no god but He. On Him do I rely, and He is the Lord of the great throne.

segments.arabic: وَ أُفَوِّضُ أَمْرِيْ إِلَى اللَّهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بَصِيْرٌ بِالْعِبَادِ
segments.transliteration: wa ufawwiz'u amree ilallaah innallaaha bas'eerum bil ibaad

translation.en: I entrust my affair to Allah, verily, Allah keeps an eye on (His) servants.

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّيْ كُنْتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِيْنَ فَاسْتَجَبْنَا لَهٗ وَ نَجَّيْنَاهُ مِنَ الْغَمِّ وَ كَذٰلِكَ نُنْجِي الْمُؤْمِنِيْنَ
segments.transliteration: laa ilaaha illaaa anta subh'aanaka innee kuntu minaz'z'aalimeen fas-tajabnaa lahoo wa najjaynaahu minal ghamm wa kad'aalika nunjil moomineen

translation.en: There is no god save You. Glory be to You. Verily I was of there unjust. Then We responded to him and delivered him from grief, thus do We deliver the believers.
Allah is sufficient for us; and most excellent is He (who) protects us.

segments.arabic: لَا حَوْلَ وَ لَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ الْعَلِيِّ الْعَظِيْمِ.
segments.transliteration: LAA H'AWLA WA LAA QUWWATA ILLAA BILLAAHIL A'LIYYIL A'Z'EEM

translation.en: There is no power and no strength save with Allah the high, the great.

segments.arabic: اَلَّلهُمَّ اَتِنِىْ بِالْعَافِيَةِ مِنْ حَيْثُ شِئْتَ وَ كَيْفَ شِئْتَ وَ اَنَّي شِئْتَ فَاِنَّكَ تَفْعَلُ مَا شِئْتَ كَيْفَ شِئْتَ-
segments.transliteration: allaahumma aatinee bin a'afiyati min h'aythu shi-ta wa kayfa shi-ta wa annaa shi-ta fa-innaka taf-a'lu maa shi-ta kayfa shi-ta

translation.en: O Allah, give us welfare from wheresoever You desire, as You wish and when You want. Verily You do that which You find satisfying because it pleases You.
O God, verily You are the closest companion to Your friends (saints),
and You are present with sufficiency for those who rely upon You.
You witness them in their innermost thoughts,
and You are aware of what is in their consciences,
and You know the extent of their insights.
So, their secrets are revealed to You,
and their hearts yearn towards You.
If loneliness makes them feel desolate, Your remembrance comforts them,
and if calamities pour down upon them,
they turn to seek Your protection,
knowing that the reins of all affairs are in Your Hand,
with their origin stemming from Your decree.
O God, if I become confused about my request,
or become unaware of what I truly need,
then guide me to what is beneficial for me,
and take my heart towards what is right for me,
for that is not unheard of among Your guidance,
nor is it an innovation among Your fulfillments.
O God, bear with me with Your pardon,
and do not bear with me with Your justice.

segments.arabic: يَا عِمادَ مَنْ لا عِمادَ لَهُ

translation.en: O Pillar of him who has no pillar,

segments.arabic: وَ يَا ذُخْرَ مَنْ لَا ذُخْرَ لَهُ
segments.transliteration: wa yā Dhukhra man lā dhukhra lah

translation.en: and O Treasure of him who has no treasure,

segments.arabic: وَ يَا سَنَدَ مَنْ لَا سَنَدَ لَهُ
segments.transliteration: wa yā Sanada man lā sanada lah

translation.en: and O Support of him who has no support,

segments.arabic: وَ يَا حِرْزَ مَنْ لَا حِرْزَ لَهُ
segments.transliteration: wa yā Ḥirza man lā ḥirza lah

translation.en: and O Sanctuary of him who has no sanctuary,

segments.arabic: وَ يَا غِيَاثَ مَنْ لَا غِيَاثَ لَهُ
segments.transliteration: wa yā Ghiyātha man lā ghiyātha lah

translation.en: and O Succour of him who has no succour,

segments.arabic: وَ يَا كَنْزَ مَنْ لَا كَنْزَ لَهُ
segments.transliteration: wa yā Kanza man lā kanza lah

translation.en: and O Treasure of him who has no treasure,

segments.arabic: وَ يَا عِزَّ مَنْ لَا عِزَّ لَهُ

translation.en: and O Honour of him who has no honour,
O Generous in Forgiveness,
O Excellent in Overlooking (sins),
O Helper of the weak,
O Treasure of the poor,
O Great in Hope,
O Rescuer of the drowned,
O Deliverer of the perished,
O Benefactor,
O Beautifier (Giver of Grace),
O Bestower of blessings,
O Giver of abundance,

segments.arabic: أَنْتَ الَّذِي سَجَدَ لَكَ سَوادُ اللَّيْلِ
segments.transliteration: Anta l-ladhī sajada laka sawādu l-layl

translation.en: You are the One to Whom the darkness of the night prostrates,
and the light of the day,
and the brightness of the moon,
and the ray of the sun,
and the rustling of the trees,
and the humming (rushing sound) of the water.

segments.arabic: يَا اللهُ يَا اللهُ يَا اللهُ
segments.transliteration: Yā Allāhu yā Allāhu yā Allāh

translation.en: O God, O God, O God,

segments.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ
segments.transliteration: lā ilāha illā Anta waḥdaka lā sharīka lak

translation.en: there is no god but You, You alone, there is no partner for You.
O My Lord! O God!

segments.arabic: صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ

translation.en: Bless Muḥammad and the Family of Muḥammad,

segments.arabic: وَ افْعَلْ بِنَا مَا أَنْتَ أَهْلُهُ
segments.transliteration: wa f‘al binā mā Anta ahluh.

translation.en: and deal with us as is befitting of You.
Then ask for your need.

text.en: From Sahifa Alavia 26. His Dua at the time of Severe Difficulties

segments.arabic: ي کنوش ارکنوش اره شش عطبيطسفينخ يَا مططرون قريالسيون وما سوما سوما طيطسالوس خبظوس مسفقيس مسا معوش افرطيعوش لطيفکش لطيفوش هَذَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنْتَ بِجَانِبِ الْغَرْبِىِّ اِذْ قَضَيْنَا اِلىَ مُوْسَى الْاَمْرَ وَمَا كُنْتَ مِنَ الْمُشَاهِدِيْنَ اُخْرُجْ بِقُدْرَةِ اللهِ مِنْهَا اَيُّهَا الْلَّعِيْنُ بِعزَّةِ اللهِ رَبِّ الْعْاَمِيْنَ اُخْرُجْ مِنْهَا وَ اِلاَّ كُنْتَ مِنَ الْمَسْجُونِيْنَ اُخْرُجْ مِنْهَا فَمَا يَكُوْنُ لَكَ اَنْ تَتَكَبَّرَ فِيْهَا فَاخْرُجْ اِنَّكَ مِنَ الصَّاغِرِيْنَ اُخْرُجْ مِنْهَا مُذْئُوْمًا مَدْحُوْرًا مَلْعُوْنًا كَمَا لَعَنَّا اَصْحَابَ السَّبْتِ وَ كَانَ اَمْرُ اللهِ مَفْعُوْلاً اُخْرُجْ يَا ذَوِىْ الْمَحْزُوْنَ اُخْرُجْ يَا سُوْرًا يَا سُوْرًا سُوْرٌ بِالاِسْمِ الْمَحْتزُوْنِ يَا طَطَرُونَ طَرْعُوْنَ مَرَعُوْنَ تَبَارَكَ اللهُ اَحْسَنَ الْخَالِقِيْنَ يَا هَيَا يَا هَيَا شَرَاهِيَّا حَيَّا قَيُّومًا بِالاِسْمِ الْمَكْتُوْبُ عَلىَ جَبْهَةِ اِسْرَافِيْلَ اَطْرُدُوْا عَنْ صَاحِبِ هَذَا الْكِتَابِ كُلَّ جِنِّىٍ وَ جِنَيَّةٍ وَ شَيْطَانٍ وَ شَيْطَانَةٍ وَتَابِعٍ وَ تَابِعَةٍ وَ صَاحِرٍ وَ صَاحِرَةٍ وَ غَوْلٍ وَ غَوْلَةٍ
segments.transliteration: iykanoosh arkanoosh arahshah a'tbeetas faneekh yaa matt'aroon qaryaa laysiyoon maa wa maa soomaa soomaa taytasaaloos khabt'oos musfaqees masaama-o'osh afartee-o'osh lateefakash lat'eefoosh haad'aa haad'aa wa maa kunta bijaanibil gharibbee id'qaz"aynaa ilaa moosal amra wa maa kunta minashshaahi deenaa ukhruj biqudratillaahi minhaa ayyuhal la-e'enu bi-i'zzatillaahi rabbil a'alameen ukhruj minhaa wa illaa kunta minal masjooneena ukhruj minhaa famaa yakoonu laka an tatakabbara feehaa fakhruj innaka minas' s'aaghireen ukhruj minhaa mad-ooman madh'ooran mal-o'onan kamaa la-a'nnaa as'h'aabas sabri wa kaana amrullaahi maf-o'olan ukhruj yaa d'awil mah'zoona ukhruj yaa sooran yaa sooran yuaa sooran bil ismil makhzooni yaa t'at'aroon tar -o'on maraa - o'on tabaarakallaahu ah'sanul hiyyaa h'ayyan qayyooman bil-ismil maktoobi a'laa jabhati israafeela ut'rudoo a'n s'aah'ibi haad'al kitaabi kulla jinnin wa jinniyyatin shayt'aanin wa shayt'aanatin wa taabi-i'n wa taabi-a'tin wa saah'irin wa saah'iratin wa ghoolin wa ghoolatin wa kulla muta-a'bbithin wa a'abithin yaa'bathu bibni aadama wa binaati h'awwaa wa laa h'awla wa laa quwwata illaa billaahil a'liyyil a'z'eem wa s'allallaahu a'laa muh'ammadin wa aalihee ajma-e'enat tayyibeenat t'aahireenal maa'soomeen

translation.en: -this (and) this-and you were not (present) in the western side when We sent our command to Musa, neither you were there to witness. O accursed, go away by the power of Allah, and by the honour of the Lord of the worlds. Get out imprisoned. Get out from this. It is not for you to show pride here, so get out, verily you are of those despised. Get out of this, despised, driven away; accursed as We cursed the people of the Sabbath, and the command of Allah is always executed. Get out, O afflicted. Get out O Sooran O Sooran in the name of that name which is kept secret, O Tat'aaroon T'aroon Mar-o'on. Blessings be for Allah who is the best creator. O Hayaa, O Hayaa, in the name of the everliving ever-subsisting name which is written on the forehead of Israafeel disappear at once from the holder of this written text, (whoever it may be) a genie (male or female), a devil (male or female) or a follower of (the devil, male or female), a sorcerer or sorcersess, a demon (male or female), a juggler or a trickster, whoever endangers the lives of the sons of Adam and daughters of Hawwaa. There is no power and strength (with any one) save with Allah, the highest, the greatest. Blessings of Allah be on Muhammad and on his pure, purified, and infallible children.

segments.arabic: يَا مُذِلَّ كُلِّ جَبَّارٍ عَنِيْدٍ يَا مُعِزَّ كُلِّ ذَلِيْلٍ قَدْ وَ حَقِّكَ بَلَغَ مَـجْهُوْدِيْ فَصَلِّ عَلٰى مُـحَمَّدٍ وَ اٰلِهٖ وَ فَرِّجْ عَنِّيْ.
segments.transliteration: yaa mozilla kulle jabbaarin a’needin yaa o-i’zza kulle zaleelin qad wa haqqeqa balagha majhoodee fa-salle a’laa mohammadin wa aalehi wa farrij a’nnee.

translation.en: O Humiliator of all tyrants! O Dignifier of all humble ones! I, swearing by You, have exerted all efforts; so, send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household and relieve me.[1]

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللهِ وَ الْـحَمْدُ لِلهِ وَ لَا اِلٰهَ اِلَّا اللهُ وَحْدَهٗ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَهٗ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَ لَهُ الْـحَمْدُ يُـحْيِيْ وَ يُـمِيْتُ وَ هُوَ حَيٌّ لَا يَـمُوْتُ بِيَدِهِ الْـخَيْرُ وَ هُوَ عَلٰى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْر
segments.transliteration: subhaanal laahe wal hamdo lillaahe wa laa elaaha illal laaho wahdahu laa shareeka lahu lahul mulko wa lahul hamdo yohyee wa yomeeto wa howa hayyun laa yamooto beyadehil khayro wa howa a’laa kulle shayin qadeer.

translation.en: Glory be to Allah, Praise be to Allah, there is no god but Allah, He is One and there are no partners for Him, He is Living, and He will not die, all good is through Him, and He has power over all things

segments.arabic: لَا حَوْلَ وَ لَا قُوَّةَ اِلَّا بِاللهِ الْعَلِيِّ الْعَظِيْمِ.
segments.transliteration: laa hawla wa laa quwwata illaa billaahil a’liyyi

translation.en: .There are no means and no power without Allah the Most High, the Most Great.

segments.arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ اِنِّيْ اَسْاَلُكَ بِـحَقِّ وَلِيِّكَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مُوْسَى الرِّضَا اِلَّا مَا سَلَّمْتَنِيْ بِهٖ فِيْ جَـمِيْعِ اَسْفَارِيْ فِيْ الْبَرَارِيْ وَ الْبِحَارِ وَ الْـجِبَالِ وَ الْقِفَارِ وَ الْاَوْدِيَةِ وَ الْغِيَاضِ مِنْ جَـمِيْعِ مَا اَخَافُهٗ وَ اَحْذَرُهٗ اِنَّكَ رَءُوْفٌ رَحِيْمٌ.
segments.transliteration: allaahumma innee as-aloka be-haqqe waliyyeka a’liy yibne moosar rezaa illaa maa sallamtanee behi fee jamee-e’ asfaaree fil baraaree wal behaare wal jebaale wal qefaare wal aw-deyate wal gheyaaze min jamee-e’ maa akhaafohu wa ahzarohu innaka raoofur raheem.

translation.en: O Allah! I implore You in by the right of Your friend, Ali Ibn Moosa al-Reza (a.s.) to secure me during my journeys; in lands and seas, on mountains and in plains, in valleys and in jungles, against all that which I fear or anticipate. Certainly You are All-Kind and All-Merciful.[3]

segments.arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ آمَنْتُ بِاللهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللهِ مَا شَاءَ اللهُ لَا حَوْلَ وَ لَا قُوَّةَ اِلَّا بِاللهِ
segments.transliteration: bismil laahe aamanto bil-laahe tawakkalto a’lal laahe maa shaa allaaho laa hawla wa laa quwwata illaa billaah.

translation.en: In the name of Allah, I believe in Allah, I place my trust in Allah, as Allah wills. There are no means and no power without Allah.

segments.arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ خَرَجْتُ وَ بِسْمِ اللهِ وَلَـجْتُ وَ عَلَى اللهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَ لَا حَوْلَ وَ لَا قُوَّةَ اِلَّا بِاللهِ الْعَلِيِّ الْعَظِيْمِ
segments.transliteration: bismil laahe kharajto wa bismil laahe walajto wa a’lal laahe tawakkalto wa laa hawla wa laa quwwata illaa bil-laahil a’liyyil a’zeem.

translation.en: In the name of Allah, I have come out, in the name of Allah, I enter, in Allah I have placed my trust, and there are no means and no power without Allah the Most High, the Most Great.
In the Name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
O God, verily my sins, and their great number,
have worn out my standing before You,
and have veiled me from deserving Your mercy,
and have distanced me from meriting Your forgiveness.
And were it not for my clinging to Your blessings,
and my holding fast to supplication,

segments.arabic: وَ مَا وَعَدْتَ أَمْثَالِي مِنَ الْمُسْرِفِينَ
segments.transliteration: wa mā wa‘adta amthālī mina l-musrifīn

translation.en: and what You have promised people like me, the transgressors,
and those similar to me, the sinners,
and what You have warned against for those despairing of Your mercy,
by Your saying: "O My servants who have exceeded limits

segments.arabic: عَلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ لَا تَقْنَطُوا مِنْ رَحْمَةِ اللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: ‘alā anfusihim lā taqnaṭū min raḥmati Llāh

translation.en: against their own souls, do not despair of the mercy of God.
Indeed, God forgives all sins.
Indeed, He is the All-Forgiving, the Most Merciful."
And You warned those who despair of Your mercy,

segments.transliteration: fa-qulta: "Wa man yaqnaṭu min raḥmati Rabbih

translation.en: and You said: "And who despairs of the mercy of his Lord
except for those astray?"
Then, with Your kindness, You invited us to supplicate to You,
and You said: "Call upon Me; I will respond to you.
Indeed, those who disdain My worship
will enter Hell, humbled."
My God, indeed that despair
was encompassing me,
and despair of Your mercy was covering me.

segments.arabic: إِلَهِي لَقَدْ وَعَدْتَ الْمُحْسِنَ ظَنَّهُ بِكَ ثَوَابًا
segments.transliteration: Ilāhī laqad wa‘adta l-muḥsina ẓannahu bika thawābā

translation.en: My God, You have promised the one who thinks well of You a reward,

segments.arabic: وَ أَوْعَدْتَ الْمُسِيءَ ظَنَّهُ بِكَ عِقَابًا

translation.en: and You have threatened the one who thinks ill of You with punishment.
O God, good thought of You

segments.arabic: حُسْنُ الظَّنِّ بِكَ فِي عِتْقِ رَقَبَتِي مِنَ النَّارِ
segments.transliteration: ḥusnu ẓ-ẓanni bika fī ‘itqi raqabatī mina n-nār

translation.en: has sustained my last breath regarding the freeing of my neck from the Fire,
and the covering of my slip,
and the overlooking of my stumble.
O God, You said in Your Book,

segments.arabic: وَ قَوْلُكَ الْحَقُّ الَّذِي لَا خُلْفَ لَهُ وَ لَا تَبْدِيلَ
segments.transliteration: wa qawluka l-ḥaqqu l-ladhī lā khulfa lahu wa lā tabdīl

translation.en: and Your Word is the Truth which has no change nor substitution,
"The Day We will call forth every people with their leader,"
and that is the Day of Resurrection,
when the Horn is blown,
and "what is in the graves is scattered."
O God, verily I fulfill, and I bear witness,

segments.arabic: وَ أُقِرُّ وَ لَا أُنْكِرُ وَ لَا أَجْحَدُ
segments.transliteration: wa uqirru wa lā unkiru wa lā ajḥad

translation.en: and I affirm, and I do not deny, and I do not disavow,

segments.arabic: وَ أُسِرُّ وَ أُعْلِنُ وَ أُظْهِرُ وَ أُبْطِنُ
segments.transliteration: wa usirru wa u‘linu wa uẓhiru wa ubṭin

translation.en: and I keep secret, and I announce, and I manifest, and I conceal,

segments.arabic: بِأَنَّكَ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
segments.transliteration: bi-annaka Anta Llāhu lā ilāha illā Ant

translation.en: that You are God, there is no god but You,
Alone, You have no partner,

segments.arabic: وَ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا عَبْدُكَ وَ رَسُولُكَ

translation.en: and that Muḥammad is Your servant and Your Messenger,
may God bless him and his progeny,
and that 'Alī, the Commander of the Faithful,

segments.arabic: سَيِّدُ الْأَوْصِيَاءِ وَ وَارِثُ عِلْمِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ

translation.en: is the Master of the Successors and the inheritor of the knowledge of the Prophets,
the flag of the religion and the destroyer of the polytheists,
and the discriminator of the hypocrites,
and the striver against the renegades,

segments.arabic: وَ إِمَامِي وَ حُجَّتِي وَ عُرْوَتِي

translation.en: and my Leader and my proof and my handhold,

segments.arabic: وَ صِرَاطِي وَ دَلِيلِي وَ حُجَّتِي
segments.transliteration: wa ṣirāṭī wa dalīlī wa ḥujjatī

translation.en: and my path and my guide and my evidence,

segments.arabic: وَ مَنْ لَا أَثِقُ بِأَعْمَالِي وَ لَوْ زَكَتْ
segments.transliteration: wa man lā athiqu bi-a‘mālī wa law zakat

translation.en: and the one without whose guardianship I do not trust my deeds, even if they are pure,

segments.arabic: وَ لَا أَرَاهَا مُنْجِيَةً لِي وَ لَوْ صَلُحَتْ
segments.transliteration: wa lā arāhā munjiyatan lī wa law ṣaluḥat

translation.en: and I do not see them as saving me, even if they are righteous,
except through his guardianship and taking him as a leader,

segments.arabic: وَ الْإِقْرَارِ بِفَضَائِلِهِ وَ الْقَبُولِ مِنْ حَمَلَتِهَا
segments.transliteration: wa l-iqrāri bi-faḍā’ilihi wa l-qabūli min ḥamaltihā

translation.en: and the affirmation of his merits and the acceptance from those who bear them (the merits),
and submission to those who narrate them.
And I affirm his successors from his sons
as leaders and proofs and guides,

segments.arabic: وَ سُرُجًا وَ أَعْلَامًا وَ مَنَارًا
segments.transliteration: wa surujān wa a‘lāmān wa manārā

translation.en: and lamps and flags and beacons,
and masters and righteous ones.
And I believe in their secret and their open,
and their apparent and their hidden,
and their present and their absent,
and their living and their dead.

segments.arabic: لَا شَكَّ فِي ذَلِكَ وَ لَا ارْتِيَابَ
segments.transliteration: Lā shakka fī dhālika wa lā rtīyāb

translation.en: There is no doubt in that, and no suspicion,
at Your changing nor Your turning.

segments.arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ فَادْعُنِي يَوْمَ حَشْرِي وَ نَشْرِي
segments.transliteration: Allāhumma fa-d‘unī yawma ḥashrī wa nashrī

translation.en: O God, call me on the Day of my gathering and my resurrection
by their leadership,

segments.arabic: وَ أَنْقِذْنِي بِهِمْ يَا مَوْلَايَ مِنْ حَرِّ النِّيرَانِ
segments.transliteration: wa anqidhnī bihim yā Mawlāya min ḥarri n-nīrān

translation.en: and save me through them, O my Master, from the heat of the Fires,

segments.arabic: وَ إِنْ لَمْ تَرْزُقْنِي رَوْحَ الْجِنَانِ
segments.transliteration: wa in lam tarzuqnī rawḥa l-jinān

translation.en: and even if You do not grant me the fragrance of the gardens (of Paradise).
For verily, if You free me from the Fire,
I will be among the successful.

segments.arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ وَ قَدْ أَصْبَحْتُ يَوْمِي هَذَا
segments.transliteration: Allāhumma wa qad aṣbaḥtu yawmī hādhā

translation.en: O God, I have entered this day

segments.arabic: لَا ثِقَةَ لِي وَ لَا رَجَاءَ وَ لَا لَجَاءَ
segments.transliteration: lā thiqata lī wa lā rajā’a wa lā lajā’a

translation.en: with no trust, no hope, and no refuge,

segments.arabic: وَ لَا مَفْزَعَ وَ لَا مَنْجَى
segments.transliteration: wa lā mafza‘a wa lā manjā

translation.en: and no shelter, and no escape,
except through those whom I have made my means to You,
seeking proximity to Your Messenger Muḥammad,
may God bless him and his progeny,
then 'Alī, the Commander of the Faithful,
and Az-Zahrā', the Mistress of the women of the worlds,

segments.arabic: وَ الْحَسَنِ وَ الْحُسَيْنِ وَ عَلِيٍّ وَ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: wa l-Ḥasani wa l-Ḥusayni wa ‘Alīyin wa Muḥammadin

translation.en: and Al-Ḥasan and Al-Ḥusayn, and 'Alī, and Muḥammad,

segments.arabic: وَ جَعْفَرٍ وَ مُوسَى وَ عَلِيٍّ

translation.en: and Ja'far, and Mūsā, and 'Alī,

segments.arabic: وَ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ عَلِيٍّ وَ الْحَسَنِ

translation.en: and Muḥammad, and 'Alī, and Al-Ḥasan,
and the one after them who establishes the clear path
to the concealed Proof from his offspring,
who is hoped for by the community after him.
O God, make them, on this day,

segments.arabic: وَ مَا بَعْدَهُ حِصْنِي مِنَ الْمَكَارِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa mā ba‘dahu ḥiṣnī mina l-makārih

translation.en: and what follows, my fortress against detestable things,
and my stronghold against fears,

segments.arabic: وَ نَجِّنِي بِهِمْ مِنْ كُلِّ عَدُوٍّ

translation.en: and save me through them from every enemy,

segments.arabic: وَ طَاغٍ وَ بَاغٍ وَ فَاسِقٍ
segments.transliteration: wa ṭāghin wa bāghin wa fāsiq

translation.en: and oppressor, and transgressor, and wicked person,

segments.arabic: وَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَعْرِفُ وَ مَا أُنْكِرُ
segments.transliteration: wa min sharri mā a‘rifu wa mā unkir

translation.en: and from the evil of what I know and what I deny,

segments.arabic: وَ مَا اسْتَتَرَ عَنِّي وَ مَا أُبْصِرُ
segments.transliteration: wa mā statara ‘annī wa mā ubṣir

translation.en: and what is hidden from me and what I see,

segments.arabic: وَ مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ دَابَّةٍ رَبِّي آخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهَا
segments.transliteration: wa min sharri kulli dābbatin Rabbī ākhidhun bi-nāṣiyatihā

translation.en: and from the evil of every creature whose forelock my Lord seizes;
indeed, You are on a straight path.
O God, through my seeking means to You by them,
and my seeking proximity through my love for them,
and my seeking protection through their leadership,

segments.arabic: افْتَحْ عَلَيَّ فِي هَذَا الْيَوْمِ أَبْوَابَ رِزْقِكَ
segments.transliteration: iftaḥ ‘alayya fī hādhā l-yawmi abwāba rizqik

translation.en: open for me on this day the doors of Your provision,
and spread Your mercy upon me,
and make me beloved to Your creation,
and keep me away from their hatred and their enmity.
Indeed, You are capable of all things.
O God, for every seeker of means there is a reward,
and for every possessor of intercession there is a right.
So, I ask You by those whom I have made my means to You,
and whom I have placed before my request,
to make me know the blessing of this day of mine,

segments.arabic: وَ شَهْرِي هَذَا وَ عَامِي هَذَا

translation.en: and this month of mine, and this year of mine.

segments.arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ وَ هُمْ مَفْزَعِي وَ مَعُونَتِي
segments.transliteration: Allāhumma wa hum mafza‘ī wa ma‘ūnatī

translation.en: O God, they are my shelter and my aid
in my hardship and my ease,
and my well-being and my tribulation,
and my sleep and my wakefulness,
and my travel and my stay,
and my difficulty and my ease,
and my open and my secret,
and my morning and my evening,
and my movements and my resting place,
and my secret and my open.

segments.arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ فَلَا تُخَيِّبْنِي بِهِمْ مِنْ نَائِلِكَ
segments.transliteration: Allāhumma fa-lā tukhayyibnī bihim min nā’ilik

translation.en: O God, do not disappoint me through them from Your gift,

segments.arabic: وَ لَا تَقْطَعْ رَجَائِي مِنْ رَحْمَتِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa lā taqṭa‘ rajā’ī min raḥmatik

translation.en: and do not cut off my hope from Your mercy,
and do not make me despair of Your solace,

segments.arabic: وَ لَا تَبْتَلِنِي بِانْغِلَاقِ أَبْوَابِ الْأَرْزَاقِ
segments.transliteration: wa lā tabtalinī bi-inghilāqi abwābi l-arzāq

translation.en: and do not try me with the closing of the doors of provisions,
and the blockage of its paths,
and the turning away of its sources.

segments.arabic: وَ افْتَحْ لِي مِنْ لَدُنْكَ فَتْحًا يَسِيرًا
segments.transliteration: Wa ftaḥ lī min ladunka fatḥan yasīrā

translation.en: And open for me from Your presence an easy opening,

segments.arabic: وَ اجْعَلْ لِي مِنْ كُلِّ ضَنَكٍ مَخْرَجًا
segments.transliteration: wa j‘al lī min kulli ḍanakin makhrajā

translation.en: and make for me an exit from every distress,
and a path to every amplitude.
Indeed, You are the Most Merciful of the merciful.

segments.arabic: وَ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِهِ الطَّيِّبِينَ الطَّاهِرِينَ
segments.transliteration: Wa ṣallā Llāhu ‘alā Muḥammadin wa ālihi ṭ-ṭayyibīna ṭ-ṭāhirīn

translation.en: And may God bless Muḥammad and his pure and clean progeny.
Amen, O Lord of the worlds.
O Compassionate, O Merciful.

segments.arabic: اُعِيْذُ نَفْسِيْ بِرَبِّ الْاَرْضِ وَ رَبِّ السَّمَاءِ اُعِيْذُ نَفْسِيْ بِالَّذِيْ لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهٖ دَاءٌ اُعِيْذُ نَفْسِيْ بِالَّذِيْ اسْمُهٗ بَرَكَةٌ وَ شِفَاءٌ.
segments.transliteration: o-e’ezo nafsee be-rabbil arze wa rabbis samaaa-e o-e’ezo nafsee bil-lazee laa yazurro ma-a’s mehi daa-un o-e’ezo nafsee bil-lazis mohu baraktun wa shefaaun.

translation.en: I seek protection for myself in the Lord of the earth and the Lord of the heavens. I seek protection for myself in Him with Whose Name no disease causes harm. I seek protection for myself in Him Whose Name is a blessing and a healing.[16]

segments.arabic: يَا مُنْزِلَ الشِّفَاءِ وَ مُذْهِبَ الدَّاءِ اَنْزِلْ عَلٰى وَجَعِيَ الشِّفَاءِ.
segments.transliteration: yaa munzelash shefaaa-e wa muzhebad daa-e anzil a’laa waja-e’yash shefaa-e.

translation.en: O Bestower of healing and Remover of illness, send down healing for my ailment.'[17]

segments.arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ الْعَظِيْمِ بِسْمِ اللهِ الْعَظِيْمِ رَبِّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيْمِ اِلَّا ذَهَبْتَ وَ انْقَرَضْتَ.
segments.transliteration: bismil laahil a’zeeme bismil laahil a’zeeme rabbil a’rshil a’zeeme illaa zahab-ta wan qarazta.

translation.en: In the name of Allah, the Great, in the name of Allah, the Great, Lord of the Mighty Throne, but that it left and perished.[19]

segments.arabic: وَعِنْدَهٗ مَفَاتِـحُ الْغَيْبِ لَا يَعْلَمُهَآ اِلَّا هُوَ۝۰ۭ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِيْ الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ۝۰ۭ وَمَا تَسْقُطُ مِنْ وَّرَقَۃٍ اِلَّا يَعْلَمُهَا وَلَا حَبَّۃٍ فِيْ ظُلُمٰتِ الْاَرْضِ وَلَا رَطْبٍ وَّلَا يَابِسٍ اِلَّا فِيْ كِتٰبٍ مُّبِيْنٍ۝۵۹
segments.transliteration: wa i’ndahu mafaatehul ghaybe laa ya’lamohaa illaa hoo, wa ya’lamo maa fil barre wal bahr, wa maa tasqoto minw waraqatin illaa ya’lamohaa wa laa habbatin fee zolomaatil arze wa laa ratbinw wa laa yaabesin illaa fee ketaabim mobeen.

translation.en: With Him are the keys of the unseen, the treasures that none knows but He. He knows whatever there is on the earth and in the sea. Not a leaf does fall but with His knowledge: there is not a grain in the darkness (or depths) of the earth, nor anything fresh or dry (green or withered), but is (inscribed) in a record clear (to those who can read).[21]

segments.arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ اِنَّكَ تَـهْدِيْ مِنَ الضَّلَالَةِ وَ تُنْجِيْ مِنَ الْعَمٰى وَ تَرُدُّ الضَّالَّةَ صَلِّ عَلٰى مُـحَمَّدٍ وَ اٰلِ مُـحَمَّدٍ وَ اغْفِرْ لِيْ وَ رُدَّ ضَالَّتِيْ وَ صَلِّ عَلٰى مُـحَمَّدٍ وَ اٰلِهٖ وَ سَلَّمَ.
segments.transliteration: allaahumma innaka tahdee menaz zalaalate wa tunjee menal a’maa wa tarudduz zaallata salle a’laa mohammadin wa aale mohammadin wagh fir lee wa rudda zaal-latee wa salle a’laa mohammadin wa aalehi wa sallam.

translation.en: O Allah! You guide one who is misguided and You save from blindness, and return the lost property. Bless Muhammad and his progeny and forgive me and return to me my lost property and bless Muhammad and his progeny and peace (be upon them).[22]
1.O Reliever of worry! O Remover of grief!
2.O Merciful in this world and the next and Compassionate in both!
3.Bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,
4.relieve my worry, and remove my grief!

segments.arabic: يَا وَاحِدُ يَا أَحَدُ، يَا صَمَدُ،
segments.transliteration: yaa waah'idu yaaa ah'adu yaa s'amad

translation.en: 5.O One, O Unique, O Eternal Refuge!
6.O He Who has not begotten, nor has been begotten,
7.and equal to Him is not any one!

segments.arabic: [وَاقْرَأْ آيَةَ الْكُرسِيّ وَالْمُعَوِّذَتَيْنِ وَقُلْ هُوَ اللهُ أَحَدٌ وَقُلْ:]
segments.transliteration: waq-raa aaayatal-kur-see-yi wal-mua'w-wad'atay-ni wa (qul huwal-laahooo ah'ad) waqul:

translation.en: 9.Here You Should Recite The Throne Verse (2: 255), The Two Suras Of Taking Refuge (113-114), And Unity (112). Then Say:

segments.arabic: أَللَّهُمَّ إنِّيْ أَسْأَلُكَ سُؤَالَ مَنِ اشْتَدَّتْ فَاقَتُهُ،
segments.transliteration: al-laahum-ma in-neee as-aluka soo-aala man ishtad-dat faaqatuh

translation.en: 10.O God, I ask You with the asking of him whose neediness is intense,

segments.transliteration: wa z''au'fat qoo-watuh wa kathurat d'unoobuh

translation.en: 11.whose strength is frail, whose sins are many,

segments.arabic: سُؤَالَ مَنْ لاَ يَجِدُ لِفَاقَتِهِ مُغِيْثاً،

translation.en: 12.the asking of one who finds no helper in his neediness,
13.no strengthener in his frailty,
14.no forgiver of his sin other than You,
15.O Possessor of majesty and munificence!

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ عَمَلاً تُحِبُّ بِهِ مَنْ عَمِلَ بِهِ،
segments.transliteration: as-aluk a'malana tuh'ib-bu bihee man a'mila bih

translation.en: 16.I ask of You a work through which You wilt love him who works it

segments.arabic: وَيَقِيناً تَنْفَعُ بِهِ مَنِ اسْتَيْقَنَ بِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa yaqeenana tanfau' bihee mani as-tay-qana bihee

translation.en: 17.and a certainty by which You wilt profit him who is certain
18.with the truth of certainty concerning the execution of Your command!

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّد وَآلِ مُحَمَّد،

translation.en: 19.O God, bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,
20.take my soul while it is firm in sincerity,
21.cut off my need for this world,
22.make my desire for what is with You
23.become a yearning to meet You,
24.and give me true confidence in You!

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ خَيْرِ كِتَاب قَدْ خَلاَ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ
segments.transliteration: as-aluka min khay-ri kitaabin qad khalaa wa au'wd'u bika

translation.en: 25.I ask of You the good of the writ that has been made and I seek refuge with You
26.from the evil of the writ that has been made.
27.I ask of You the fear of The worshipers,
28.the worship of those humbly fearful of You,
29.the certainty of those who have confidence in You,
30.and the confidence of those who have faith in You.
31.O God, make my desire in my asking
32.like the desire of Your friends in their asking,
33.and my fear like the fear of Your friends!
34.Employ me in Your good pleasure through works

segments.arabic: عَمَلاً لاَ أَتْرُكُ مَعَهُ شَيْئاً مِنْ دِيْنِكَ

translation.en: 35.in which I will not leave aside anything of Your religion
36.fearing any of Your creatures!

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ هَذِهِ حَاجَتِي، فَأَعْظِمْ فِيهَا رَغْبَتِي،
segments.transliteration: al-laahum-ma hd'ihee h'aajatee faaa'-z'im feehaa ragh-batee

translation.en: 37.God, this is my need, so make my desire for it great,

segments.arabic: وَأَظْهِرْ فِيهَا عُذْرِي، وَلَقِّنِي فِيهَا حُجَّتِي وَعَافِ فِيْهَا جَسَدِيْ.
segments.transliteration: wa az'-hir feehaa u'd'-ree wa laq-qinee feehaa h'uj-jatee wa a'afi feehaa jasadee

translation.en: 38.within it make manifest my excuse, through it instil me with my argument, and by means of it make well my body!

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ مَنْ أَصْبَحَ لَهُ ثِقَةٌ أَوْ رَجَآءٌ غَيْرُكَ،
segments.transliteration: al-laahum-ma man as'-bah'a lahoo thiqatun aw rajaaa-un ghay-ruk

translation.en: 39.O God, some rise in the morning having trust or hope in other than You.
40.I rise in the morning, and You art my trust
41.and my hope in all affairs,
42.so decree for me those which are best in outcome
43.and deliver me from misguiding trials,
44.O Most Merciful of the merciful!

segments.arabic: وَصَلَّى اللهُ عَلَى سَيِّدِنَا مُحَمَّد رُسُولِ اللهِ المُصْطَفَى،

translation.en: 45.And God bless our chief, Muhammad the Messenger of God, the chosen,
46.and his Household, the pure!

text.en: Duas for Fending of Distress,Grief & Fear
Baqiyatus Salehat -chapter Seven
tweleve prayers for fending of distress , grief & fear
<ref=2:50>Sahifa Sajjadia dua no 50 in fear</ref>
Twelve in number, the following supplicatory prayers are said for imploring Almighty Allah for protection against distress, grief, fear, and the like things.

First: Imam al-Baqir (`a) is reported to have said: Whenever a horrifying matter inflicts you, you may turn your face towards the kiblah direction and offer a two-unit prayer. Upon accomplishment, you may say this supplicatory prayer seventy times, and submit your requests each time you come to its end:

translation.en: O best seer of all those who can see,
O best hearer of all those who can hear,
O swiftest of those who settle account,
and O most merciful of all those who show mercy!

text.en: Second: The Holy Prophet (s) has said: Whoever is inflicted by a distress, a grief, angst, or a hardship, may say the following statement::

segments.arabic: اللَّهُ رَبِّي لاَ أُشْرِكُ بِهِ شَيْئاً
segments.transliteration: allahu rabbi la ushriku bihi shay'an

translation.en: Allah is my Lord; I never associate any thing to Him.

segments.arabic: تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَىٰ ٱلْحَيِّ ٱلَّذِي لاَ يَمُوتُ

translation.en: I put my trust in the Ever-living Who never dies.

text.en: Third: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have said:

When his brothers threw (Prophet) Joseph in the well, Archangel Gabriel came to him and said, “O boy, what are doing here?” Joseph answered, “My brothers have thrown me in this well.” “Do you like to come out of it?” asked the archangel. “This is up to Allah the Almighty and All-majestic.” answered Joseph, “If He wishes, He will do.” “Allah the All-exalted says to you that if you pray me with this supplicatory prayer, I will take you out of this well,” taught Gabriel. “What is this supplicatory prayer?” asked Joseph.“It is this one,” taught Archangel Gabriel:

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِأَنَّ لَكَ ٱلْحَمْدَ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka bi-anna laka lhamda

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You in the name of (the fact) that to You is all praise;
there is no god save You, the All-donor,
the excellent originator of the heavens and the earth,
and the Lord of majesty and honor

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: to bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَجْعَلَ لِي مِمَّا أَنَا فِيهِ فَرَجاً وَمَخْرَجاً
segments.transliteration: wa an taj`ala li mimma ana fihi farajan wa makhrajan

translation.en: and to prepare for me a relief and way out from what I am in. After that, the caravan came and took Prophet Joseph out of the well, as the Holy Qur'an has related.

text.en: Fourth: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have said: Whenever you fear something, you may say the following supplicatory prayer

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ لاَ يَكْفِي مِنْكَ أَحَدٌ
segments.transliteration: allahumma innaka la yakfi minka ahadun

translation.en: O Allah, nothing can ever save from You

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ تَكْفِي مِنْ كُلِّ أَحَدٍ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa anta takfi min kulli ahadin min khalqika

translation.en: while You alone can save from any of Your creatures;
so, save me from…

text.en: (You may now mention the thing that terrifies you.)
According to another tradition, you may say the following supplicatory prayer (whenever you fear something):

translation.en: O He Who saves from all things
while nothing can ever save from You
neither in the heavens nor on the earth,

segments.arabic: ٱكْفِنِي مَا أَهَمَّنِي مِنْ أَمْرِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلآخِرَةِ
segments.transliteration: ikfini ma ahammani min amri alddunya wal-akhirati

translation.en: (please) save me from what has distressed me, be it a this-worldly or a otherworldly affair,
and send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household.

text.en: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) has also said: Whoever comes to a ruler whom he fears, may say the following supplicatory prayer (before presence before the ruler):

translation.en: With Allah do I commence,
from Allah do I seek success,

segments.arabic: وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ أَتَوَجَّهُ
segments.transliteration: wa bimuhammadin salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi atawajjahu

translation.en: and in the name of Muhammad, may Allah bless him and his Household, do I turn my face (towards Allah).
O Allah, (please) make easy for me the difficulty of this matter
and facilitate for me its complication,

segments.transliteration: fa'innaka tamhu ma tasha'u wa tuthbitu

translation.en: for You verily efface what You will and establish (what You will)
and with You is the source of ordinance.
Sufficient to me is Allah.
There is no god but Him.
In Him do I trust
and He is the Lord of the Great Throne.

segments.transliteration: wa amtani`u bihawli allahi wa quwwatihi

translation.en: I take shelter in Allah’s might and power
against their might and power.
I take shelter in the Lord of the dawn
against the evil of what He has created.

segments.arabic: وَلاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billahi

translation.en: There is neither might nor power except with Allah.

text.en: Fifth: This supplicatory prayer is reported to have said by Imam al-Baqir (`a) whenever a distressing matter would take place::

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please) bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,
forgive me, have mercy upon me,
multiply my (good) deed, make easy my place of return,
guide my heart, secure my fear,
grant me wellbeing throughout my whole lifetime,
admit my plea, forgive my wrongdoings,

segments.transliteration: wa bayyid wajhi wa`simni fi dini

translation.en: whiten my face, preserve me with regard to my faith,

segments.arabic: وَسَهِّلْ مَطْلَبِي وَوَسِّعْ عَلَيَّ فِي رِزْقِي
